fields.name: path_specs
fields.label: Path Specs
fields.description: List of PathSpec. See [below](#path-spec) the details about PathSpec

fields.name: azure_config
fields.label: Azure Config
fields.description: Azure configuration

group_fields.name: base_path
group_fields.label: Base Path
group_fields.description: Base folder in hierarchical namespaces to start from.

group_fields.name: container_name
group_fields.label: Container Name
group_fields.description: Azure storage account container name.

group_fields.name: account_name
group_fields.label: Account Name
group_fields.description: Name of the Azure storage account. See [Microsoft official documentation on how to create a storage account.](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/create-data-lake-storage-account)

group_fields.name: account_key
group_fields.label: Account Key
group_fields.description: Azure storage account access key that can be used as a credential. **An account key, a SAS token or a client secret is required for authentication.**

group_fields.name: sas_token
group_fields.label: Sas Token
group_fields.description: Azure storage account Shared Access Signature (SAS) token that can be used as a credential. **An account key, a SAS token or a client secret is required for authentication.**

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Azure client secret that can be used as a credential. **An account key, a SAS token or a client secret is required for authentication.**

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Azure client (Application) ID required when a `client_secret` is used as a credential.

group_fields.name: tenant_id
group_fields.label: Tenant Id
group_fields.description: Azure tenant (Directory) ID required when a `client_secret` is used as a credential.

fields.name: profile_patterns
fields.label: Profile Patterns
fields.description: regex patterns for tables to profile

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Data profiling configuration

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

fields.name: use_abs_blob_tags
fields.label: Use Abs Blob Tags
fields.description: Whether to create tags in datahub from the abs blob tags

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: The platform that this source connects to (either 'abs' or 'file'). If not specified, the platform will be inferred from the path_specs.

fields.name: use_abs_container_properties
fields.label: Use Abs Container Properties
fields.description: Whether to create tags in datahub from the abs container properties

fields.name: use_abs_blob_properties
fields.label: Use Abs Blob Properties
fields.description: Whether to create tags in datahub from the abs blob properties

fields.name: spark_driver_memory
fields.label: Spark Driver Memory
fields.description: Max amount of memory to grant Spark.

fields.name: spark_config
fields.label: Spark Config
fields.description: Spark configuration properties to set on the SparkSession. Put config property names into quotes. For example: '"spark.executor.memory": "2g"'

fields.name: max_rows
fields.label: Max Rows
fields.description: Maximum number of rows to use when inferring schemas for TSV and CSV files.

fields.name: add_partition_columns_to_schema
fields.label: Add Partition Columns To Schema
fields.description: Whether to add partition fields to the schema.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Either a boolean, in which case it controls whether we verify the server's TLS certificate, or a string, in which case it must be a path to a CA bundle to use.

fields.name: number_of_files_to_sample
fields.label: Number Of Files To Sample
fields.description: Number of files to list to sample for schema inference. This will be ignored if sample_files is set to False in the pathspec.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Aerospike username.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Aerospike password.

fields.name: namespace_pattern
fields.label: Namespace Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for namespaces to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: set_pattern
fields.label: Set Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for sets to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: include_xdr
fields.label: Include Xdr
fields.description: Include XDR information in the dataset properties.

fields.name: schema_query_timeout_ms
fields.label: Schema Query Timeout Ms
fields.description: Socket timeout in milliseconds for schema inference queries. Default None uses the Aerospike client default.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: hosts
fields.label: Hosts
fields.description: Aerospike hosts list.

fields.name: auth_mode
fields.label: Auth Mode
fields.description: The authentication mode with the server.

fields.name: tls_enabled
fields.label: Tls Enabled
fields.description: Whether to use TLS for the connection.

fields.name: tls_capath
fields.label: Tls Capath
fields.description: Path to the CA certificate directory.

fields.name: tls_cafile
fields.label: Tls Cafile
fields.description: Path to the CA certificate file.

fields.name: inferSchemaDepth
fields.label: Inferschemadepth
fields.description: The depth of nested fields to infer schema. If set to `-1`, infer schema at all levels. If set to `0`, does not infer the schema. Default is `1`.

fields.name: schemaSamplingSize
fields.label: Schemasamplingsize
fields.description: Number of documents to use when inferring schema. If set to `null`, all documents will be scanned.

fields.name: maxSchemaSize
fields.label: Maxschemasize
fields.description: Maximum number of fields to include in the schema.

fields.name: ignore_empty_sets
fields.label: Ignore Empty Sets
fields.description: Ignore empty sets in the schema inference.

fields.name: records_per_second
fields.label: Records Per Second
fields.description: Number of records per second for Aerospike query. Default is 0, which means no limit.

fields.name: login_timeout_ms
fields.label: Login Timeout Ms
fields.description: Login timeout in milliseconds. Default None, using the default value of the Aerospike Python client.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: Airbyte API host and port (e.g., http://localhost:8000) - required for OSS deployment

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Username for basic authentication (OSS deployment)

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for basic authentication (OSS deployment)

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Api Key
fields.description: API key or Personal Access Token for authentication (OSS deployment)

fields.name: oauth2_client_secret
fields.label: Oauth2 Client Secret
fields.description: OAuth2 client secret for OSS (Airbyte 1.0+) and Cloud deployments

fields.name: oauth2_refresh_token
fields.label: Oauth2 Refresh Token
fields.description: OAuth2 refresh token (Cloud only). If provided, uses refresh_token grant; otherwise uses client_credentials

fields.name: workspace_pattern
fields.label: Workspace Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter workspaces. Use the pattern format as in other DataHub sources.

fields.name: connection_pattern
fields.label: Connection Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter connections. Use the pattern format as in other DataHub sources.

fields.name: source_pattern
fields.label: Source Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter sources. Use the pattern format as in other DataHub sources.

fields.name: destination_pattern
fields.label: Destination Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter destinations. Use the pattern format as in other DataHub sources.

fields.name: include_statuses
fields.label: Include Statuses
fields.description: Whether to ingest run statuses

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: extract_column_level_lineage
fields.label: Extract Column Level Lineage
fields.description: Extract column-level lineage

fields.name: extract_tags
fields.label: Extract Tags
fields.description: Extract tags from Airbyte metadata

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: deployment_type
fields.label: Deployment Type
fields.description: Type of Airbyte deployment ('oss' or 'cloud')

fields.name: oauth2_client_id
fields.label: Oauth2 Client Id
fields.description: OAuth2 client ID for OSS (Airbyte 1.0+) and Cloud deployments

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Whether to verify SSL certificates

fields.name: ssl_ca_cert
fields.label: Ssl Ca Cert
fields.description: Path to CA certificate file (.pem) for SSL verification

fields.name: extra_headers
fields.label: Extra Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers to send with each request

fields.name: request_timeout
fields.label: Request Timeout
fields.description: Timeout for API requests in seconds

fields.name: max_retries
fields.label: Max Retries
fields.description: Maximum number of retries for failed API requests

fields.name: retry_backoff_factor
fields.label: Retry Backoff Factor
fields.description: Backoff factor for retries (wait time is {factor} * (2 ^ retry_number))

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: Number of items to fetch per page in API requests

fields.name: cloud_workspace_id
fields.label: Cloud Workspace Id
fields.description: Workspace ID for Airbyte Cloud (required for cloud deployment)

fields.name: cloud_api_url
fields.label: Cloud Api Url
fields.description: Base URL for Airbyte Cloud API (defaults to production URL)

fields.name: cloud_oauth_token_url
fields.label: Cloud Oauth Token Url
fields.description: OAuth token URL for Airbyte Cloud (defaults to production URL)

fields.name: source_type_mapping
fields.label: Source Type Mapping
fields.description: Mapping from Airbyte sourceType/destinationType to DataHub platform names. Use this to normalize Airbyte's source types to DataHub platform names. Example: {'PostgreSQL': 'postgres', 'MySQL': 'mysql'}. If not specified, the sourceType/destinationType from Airbyte is sanitized and used directly.

fields.name: sources_to_platform_instance
fields.label: Sources To Platform Instance
fields.description: A mapping from Airbyte source ID to its platform/instance/env/database details. Use this to override platform details for specific sources. Example: {'11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111': {'platform': 'postgres', 'platform_instance': 'prod-postgres', 'env': 'PROD'}}

fields.name: destinations_to_platform_instance
fields.label: Destinations To Platform Instance
fields.description: A mapping from Airbyte destination ID to its platform/instance/env/database details. Use this to override platform details for specific destinations.

fields.name: job_status_start_date
fields.label: Job Status Start Date
fields.description: Start date for job status retrieval (format: yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SSZ). Default is 7 days ago.

fields.name: job_status_end_date
fields.label: Job Status End Date
fields.description: End date for job status retrieval (format: yyyy-mm-ddTHH:MM:SSZ). Default is current time.

fields.name: job_statuses_limit
fields.label: Job Statuses Limit
fields.description: Maximum number of job statuses to retrieve per connection

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The athena database to ingest from. If not set it will be autodetected

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Username credential. If not specified, detected with boto3 rules. See https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Same detection scheme as username

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: Aws Region
fields.description: Aws region where your Athena database is located

fields.name: work_group
fields.label: Work Group
fields.description: The name of your Amazon Athena Workgroups

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: query_result_location
fields.label: Query Result Location
fields.description: S3 path to the [query result bucket](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/querying.html#query-results-specify-location) which should be used by AWS Athena to store results of thequeries executed by DataHub.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable partition profiling. This will profile the latest partition of the table.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: s3_staging_dir
fields.label: S3 Staging Dir
fields.description: [deprecated in favor of `query_result_location`] S3 query location

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: aws_role_arn
fields.label: Aws Role Arn
fields.description: AWS Role arn for Pyathena to assume in its connection

fields.name: aws_role_assumption_duration
fields.label: Aws Role Assumption Duration
fields.description: Duration to assume the AWS Role for. Maximum of 43200 (12 hours)

fields.name: catalog_name
fields.label: Catalog Name
fields.description: Athena Catalog Name. When set to an S3 Tables catalog (i.e. a value starting with `s3tablescatalog/`), the catalog name is automatically used as `platform_instance` if not explicitly set, so datasets and containers are namespaced under the catalog in DataHub.

fields.name: glue_platform_instance
fields.label: Glue Platform Instance
fields.description: Platform instance of the upstream Glue datasets that Athena lineage points to. Set this if your Glue connector is configured with a non-default platform_instance; leave unset otherwise.

fields.name: iceberg_platform_instance
fields.label: Iceberg Platform Instance
fields.description: Platform instance of the upstream Iceberg datasets that Athena lineage points to. Set this if your Iceberg connector is configured with a non-default platform_instance; leave unset otherwise. For S3 Tables catalogs this defaults to the catalog name so the Athena dataset URN and its upstream Iceberg URN stitch correctly.

fields.name: extract_partitions
fields.label: Extract Partitions
fields.description: Extract partitions for tables. Partition extraction needs to run a query (`select * from table$partitions`) on the table. Disable this if you don't want to grant select permission.

fields.name: extract_partitions_using_create_statements
fields.label: Extract Partitions Using Create Statements
fields.description: Extract partitions using the `SHOW CREATE TABLE` statement instead of querying the table's partitions directly. This needs to be enabled to extract Iceberg partitions. If extraction fails it falls back to the default partition extraction. This is experimental.

fields.name: emit_schema_fieldpaths_as_v1
fields.label: Emit Schema Fieldpaths As V1
fields.description: Convert simple field paths to DataHub field path v1 format. Simple column paths are those that do not contain any nested fields.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant Id
fields.description: Directory ID. Found in your app registration on Azure AD Portal

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Application ID. Found in your app registration on Azure AD Portal

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Client secret. Found in your app registration on Azure AD Portal

fields.name: authority
fields.label: Authority
fields.description: The authority (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/msal-client-application-configuration) is a URL that indicates a directory that MSAL can request tokens from.

fields.name: token_url
fields.label: Token Url
fields.description: The token URL that acquires a token from Azure AD for authorizing requests. This source will only work with v1.0 endpoint.

fields.name: ingest_users
fields.label: Ingest Users
fields.description: Whether users should be ingested into DataHub.

fields.name: ingest_groups
fields.label: Ingest Groups
fields.description: Whether groups should be ingested into DataHub.

fields.name: ingest_group_membership
fields.label: Ingest Group Membership
fields.description: Whether group membership should be ingested into DataHub. ingest_groups must be True if this is True.

fields.name: ingest_groups_users
fields.label: Ingest Groups Users
fields.description: This option is useful only when `ingest_users` is set to False and `ingest_group_membership` to True. As effect, only the users which belongs to the selected groups will be ingested.

fields.name: users_pattern
fields.label: Users Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for users to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: groups_pattern
fields.label: Groups Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for groups to include in ingestion.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Azure AD Stateful Ingestion Config.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: redirect
fields.label: Redirect
fields.description: Redirect URI. Found in your app registration on Azure AD Portal.

fields.name: graph_url
fields.label: Graph Url
fields.description: [Microsoft Graph API endpoint](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/use-the-api)

fields.name: azure_ad_response_to_username_attr
fields.label: Azure Ad Response To Username Attr
fields.description: Which Azure AD User Response attribute to use as input to DataHub username mapping.

fields.name: azure_ad_response_to_username_regex
fields.label: Azure Ad Response To Username Regex
fields.description: A regex used to parse the DataHub username from the attribute specified in `azure_ad_response_to_username_attr`.

fields.name: azure_ad_response_to_groupname_attr
fields.label: Azure Ad Response To Groupname Attr
fields.description: Which Azure AD Group Response attribute to use as input to DataHub group name mapping.

fields.name: azure_ad_response_to_groupname_regex
fields.label: Azure Ad Response To Groupname Regex
fields.description: A regex used to parse the DataHub group name from the attribute specified in `azure_ad_response_to_groupname_attr`.

fields.name: mask_group_id
fields.label: Mask Group Id
fields.description: Whether workunit ID's for groups should be masked to avoid leaking sensitive information.

fields.name: mask_user_id
fields.label: Mask User Id
fields.description: Whether workunit ID's for users should be masked to avoid leaking sensitive information.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: bigid_url
fields.label: Bigid Url
fields.description: Base URL of the BigID instance (e.g. 'https://bigid.example.com').

fields.name: user_token
fields.label: User Token
fields.description: Recommended auth. Long-lived BigID user token, generated under Administration → Access Management → System Users (Save the user after generating so the token activates). Exchanged for a short-lived session token at startup and auto-refreshed on expiry, so it is safe for scheduled ingestion. Provide the raw token — no 'Bearer' prefix. Provide either this or access_token; if both are set, user_token takes precedence because it can auto-refresh.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Short-lived BigID session token, used directly without the startup exchange and NOT auto-refreshed — a run that outlives it fails. Intended for one-off runs or SSO-only tenants where a service-account user_token cannot be created; prefer user_token for scheduled ingestion. Provide either this or user_token; if both are set, user_token is used and this value is ignored.

fields.name: connection_pattern
fields.label: Connection Pattern
fields.description: Regex allow/deny patterns matched against the BigID connection (data source) name. Use this to scope ingestion to a subset of connections in large BigID deployments that expose hundreds of data sources. Catalog objects whose source connection is denied are skipped entirely.

fields.name: dataset_pattern
fields.label: Dataset Pattern
fields.description: Regex allow/deny patterns matched against the BigID catalog object's fully-qualified name. Complements connection_pattern with finer, dataset-level scoping for large deployments where a single connection exposes many objects. Objects whose fully-qualified name is denied are skipped.

fields.name: confidence_level_tag
fields.label: Confidence Level Tag
fields.description: Emit `urn:li:tag:bigid.confidence:{LEVEL}` alongside each GlossaryTerm on a column. Lossy (can't tie level to a specific term when multiple exist), but visible in DataHub UI.

fields.name: owner_type
fields.label: Owner Type
fields.description: How to interpret BigID owner strings. 'user' → `urn:li:corpuser:{owner}`. 'group' → `urn:li:corpGroup:{owner}`. 'none' → stored in customProperties only.

fields.name: sync_tags
fields.label: Sync Tags
fields.description: Emit BigID tags as DataHub Tag entities.

fields.name: tag_application_types
fields.label: Tag Application Types
fields.description: BigID applicationType values to sync as tags.

fields.name: risk_score_structured_property_urn
fields.label: Risk Score Structured Property Urn
fields.description: URN of the StructuredProperty used for riskScore values.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: timeout
fields.label: Timeout
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds.

fields.name: max_retries
fields.label: Max Retries
fields.description: Maximum number of retries for transient errors.

fields.name: datasource_platform_mapping
fields.label: Datasource Platform Mapping
fields.description: Map BigID connection name → platform config. Auto-detected from ds-connections API if omitted; explicit entries override.

fields.name: create_datasets
fields.label: Create Datasets
fields.description: If True, emit DatasetProperties + SchemaMetadata for datasets not yet in DataHub. Default False (pure enrichment mode — never emits structural aspects).

fields.name: minimum_confidence_threshold
fields.label: Minimum Confidence Threshold
fields.description: Filter column classification findings below this confidence level. Accepts 0.0–1.0 (not a rank string). BigID ranks map to: HIGH = 0.75, MEDIUM = 0.50, LOW = 0.25 (unknown ranks = 0.0).

fields.name: item_types
fields.label: Item Types
fields.description: Allow-list of BigID item types to sync. OOTB Personal Data Items are always included regardless of this filter.

fields.name: domain_mode
fields.label: Domain Mode
fields.description: Domain handling mode. 'none': store raw domain/sub_domain in customProperties only. 'auto_namespaced': auto-create GUID-based `urn:li:domain` entities (one per BigID domain/sub-domain, keyed deterministically by name). 'config_map': map BigID domain values to existing DataHub domain URNs.

fields.name: domain_mapping
fields.label: Domain Mapping
fields.description: Used when domain_mode='config_map'. Maps BigID domain string → DataHub domain URN.

fields.name: sync_unlinked_classifiers
fields.label: Sync Unlinked Classifiers
fields.description: Emit GlossaryTerms for classifier findings that have no Business Glossary linkage in BigID. Terms are auto-generated on demand (only when a column finding references the classifier) and placed under the same 'bigid' root GlossaryNode. Term URNs are deterministic GUIDs keyed on the classifier identity.

fields.name: sync_idsor
fields.label: Sync Idsor
fields.description: Emit GlossaryTerms for IDSoR (Identity Source of Record) attribute findings from BigID's correlation engine. IDSoR findings are separate from classifier findings and only appear when a Correlation Set is configured and enabled in the scan profile. When the attribute links to an existing Business Glossary term (via glossaryId), that term is reused. Otherwise an auto-generated term is created under a dedicated 'bigid.idsor' GlossaryNode. Term URNs are deterministic GUIDs keyed on the attribute identity.

fields.name: sync_unstructured_enrichment
fields.label: Sync Unstructured Enrichment
fields.description: Emit dataset-level GlossaryTerms and DatasetProfile for unstructured and email sources (SharePoint, Google Drive, O365, Kafka, AI models, etc.) using the attribute_details field returned by BigID's catalog API. Only applies to objects where BigID has classification findings (attribute_details non-empty). Controlled by the same sync_unlinked_classifiers and sync_idsor flags as structured enrichment.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project Id
fields.description: Project id to set the credentials

fields.name: private_key_id
fields.label: Private Key Id
fields.description: Private key id

fields.name: private_key
fields.label: Private Key
fields.description: Private key in a form of '<PEM block>\n'

fields.name: client_email
fields.label: Client Email
fields.description: Client email

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Client Id

fields.name: auth_uri
fields.label: Auth Uri
fields.description: Authentication uri

fields.name: token_uri
fields.label: Token Uri
fields.description: Token uri

fields.name: auth_provider_x509_cert_url
fields.label: Auth Provider X509 Cert Url
fields.description: Auth provider x509 certificate url

fields.name: type
fields.label: Type
fields.description: Authentication type

fields.name: client_x509_cert_url
fields.label: Client X509 Cert Url
fields.description: If not set it will be default to https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/client_email

fields.name: auth_type
fields.label: Auth Type
fields.description: Authentication type to use. Defaults to 'service_account'. Set to 'workload_identity_federation' to authenticate via [Workload Identity Federation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/workload-identity-federation), which avoids long-lived service account keys.

fields.name: extra_client_options
fields.label: Extra Client Options
fields.description: Additional keyword arguments passed to GCP client constructors (bigquery.Client, GCPLoggingClient, etc.).

fields.name: project_on_behalf
fields.label: Project On Behalf
fields.description: [Advanced] The BigQuery project in which queries are executed. Will be passed when creating a job. If not passed, falls back to the project associated with the service account.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: include_data_platform_instance
fields.label: Include Data Platform Instance
fields.description: Whether to create a DataPlatformInstance aspect, equal to the BigQuery project id. If enabled, will cause redundancy in the browse path for BigQuery entities in the UI, because the project id is represented as the top-level container.

fields.name: project_ids
fields.label: Project Ids
fields.description: Ingests specified project_ids. Use this property if you want to specify what projects to ingest or don't want to give project resourcemanager.projects.list to your service account. Overrides `project_id_pattern`.

fields.name: project_labels
fields.label: Project Labels
fields.description: Ingests projects with the specified labels. Set value in the format of `key:value`. Use this property to define which projects to ingest basedon project-level labels. If project_ids or project_id is set, this configuration has no effect. The ingestion process filters projects by label first, and then applies the project_id_pattern.

fields.name: project_id_pattern
fields.label: Project Id Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for project_id to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: dataset_pattern
fields.label: Dataset Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for dataset to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: match_fully_qualified_names
fields.label: Match Fully Qualified Names
fields.description: [deprecated] Whether `dataset_pattern` is matched against fully qualified dataset name `<project_id>.<dataset_name>`.

fields.name: table_snapshot_pattern
fields.label: Table Snapshot Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for table snapshots to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire snapshot name in database.schema.snapshot format. e.g. to match all snapshots starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: sharded_table_pattern
fields.label: Sharded Table Pattern
fields.description: The regex pattern to match sharded tables and group as one table. This is a very low level config parameter, only change if you know what you are doing,

fields.name: include_schema_metadata
fields.label: Include Schema Metadata
fields.description: Whether to ingest the BigQuery schema, i.e. projects, schemas, tables, and views.

fields.name: include_table_constraints
fields.label: Include Table Constraints
fields.description: Whether to ingest table constraints. If you know you don't use table constraints, you can disable it to save one extra query per dataset. In general it should be enabled

fields.name: include_external_url
fields.label: Include External Url
fields.description: Whether to populate BigQuery Console url to Datasets/Tables

fields.name: include_table_snapshots
fields.label: Include Table Snapshots
fields.description: Whether table snapshots should be ingested.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: gcs_lineage_config
fields.label: Gcs Lineage Config
fields.description: Common config for gcs lineage generation

group_fields.name: path_specs
group_fields.label: Path Specs
group_fields.description: List of PathSpec. See below the details about PathSpec

group_fields.name: strip_urls
group_fields.label: Strip Urls
group_fields.description: Strip filename from gcs url. It only applies if path_specs are not specified.

group_fields.name: ignore_non_path_spec_path
group_fields.label: Ignore Non Path Spec Path
group_fields.description: Ignore paths that are not match in path_specs. It only applies if path_specs are specified.

fields.name: lineage_use_sql_parser
fields.label: Lineage Use Sql Parser
fields.description: Use sql parser to resolve view/table lineage.

fields.name: lineage_sql_parser_use_raw_names
fields.label: Lineage Sql Parser Use Raw Names
fields.description: This parameter ignores the lowercase pattern stipulated in the SQLParser. NOTE: Ignored if lineage_use_sql_parser is False.

fields.name: extract_column_lineage
fields.label: Extract Column Lineage
fields.description: If enabled, generate column level lineage. Requires lineage_use_sql_parser to be enabled.

fields.name: extract_lineage_from_catalog
fields.label: Extract Lineage From Catalog
fields.description: This flag enables the data lineage extraction from Data Lineage API exposed by Google Data Catalog. NOTE: This extractor can't build views lineage. It's recommended to enable the view's DDL parsing. Read the docs to have more information about: https://cloud.google.com/data-catalog/docs/concepts/about-data-lineage

fields.name: include_table_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Lineage
fields.description: Option to enable/disable lineage generation. Is enabled by default.

fields.name: include_column_lineage_with_gcs
fields.label: Include Column Lineage With Gcs
fields.description: When enabled, column-level lineage will be extracted from the gcs.

fields.name: upstream_lineage_in_report
fields.label: Upstream Lineage In Report
fields.description: Useful for debugging lineage information. Set to True to see the raw lineage created internally. Only works with legacy approach (`use_queries_v2: False`).

fields.name: usage
fields.label: Usage
fields.description: Usage related configs

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats. **Deprecated**: set the top-level `bucket_duration` instead - it governs lineage, usage, and operations together.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC **Deprecated**: set the top-level `end_time` instead - it governs lineage, usage, and operations together.

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'. **Deprecated**: set the top-level `start_time` instead - it governs lineage, usage, and operations together.

group_fields.name: top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

group_fields.name: user_email_pattern
group_fields.label: User Email Pattern
group_fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

group_fields.name: include_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

group_fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental. Only applied with the legacy extraction path (`use_queries_v2: False`); ignored under queries-v2.

group_fields.name: format_sql_queries
group_fields.label: Format Sql Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

group_fields.name: include_top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Include Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

group_fields.name: max_query_duration
group_fields.label: Max Query Duration
group_fields.description: Correction to pad start_time and end_time with. For handling the case where the read happens within our time range but the query completion event is delayed and happens after the configured end time. **Deprecated**: set the top-level `max_query_duration` instead. Note it only takes effect with the legacy extraction path (`use_queries_v2: False`).

group_fields.name: apply_view_usage_to_tables
group_fields.label: Apply View Usage To Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to apply view's usage to its base tables. If set to False, uses sql parser and applies usage to views / tables mentioned in the query. If set to True, usage is applied to base tables only. Only applied with the legacy extraction path (`use_queries_v2: False`); ignored under queries-v2.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistic

fields.name: use_tables_list_query_v2
fields.label: Use Tables List Query V2
fields.description: List tables using an improved query that extracts partitions and last modified timestamps more accurately. Requires the ability to read table data. Automatically enabled when profiling is enabled.

fields.name: use_queries_v2
fields.label: Use Queries V2
fields.description: If enabled, uses the new queries extractor to extract queries from bigquery.

fields.name: include_queries
fields.label: Include Queries
fields.description: If enabled, generate query entities associated with lineage edges. Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled.

fields.name: include_query_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Query Usage Statistics
fields.description: If enabled, generate query popularity statistics. Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled.

fields.name: max_query_duration
fields.label: Max Query Duration
fields.description: Correction to pad start_time and end_time with. For handling the case where the read happens within our time range but the query completion event is delayed and happens after the configured end time.

fields.name: bigquery_audit_metadata_datasets
fields.label: Bigquery Audit Metadata Datasets
fields.description: A list of datasets that contain a table named cloudaudit_googleapis_com_data_access which contain BigQuery audit logs, specifically, those containing BigQueryAuditMetadata. It is recommended that the project of the dataset is also specified, for example, projectA.datasetB.

fields.name: use_exported_bigquery_audit_metadata
fields.label: Use Exported Bigquery Audit Metadata
fields.description: When configured, use BigQueryAuditMetadata in bigquery_audit_metadata_datasets to compute lineage information.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: capture_table_label_as_tag
fields.label: Capture Table Label As Tag
fields.description: Capture BigQuery table labels as DataHub tag

fields.name: capture_view_label_as_tag
fields.label: Capture View Label As Tag
fields.description: Capture BigQuery view labels as DataHub tag

fields.name: capture_dataset_label_as_tag
fields.label: Capture Dataset Label As Tag
fields.description: Capture BigQuery dataset labels as DataHub tag

fields.name: extract_policy_tags_from_catalog
fields.label: Extract Policy Tags From Catalog
fields.description: Extract policy tags from BigQuery tables using INFORMATION_SCHEMA and Data Catalog API. Policy tag display names are resolved by listing all tags per taxonomy (one API call per unique taxonomy, typically 3-10 calls per ingestion run). If the Data Catalog API is blocked by VPC Service Controls, policy tag resource names will be stored instead of display names (graceful degradation). Requires BigQuery API v2 (available since ~2020) and Data Catalog API access. For more information about policy tags and column-level security, refer to: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/column-level-security-intro

fields.name: enable_stateful_profiling
fields.label: Enable Stateful Profiling
fields.description: Enable stateful profiling. This will store profiling timestamps per dataset after successful profiling. and will not run profiling again in subsequent run if table has not been updated.

fields.name: enable_stateful_time_window
fields.label: Enable Stateful Time Window
fields.description: Enable stateful time window tracking. This will store the time window after successful extraction and adjust the time window in subsequent runs to avoid reprocessing. NOTE: This is ONLY applicable when using queries v2 (use_queries_v2=True). This replaces enable_stateful_lineage_ingestion and enable_stateful_usage_ingestion for the queries v2 extraction path, since queries v2 extracts lineage, usage, operations, and queries together from a single audit log and uses a unified time window.

fields.name: enable_stateful_lineage_ingestion
fields.label: Enable Stateful Lineage Ingestion
fields.description: Enable stateful lineage ingestion. This will store lineage window timestamps after successful lineage ingestion. and will not run lineage ingestion for same timestamps in subsequent run. NOTE: This only works with use_queries_v2=False (legacy extraction path). For queries v2, use enable_stateful_time_window instead.

fields.name: enable_stateful_usage_ingestion
fields.label: Enable Stateful Usage Ingestion
fields.description: Enable stateful lineage ingestion. This will store usage window timestamps after successful usage ingestion. and will not run usage ingestion for same timestamps in subsequent run. NOTE: This only works with use_queries_v2=False (legacy extraction path). For queries v2, use enable_stateful_time_window instead.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: enable_legacy_sharded_table_support
fields.label: Enable Legacy Sharded Table Support
fields.description: Use the legacy sharded table urn suffix added.

fields.name: rate_limit
fields.label: Rate Limit
fields.description: Should we rate limit requests made to API.

fields.name: requests_per_min
fields.label: Requests Per Min
fields.description: Used to control number of API calls made per min. Only used when `rate_limit` is set to `True`.

fields.name: temp_table_dataset_prefix
fields.label: Temp Table Dataset Prefix
fields.description: If you are creating temp tables in a dataset with a particular prefix you can use this config to set the prefix for the dataset. This is to support workflows from before bigquery's introduction of temp tables. By default we use `_` because of datasets that begin with an underscore are hidden by default https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/datasets#dataset-naming.

fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration
fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration
fields.description: Path to the GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration JSON file. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration_json and gcp_wif_configuration_json_string.

fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration_json
fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration Json
