fields.description: GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration as a dict or a JSON string. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration and gcp_wif_configuration_json_string.

fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration_json_string
fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration Json String
fields.description: GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration as a JSON string (contents of the configuration file). Useful for injecting configuration from secrets managers. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration and gcp_wif_configuration_json. Note: WIF configuration typically contains public endpoint URLs rather than private keys, so SecretStr masking is not applied. If your WIF config contains sensitive material, ensure it is not logged at DEBUG level.

fields.name: debug_include_full_payloads
fields.label: Debug Include Full Payloads
fields.description: Include full payload into events. It is only for debugging and internal use.

fields.name: number_of_datasets_process_in_batch_if_profiling_enabled
fields.label: Number Of Datasets Process In Batch If Profiling Enabled
fields.description: Number of partitioned table queried in batch when getting metadata. This is a low level config property which should be touched with care. This restriction is needed because we query partitions system view which throws error if we try to touch too many tables.

fields.name: column_limit
fields.label: Column Limit
fields.description: Maximum number of columns to process in a table. This is a low level config property which should be touched with care. This restriction is needed because excessively wide tables can result in failure to ingest the schema.

fields.name: log_page_size
fields.label: Log Page Size
fields.description: The number of log item will be queried per page for lineage collection

fields.name: use_date_sharded_audit_log_tables
fields.label: Use Date Sharded Audit Log Tables
fields.description: Whether to read date sharded tables or time partitioned tables when extracting usage from exported audit logs.

fields.name: convert_column_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Column Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: When enabled, converts column URNs to lowercase to ensure cross-platform compatibility.

fields.name: exclude_empty_projects
fields.label: Exclude Empty Projects
fields.description: Option to exclude empty projects from being ingested.

fields.name: max_threads_dataset_parallelism
fields.label: Max Threads Dataset Parallelism
fields.description: Number of worker threads to use to parallelize BigQuery Dataset Metadata Extraction. Set to 1 to disable.

fields.name: region_qualifiers
fields.label: Region Qualifiers
fields.description: BigQuery regions to be scanned for bigquery jobs when using `use_queries_v2`. See [this](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/information-schema-jobs#scope_and_syntax) for details.

fields.name: region_qualifiers_auto_discovery
fields.label: Region Qualifiers Auto Discovery
fields.description: When True, automatically extends `region_qualifiers` with any BigQuery regions detected from dataset locations during schema ingestion. Defaults to False to avoid unexpected query cost increases. Set to True if your project has datasets in regions beyond `region-us` and `region-eu`.

fields.name: pushdown_deny_usernames
fields.label: Pushdown Deny Usernames
fields.description: List of user email patterns using SQL LIKE syntax (e.g., 'bot_%', '%@%.iam.gserviceaccount.com') which will NOT be considered for lineage/usage/queries extraction. Uses case-insensitive LIKE for server-side filtering (e.g., 'bot_%' matches 'Bot_User@example.com'). This is primarily useful for improving performance by filtering out users with extremely high query volumes. Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled.

fields.name: pushdown_allow_usernames
fields.label: Pushdown Allow Usernames
fields.description: List of user email patterns using SQL LIKE syntax (e.g., 'analyst_%@company.com') which WILL be considered for lineage/usage/queries extraction. Uses case-insensitive LIKE for server-side filtering (e.g., 'analyst_%' matches 'Analyst_John@company.com'). Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled. If not specified, all users not in deny list are included.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Username credential with read access to the system_schema keyspace.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password credential associated with the specified username.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Port number to connect to the Cassandra instance.

fields.name: cloud_config
fields.label: Cloud Config
fields.description: Configuration for cloud-based Cassandra, such as DataStax Astra DB.

group_fields.name: token
group_fields.label: Token
group_fields.description: The Astra DB application token used for authentication.

group_fields.name: secure_connect_bundle
group_fields.label: Secure Connect Bundle
group_fields.description: File path to the Secure Connect Bundle (.zip) used for a secure connection to DataStax Astra DB.

group_fields.name: connect_timeout
group_fields.label: Connect Timeout
group_fields.description: Timeout in seconds for establishing new connections to Cassandra.

group_fields.name: request_timeout
group_fields.label: Request Timeout
group_fields.description: Timeout in seconds for individual Cassandra requests.

fields.name: keyspace_pattern
fields.label: Keyspace Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter keyspaces for ingestion.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter keyspaces.tables for ingestion.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to profile

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Configuration for profiling

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration for stateful ingestion and stale metadata removal.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: contact_point
fields.label: Contact Point
fields.description: Domain or IP address of the Cassandra instance (excluding port).

fields.name: ssl_ca_certs
fields.label: Ssl Ca Certs
fields.description: Path to the CA certificate file for SSL connections.

fields.name: ssl_certfile
fields.label: Ssl Certfile
fields.description: Path to the SSL certificate file for SSL connections.

fields.name: ssl_keyfile
fields.label: Ssl Keyfile
fields.description: Path to the SSL key file for SSL connections.

fields.name: ssl_version
fields.label: Ssl Version
fields.description: SSL protocol version to use for connections. Options: TLS_CLIENT, TLSv1, TLSv1_1, TLSv1_2, TLSv1_3. Defaults to TLS_CLIENT.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: ClickHouse host URL.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: temporary_tables_pattern
fields.label: Temporary Tables Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for temporary tables to filter from lineage.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform_instance mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_table_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Lineage
fields.description: Whether table lineage should be ingested.

fields.name: include_query_log_lineage
fields.label: Include Query Log Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract lineage from query_log (INSERT/CREATE queries). This complements the definition-based lineage from views/materialized views.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Whether to extract usage statistics from query_log. Tracks which tables and columns are queried by SELECT statements.

fields.name: include_query_log_operations
fields.label: Include Query Log Operations
fields.description: Whether to show recent write activity on datasets. Displays INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE history in the dataset's activity tab.

fields.name: query_log_deny_usernames
fields.label: Query Log Deny Usernames
fields.description: List of ClickHouse usernames to exclude from query log extraction.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table for usage statistics.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: secure
fields.label: Secure
fields.description: [deprecated] Use uri_opts instead.

fields.name: protocol
fields.label: Protocol
fields.description: [deprecated] Use uri_opts instead.

fields.name: uri_opts
fields.label: Uri Opts
fields.description: The part of the URI and it's used to provide additional configuration options or parameters for the database connection.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: ClickHouse host URL.

fields.name: database

fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: secure
fields.label: Secure
fields.description: [deprecated] Use uri_opts instead.

fields.name: protocol
fields.label: Protocol
fields.description: [deprecated] Use uri_opts instead.

fields.name: uri_opts
fields.label: Uri Opts
fields.description: The part of the URI and it's used to provide additional configuration options or parameters for the database connection.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: host URL

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog). If set to Null, all databases will be considered for ingestion.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: AWS configuration for RDS IAM authentication (only used when auth_mode is AWS_IAM). Provides full control over AWS credentials, region, profiles, role assumption, retry logic, and proxy settings. If not explicitly configured, boto3 will automatically use the default credential chain and region from environment variables (AWS_DEFAULT_REGION, AWS_REGION), AWS config files (~/.aws/config), or IAM role metadata.

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: A class to store allow deny regexes

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion. Note: this is not used if `database` or `sqlalchemy_uri` are provided.

fields.name: include_stored_procedures
fields.label: Include Stored Procedures
fields.description: Include ingest of stored procedures.

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for stored procedures to filter in ingestion.Specify regex to match the entire procedure name in database.schema.procedure_name format. e.g. to match all procedures starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_query_lineage
fields.label: Include Query Lineage
fields.description: Enable query-based lineage extraction from pg_stat_statements. Requires the pg_stat_statements extension to be installed and enabled. See documentation for setup instructions.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: include_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

fields.name: format_sql_queries
fields.label: Format Sql Queries
fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

fields.name: include_top_n_queries
fields.label: Include Top N Queries
fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: max_queries_to_extract
fields.label: Max Queries To Extract
fields.description: Maximum number of queries to extract from pg_stat_statements for lineage analysis. Queries are prioritized by execution time and frequency.

fields.name: min_query_calls
fields.label: Min Query Calls
fields.description: Minimum number of executions required for a query to be included. Set higher to focus on frequently-used queries.

fields.name: query_exclude_patterns
fields.label: Query Exclude Patterns
fields.description: SQL LIKE patterns to exclude from query extraction. Example: ['%pg_catalog%', '%temp_%'] to exclude catalog and temp tables.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistics from query history. Requires include_query_lineage to be enabled. Collects metrics like unique user counts, query frequencies, and column access patterns. Statistics appear in DataHub UI under the Dataset Profile > Usage tab.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: auth_mode
fields.label: Auth Mode
fields.description: Authentication mode to use for the PostgreSQL connection. Options are 'PASSWORD' (default) for standard username/password authentication, or 'AWS_IAM' for AWS RDS IAM authentication.

fields.name: initial_database
fields.label: Initial Database
fields.description: Initial database used to query for the list of databases, when ingesting multiple databases. Note: this is not used if `database` or `sqlalchemy_uri` are provided.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: url
fields.label: Url
fields.description: Base URL of your Confluence instance. Examples: 'https://your-domain.atlassian.net/wiki' (Cloud) or 'https://confluence.your-company.com' (Data Center)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Username for Confluence Cloud authentication (required for Cloud).

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: Api Token
fields.description: API token for Confluence Cloud authentication. Generate at: https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens

fields.name: personal_access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Personal Access Token for Confluence Data Center authentication. Generate from: User Profile > Settings > Personal Access Tokens

fields.name: embedding
fields.label: Embedding
fields.description: Configuration for generating vector embeddings for semantic search.

group_fields.name: provider
group_fields.label: Provider
group_fields.description: Embedding provider. 'local' calls a locally-running OpenAI-compatible server (e.g. Ollama). 'vertex_ai' uses GCP. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: endpoint
group_fields.label: Endpoint
group_fields.description: Endpoint URL for local embedding server (e.g., http://localhost:11434/v1/embeddings). Only used when provider='local'. Falls back to LOCAL_EMBEDDING_ENDPOINT env var.

group_fields.name: model
group_fields.label: Model
group_fields.description: Model name. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: model_embedding_key
group_fields.label: Model Embedding Key
group_fields.description: Storage key for embeddings (e.g., 'cohere_embed_v3'). Required if overriding server config. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region for Bedrock. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: vertex_project_id
group_fields.label: Vertex Project Id
group_fields.description: GCP project ID for Vertex AI. Required when provider is 'vertex_ai' and using local config. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: vertex_location
group_fields.label: Vertex Location
group_fields.description: GCP region for the Vertex AI endpoint (e.g., 'us-east1', 'us-central1'). Matches the Java server default.

group_fields.name: api_key
group_fields.label: Api Key
group_fields.description: API key for Cohere (not needed for Bedrock with IAM roles)

group_fields.name: batch_size
group_fields.label: Batch Size
group_fields.description: Batch size for embedding API calls

group_fields.name: request_timeout
group_fields.label: Request Timeout
group_fields.description: Per-call HTTP timeout in seconds for embedding providers.

group_fields.name: input_type
group_fields.label: Input Type
group_fields.description: Input type for Cohere embeddings

group_fields.name: rate_limit
group_fields.label: Rate Limit
group_fields.description: Enable rate limiting for embedding API calls.

group_fields.name: documents_per_minute
group_fields.label: Documents Per Minute
group_fields.description: Maximum number of documents to embed per minute when rate_limit is enabled.

group_fields.name: allow_local_embedding_config
group_fields.label: Allow Local Embedding Config
group_fields.description: BREAK-GLASS: Allow local config without server validation. NOT RECOMMENDED - may break semantic search.

fields.name: ingest_folders
fields.label: Ingest Folders
fields.description: Ingest Confluence folders as document entities so that pages nested under folders keep their place in the hierarchy. Confluence folders are a separate content type that the page APIs never return, so when this is disabled a page whose immediate parent is a folder is emitted without a parent link and appears as a root document.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: cloud
fields.label: Cloud
fields.description: Whether this is a Confluence Cloud instance (True) or Data Center/Server (False).

fields.name: spaces
fields.label: Spaces
fields.description: Configuration for filtering Confluence spaces.

group_fields.name: allow
group_fields.label: Allow
group_fields.description: List of Confluence spaces to include in ingestion. By default, all accessible spaces are discovered. Specify space keys or URLs to limit ingestion to specific spaces. Examples: - Space keys: ['ENGINEERING', 'PRODUCT', 'DESIGN'] - Space URLs: ['https://domain.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TEAM'] - Mixed: ['ENGINEERING', 'https://domain.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/PRODUCT'] If specified, only these spaces will be ingested. Use deny to exclude specific spaces from discovery.

group_fields.name: deny
group_fields.label: Deny
group_fields.description: List of Confluence spaces to exclude from ingestion. Applies after allow filtering. Examples: - Exclude personal spaces: ['~user1', '~user2'] - Exclude specific spaces: ['ARCHIVE', 'OLD_DOCS'] - Space URLs: ['https://domain.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TEST'] Useful for excluding personal spaces or archived content.

fields.name: pages
fields.label: Pages
fields.description: Configuration for filtering Confluence pages.

group_fields.name: allow
group_fields.label: Allow
group_fields.description: List of specific Confluence pages to include in ingestion. By default, all pages in discovered spaces are included. Specify page IDs or URLs to limit ingestion to specific pages and their children. Examples: - Page IDs: ['123456', '789012'] - Page URLs: ['https://domain.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/123456/API-Docs'] When specified, only these page trees will be ingested (if recursive=true). This allows focusing on specific documentation sections.

group_fields.name: deny
group_fields.label: Deny
group_fields.description: List of specific Confluence pages to exclude from ingestion. Applies after allow filtering. Examples: - Exclude specific pages: ['123456', '789012'] - Page URLs: ['https://domain.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/999999/Draft'] Useful for excluding specific pages within otherwise included spaces.

fields.name: max_spaces
fields.label: Max Spaces
fields.description: Maximum number of spaces to ingest when auto-discovering (applies when urls is not set).

fields.name: max_pages_per_space
fields.label: Max Pages Per Space
fields.description: Maximum number of pages to ingest per space.

fields.name: recursive
fields.label: Recursive
fields.description: Whether to recursively fetch child pages (applies to page URLs only).

fields.name: document_import_mode
fields.label: Document Import Mode
fields.description: NATIVE imports editable documents in DataHub. EXTERNAL imports read-only references that link back to Confluence.

fields.name: parent_document_urn
fields.label: Parent Document Urn
fields.description: Optional parent document URN for top-level imported pages.

fields.name: processing
fields.label: Processing
fields.description: Document processing configuration (partitioning strategy, OCR, etc.).

group_fields.name: partition
group_fields.label: Partition
group_fields.description: Partition configuration

group_fields.name: strategy
group_fields.label: Strategy
group_fields.description: Partitioning strategy

group_fields.name: partition_by_api
group_fields.label: Partition By Api
group_fields.description: Use Unstructured API for partitioning

group_fields.name: api_key
group_fields.label: Api Key
group_fields.description: Unstructured API key

group_fields.name: split_pdf_page
group_fields.label: Split Pdf Page
group_fields.description: Enable page-level splitting for large PDFs

group_fields.name: split_pdf_concurrency_level
group_fields.label: Split Pdf Concurrency Level
group_fields.description: Number of parallel requests for PDF pages

group_fields.name: ocr_languages
group_fields.label: Ocr Languages
group_fields.description: Languages for OCR

group_fields.name: additional_args
group_fields.label: Additional Args
group_fields.description: Additional partition arguments

group_fields.name: parallelism
group_fields.label: Parallelism
group_fields.description: Parallelism configuration

group_fields.name: num_processes
group_fields.label: Num Processes
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes

group_fields.name: disable_parallelism
group_fields.label: Disable Parallelism
group_fields.description: Disable all parallelism

group_fields.name: max_connections
group_fields.label: Max Connections
group_fields.description: Max concurrent connections for async operations

fields.name: document_mapping
fields.label: Document Mapping
fields.description: Configuration for mapping Confluence pages to DataHub documents.

group_fields.name: id_pattern
group_fields.label: Id Pattern
group_fields.description: Pattern for generating document IDs

group_fields.name: id_normalization
group_fields.label: Id Normalization
group_fields.description: ID normalization rules

group_fields.name: lowercase
group_fields.label: Lowercase
group_fields.description: Convert to lowercase

group_fields.name: replace_spaces_with
group_fields.label: Replace Spaces With
group_fields.description: Replace spaces with this character

group_fields.name: remove_special_chars
group_fields.label: Remove Special Chars
group_fields.description: Remove special characters except _ and -

group_fields.name: max_length
group_fields.label: Max Length
group_fields.description: Maximum ID length

group_fields.name: title
group_fields.label: Title
group_fields.description: Title extraction configuration

group_fields.name: extract_from_content
group_fields.label: Extract From Content
group_fields.description: Try to extract title from document content

group_fields.name: fallback_to_filename
group_fields.label: Fallback To Filename
group_fields.description: Use filename as title if not found in content

group_fields.name: max_length
group_fields.label: Max Length
group_fields.description: Maximum title length

group_fields.name: source
group_fields.label: Source
group_fields.description: Source configuration

group_fields.name: type
group_fields.label: Type
group_fields.description: Document source type: NATIVE for editable DataHub documents, EXTERNAL for read-only references.

group_fields.name: include_external_url
group_fields.label: Include External Url
group_fields.description: Include external URL in DocumentSource

group_fields.name: include_external_id
group_fields.label: Include External Id
group_fields.description: Include external ID in DocumentSource

group_fields.name: status
group_fields.label: Status
group_fields.description: Default publication status

fields.name: hierarchy
fields.label: Hierarchy
fields.description: Parent-child relationship configuration.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable parent-child relationships

group_fields.name: parent_strategy
group_fields.label: Parent Strategy
group_fields.description: Parent document creation strategy. 'notion' extracts parent from Notion API metadata. 'confluence' extracts parent from Confluence page ancestors.

group_fields.name: folder_mapping
group_fields.label: Folder Mapping
group_fields.description: Folder mapping configuration

group_fields.name: create_parent_docs
group_fields.label: Create Parent Docs
group_fields.description: Create Document entities for folders

group_fields.name: parent_id_pattern
group_fields.label: Parent Id Pattern
group_fields.description: Pattern for parent document IDs

group_fields.name: max_depth
group_fields.label: Max Depth
group_fields.description: Maximum hierarchy depth

group_fields.name: root_parent
group_fields.label: Root Parent
group_fields.description: Optional root document URN

group_fields.name: custom_mapping
group_fields.label: Custom Mapping
group_fields.description: Custom mapping configuration

group_fields.name: rules
group_fields.label: Rules
group_fields.description: Custom parent mapping rules

fields.name: filtering
fields.label: Filtering
fields.description: Filtering options for document content.

group_fields.name: include_patterns
group_fields.label: Include Patterns
group_fields.description: Glob patterns to include

group_fields.name: exclude_patterns
group_fields.label: Exclude Patterns
group_fields.description: Glob patterns to exclude

group_fields.name: min_file_size
group_fields.label: Min File Size
group_fields.description: Minimum file size in bytes

group_fields.name: max_file_size
group_fields.label: Max File Size
group_fields.description: Maximum file size in bytes

group_fields.name: modified_after
group_fields.label: Modified After
group_fields.description: Only files modified after this date (ISO format)

group_fields.name: modified_before
group_fields.label: Modified Before
group_fields.description: Only files modified before this date (ISO format)

group_fields.name: skip_empty_documents
group_fields.label: Skip Empty Documents
group_fields.description: Skip documents with no text content

group_fields.name: min_text_length
group_fields.label: Min Text Length
group_fields.description: Minimum text length in characters

fields.name: chunking
fields.label: Chunking
fields.description: Configuration for document chunking (required for embeddings).

group_fields.name: strategy
group_fields.label: Strategy
group_fields.description: Chunking strategy to use

group_fields.name: max_characters
group_fields.label: Max Characters
group_fields.description: Maximum characters per chunk

group_fields.name: overlap
group_fields.label: Overlap
group_fields.description: Character overlap between chunks

group_fields.name: combine_text_under_n_chars
group_fields.label: Combine Text Under N Chars
group_fields.description: Combine chunks smaller than this size

fields.name: max_documents
fields.label: Max Documents
fields.description: Maximum number of documents to process per ingestion run. The job will stop and fail with an error once this limit is reached. Set to 0 or -1 to disable the limit.

fields.name: advanced
fields.label: Advanced
fields.description: Advanced ingestion options.

group_fields.name: retry
group_fields.label: Retry
group_fields.description: Retry configuration.

group_fields.name: cache
group_fields.label: Cache
group_fields.description: Cache configuration.

group_fields.name: ttl
group_fields.label: Ttl
group_fields.description: Cache TTL in seconds

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: Optional human-readable identifier for this Confluence instance (e.g., 'mycompany-prod', 'team-a-confluence'). If not provided, automatically generated by hashing the base URL, which guarantees global uniqueness across all Confluence installations (both Cloud and Data Center). Use explicit values for more readable URNs, but auto-generated hashes are perfectly fine and require no manual configuration.

fields.name: filename
fields.label: Filename
fields.description: File path or URL of CSV file to ingest.

fields.name: structured_properties
fields.label: Structured Properties
fields.description: Explicit mapping from CSV column names to structured property ids or URNs. Example: {'classification': 'urn:li:structuredProperty:io.acryl.privacy.classification'}

fields.name: write_semantics
fields.label: Write Semantics
fields.description: Whether the new tags, terms and owners to be added will override the existing ones added only by this source or not. Value for this config can be "PATCH" or "OVERRIDE". NOTE: this will apply to all metadata for the entity, not just a single aspect.

fields.name: delimiter
fields.label: Delimiter
fields.description: Delimiter to use when parsing CSV

fields.name: array_delimiter
fields.label: Array Delimiter
fields.description: Delimiter to use when parsing array fields (tags, terms and owners)

fields.name: api_url
fields.label: Api Url
fields.description: Base URL of the Cube REST API, including the base path. For Cube Core this is typically `http://localhost:4000/cubejs-api`; for Cube Cloud it looks like `https://<name>.cubecloud.dev/cubejs-api`.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: Api Token
fields.description: API token used to authenticate against Cube. For Cube Core this is a JWT signed with `CUBEJS_API_SECRET`; for the Cube Cloud Metadata API use a token obtained from the Control Plane API.

fields.name: cloud_api_key
fields.label: Cloud Api Key
fields.description: Cube Cloud Control Plane API key (Account → API keys). When set together with `deployment_id` and `environment_id`, the connector automatically mints a metadata-scoped JWT via the Control Plane `tokens-for-meta-sync` endpoint to access the Metadata API, instead of requiring a pre-generated token in `api_token`.

fields.name: include_cubes
fields.label: Include Cubes
fields.description: Whether to ingest base cubes as datasets.

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether to ingest views as datasets.

fields.name: include_reports
fields.label: Include Reports
fields.description: Cube Cloud only. Whether to ingest saved reports as DataHub charts, with lineage to the cubes/views they query. Requires Platform API access (`cloud_api_key` + `deployment_id`).

fields.name: include_workbooks
fields.label: Include Workbooks
fields.description: Cube Cloud only. Whether to ingest workbooks as DataHub dashboards containing their reports' charts. Requires Platform API access (`cloud_api_key` + `deployment_id`).

fields.name: cube_pattern
fields.label: Cube Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering cubes to ingest (matched on the cube name).

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering views to ingest (matched on the view name).

fields.name: report_pattern
fields.label: Report Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering reports to ingest (matched on the report name).

fields.name: workbook_pattern
fields.label: Workbook Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering workbooks to ingest (matched on the workbook name).

fields.name: include_hidden
fields.label: Include Hidden
fields.description: Whether to ingest cubes, views, and members that Cube marks as hidden (`public: false` / `isVisible: false`). Hidden cubes are typically excluded from Cube's own API consumers; enable this to surface them in DataHub anyway.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Whether to emit lineage. This includes view->cube lineage and, where available, lineage from cubes to their upstream warehouse tables.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: Whether to emit column-level (fine-grained) lineage. Requires `include_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: parse_sql_for_lineage
fields.label: Parse Sql For Lineage
fields.description: Cube Core only. When the `/v1/meta?extended` response includes a cube's SQL definition, parse it to derive upstream warehouse lineage. Requires `warehouse_platform` to be set. The Cloud Metadata API provides lineage directly, so this is ignored for Cube Cloud.

fields.name: convert_lineage_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Lineage Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to lowercase upstream warehouse table and column names when building lineage URNs. Must match the `convert_urns_to_lowercase` setting of the warehouse connector (e.g. Snowflake ingests lowercased URNs by default) so that the lineage edges resolve.

fields.name: tag_measures_and_dimensions
fields.label: Tag Measures And Dimensions
fields.description: Whether to tag schema fields with `Measure`/`Dimension` (and `Temporal` for time dimensions) so the kinds of Cube members can be distinguished and filtered in DataHub.

fields.name: tag_prefix
fields.label: Tag Prefix
fields.description: Prefix added to tags created via `meta_mapping`.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: deployment_type
fields.label: Deployment Type
fields.description: Whether the target is Cube Core (`CORE`) or Cube Cloud (`CLOUD`).

fields.name: use_metadata_api
fields.label: Use Metadata Api
fields.description: Cube Cloud only. When enabled, the richer Metadata API (`/v1/entities`) is used to extract warehouse and column-level lineage, which is merged with the structural metadata from `/v1/meta`.
