When disabled, only the `/v1/meta` endpoint is used. Has no effect for Cube Core deployments.

fields.name: cloud_api_url
fields.label: Cloud Api Url
fields.description: Base URL of the Cube Cloud Control Plane API (e.g. `https://<tenant>.cubecloud.dev`). If unset, it is derived from the scheme and host of `api_url`. Only used when `cloud_api_key` is set.

fields.name: deployment_id
fields.label: Deployment Id
fields.description: Cube Cloud deployment id, used to mint a Metadata API token via the Control Plane API.

fields.name: environment_id
fields.label: Environment Id
fields.description: Cube Cloud environment id, used to mint a Metadata API token via the Control Plane API.

fields.name: security_context
fields.label: Security Context
fields.description: Security context embedded in the minted Metadata API token. Controls which parts of the data model are visible, following Cube's multi-tenancy rules.

fields.name: meta_sync_token_expires_in
fields.label: Meta Sync Token Expires In
fields.description: Expiry (in seconds) of the minted Metadata API token. Defaults to 24 hours.

fields.name: request_timeout_sec
fields.label: Request Timeout Sec
fields.description: Per-request timeout, in seconds.

fields.name: warehouse_platform
fields.label: Warehouse Platform
fields.description: DataHub platform name of the warehouse that backs the Cube data model (e.g. `snowflake`, `bigquery`, `postgres`). Used to build upstream lineage URNs. If unset, it is auto-detected from the Cube data source type when the Metadata API is available.

fields.name: warehouse_platform_instance
fields.label: Warehouse Platform Instance
fields.description: Platform instance of the upstream warehouse, used when building lineage URNs.

fields.name: warehouse_env
fields.label: Warehouse Env
fields.description: Environment of the upstream warehouse datasets referenced by lineage.

fields.name: warehouse_database
fields.label: Warehouse Database
fields.description: Database name to prepend to upstream warehouse table references that do not already include one. If unset, it is taken from the Cube data source definition when available.

fields.name: deployment_url
fields.label: Deployment Url
fields.description: Base URL of the Cube deployment UI, used to build an external link on the deployment container. If unset, it is derived from `api_url` by stripping the API base path.

fields.name: emit_member_details
fields.label: Emit Member Details
fields.description: Whether to capture Cube member presentation hints (format, drill-down members, cumulative flag) as schema-field `jsonProps`, and structural metadata (joins, hierarchies, folders, pre-aggregations) as dataset custom properties.

fields.name: enable_meta_mapping
fields.label: Enable Meta Mapping
fields.description: Whether to process `meta_mapping` and `column_meta_mapping` rules.

fields.name: meta_mapping
fields.label: Meta Mapping
fields.description: Mapping rules applied to the `meta` of each cube/view to derive tags, glossary terms, owners, domains, and documentation links. Uses the same syntax as the dbt connector's `meta_mapping`.

fields.name: column_meta_mapping
fields.label: Column Meta Mapping
fields.description: Mapping rules applied to the `meta` of each measure/dimension to derive schema-field tags and glossary terms.

fields.name: strip_user_ids_from_email
fields.label: Strip User Ids From Email
fields.description: Whether to strip the email domain from owners derived via `meta_mapping`.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Regex patterns matched against a cube/view name to assign it to a DataHub domain (keyed by domain id or urn).

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: database_connection
fields.label: Database Connection
fields.description: Database connection config

group_fields.name: username
group_fields.label: Username
group_fields.description: username

group_fields.name: password
group_fields.label: Password
group_fields.description: password

group_fields.name: host_port
group_fields.label: Host Port
group_fields.description: host URL

group_fields.name: database
group_fields.label: Database
group_fields.description: database (catalog)

group_fields.name: scheme
group_fields.label: Scheme
group_fields.description: scheme

group_fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
group_fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
group_fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

group_fields.name: options
group_fields.label: Options
group_fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: include_all_versions
fields.label: Include All Versions
fields.description: If enabled, include all versions of each aspect. Otherwise, only include the latest version of each aspect.

fields.name: include_soft_deleted_entities
fields.label: Include Soft Deleted Entities
fields.description: If enabled, include entities that have been soft deleted. Otherwise, include all entities regardless of removal status.

fields.name: urn_pattern
fields.label: Urn Pattern
fields.description: URN patterns to filter entities. Defaults exclude environment-specific entities (ingestion sources, secrets, settings) to prevent copying encrypted credentials and creating broken entities. Recommended to keep these defaults when customizing.

fields.name: database_query_batch_size
fields.label: Database Query Batch Size
fields.description: Number of records to fetch from the database at a time

fields.name: query_timeout
fields.label: Query Timeout
fields.description: Timeout for each query in seconds.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

fields.name: kafka_connection
fields.label: Kafka Connection
fields.description: Kafka connection config

group_fields.name: schema_registry_url
group_fields.label: Schema Registry Url
group_fields.description: Schema registry URL. Can be overridden with KAFKA_SCHEMAREGISTRY_URL environment variable, or will use DATAHUB_GMS_BASE_PATH if not set.

group_fields.name: schema_registry_config
group_fields.label: Schema Registry Config
group_fields.description: Extra schema registry config serialized as JSON. These options will be passed into Kafka's SchemaRegistryClient. https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/clients/confluent-kafka-python/html/index.html?#schemaregistryclient

group_fields.name: client_timeout_seconds
group_fields.label: Client Timeout Seconds
group_fields.description: The request timeout used when interacting with the Kafka APIs.

group_fields.name: consumer_config
group_fields.label: Consumer Config
group_fields.description: Extra consumer config serialized as JSON. These options will be passed into Kafka's DeserializingConsumer. See https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/clients/confluent-kafka-python/html/index.html#deserializingconsumer and https://github.com/edenhill/librdkafka/blob/master/CONFIGURATION.md .

fields.name: exclude_aspects
fields.label: Exclude Aspects
fields.description: Aspect names to exclude from entities that are being ingested. Note: This only makes sense for entities you want to ingest but without certain aspects. To completely exclude entity types, use 'urn_pattern.deny' instead. Warning: Excluding key aspects while keeping others can create invalid entities.

fields.name: database_table_name
fields.label: Database Table Name
fields.description: Name of database table containing all versioned aspects

fields.name: kafka_topic_name
fields.label: Kafka Topic Name
fields.description: Name of kafka topic containing timeseries MCLs

fields.name: commit_state_interval
fields.label: Commit State Interval
fields.description: Number of records to process before committing state

fields.name: commit_with_parse_errors
fields.label: Commit With Parse Errors
fields.description: Whether to update createdon timestamp and kafka offset despite parse errors. Enable if you want to ignore the errors.

fields.name: drop_duplicate_schema_fields
fields.label: Drop Duplicate Schema Fields
fields.description: Whether to drop duplicate schema fields in the schemaMetadata aspect. Useful if the source system has duplicate field paths in the db, but we're pushing to a system with server-side duplicate checking.

fields.name: preserve_system_metadata
fields.label: Preserve System Metadata
fields.description: Copy system metadata from the source system

fields.name: tag_apply
fields.label: Tag Apply
fields.description: List to apply tags to assets

fields.name: owner_apply
fields.label: Owner Apply
fields.description: List to apply owners to assets

fields.name: domain_apply
fields.label: Domain Apply
fields.description: List to apply domains to assets

fields.name: term_apply
fields.label: Term Apply
fields.description: List to apply terms to assets

fields.name: file
fields.label: File
fields.description: File path or URL to business glossary file to ingest.

fields.name: enable_auto_id
fields.label: Enable Auto Id
fields.description: Generate guid urns instead of a plaintext path urn with the node/term's hierarchy.

fields.name: datahub
fields.label: Datahub
fields.description: DataHub connection configuration. Only used when running standalone (e.g., CLI ingestion). In managed ingestion (deployed sources), the connection is automatically configured from the sink.

group_fields.name: server
group_fields.label: Server
group_fields.description: DataHub GMS server URL (defaults to DATAHUB_GMS_URL env var, ~/.datahubenv, or localhost:8080)

group_fields.name: token
group_fields.label: Token
group_fields.description: DataHub API token for authentication (defaults to DATAHUB_GMS_TOKEN env var or ~/.datahubenv)

fields.name: embedding
fields.label: Embedding
fields.description: Embedding generation configuration (Bedrock, Cohere, OpenAI, or Vertex AI)

group_fields.name: provider
group_fields.label: Provider
group_fields.description: Embedding provider. 'local' calls a locally-running OpenAI-compatible server (e.g. Ollama). 'vertex_ai' uses GCP. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: endpoint
group_fields.label: Endpoint
group_fields.description: Endpoint URL for local embedding server (e.g., http://localhost:11434/v1/embeddings). Only used when provider='local'. Falls back to LOCAL_EMBEDDING_ENDPOINT env var.

group_fields.name: model
group_fields.label: Model
group_fields.description: Model name. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: model_embedding_key
group_fields.label: Model Embedding Key
group_fields.description: Storage key for embeddings (e.g., 'cohere_embed_v3'). Required if overriding server config. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region for Bedrock. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: vertex_project_id
group_fields.label: Vertex Project Id
group_fields.description: GCP project ID for Vertex AI. Required when provider is 'vertex_ai' and using local config. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: vertex_location
group_fields.label: Vertex Location
group_fields.description: GCP region for the Vertex AI endpoint (e.g., 'us-east1', 'us-central1'). Matches the Java server default.

group_fields.name: api_key
group_fields.label: Api Key
group_fields.description: API key for Cohere (not needed for Bedrock with IAM roles)

group_fields.name: batch_size
group_fields.label: Batch Size
group_fields.description: Batch size for embedding API calls

group_fields.name: request_timeout
group_fields.label: Request Timeout
group_fields.description: Per-call HTTP timeout in seconds for embedding providers.

group_fields.name: input_type
group_fields.label: Input Type
group_fields.description: Input type for Cohere embeddings

group_fields.name: rate_limit
group_fields.label: Rate Limit
group_fields.description: Enable rate limiting for embedding API calls.

group_fields.name: documents_per_minute
group_fields.label: Documents Per Minute
group_fields.description: Maximum number of documents to embed per minute when rate_limit is enabled.

group_fields.name: allow_local_embedding_config
group_fields.label: Allow Local Embedding Config
group_fields.description: BREAK-GLASS: Allow local config without server validation. NOT RECOMMENDED - may break semantic search.

fields.name: include_external_documents
fields.label: Include External Documents
fields.description: Index EXTERNAL documents (sourceType=EXTERNAL), not just NATIVE ones. When platform_filter is set, EXTERNAL documents are still restricted to those platforms; when platform_filter is empty, all EXTERNAL documents are included. Set to False to restore NATIVE-only behavior.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration. Enabled by default to support incremental mode (document hash tracking) and event mode (offset tracking).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

fields.name: platform_filter
fields.label: Platform Filter
fields.description: Filter documents by platforms. Default (None): Process all NATIVE documents (sourceType=NATIVE) regardless of platform. EXTERNAL documents are also processed by default (see include_external_documents); set platform_filter to restrict EXTERNAL documents to specific platforms (e.g., ['notion', 'confluence']). Use ['*'] or ['ALL'] to process all documents regardless of source type or platform.

fields.name: document_urns
fields.label: Document Urns
fields.description: Specific document URNs to process (if None, process all matching platforms)

fields.name: event_mode
fields.label: Event Mode
fields.description: Event-driven mode configuration (polls Kafka MCL events)

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable event-driven mode (polls MCL events instead of GraphQL batch)

group_fields.name: consumer_id
group_fields.label: Consumer Id
group_fields.description: Consumer ID for offset tracking (defaults to 'datahub-documents-{pipeline_name}')

group_fields.name: topics
group_fields.label: Topics
group_fields.description: Topics to consume for document changes

group_fields.name: lookback_days
group_fields.label: Lookback Days
group_fields.description: Number of days to look back for events on first run (None means start from latest)

group_fields.name: reset_offsets
group_fields.label: Reset Offsets
group_fields.description: Reset consumer offsets to start from beginning

group_fields.name: idle_timeout_seconds
group_fields.label: Idle Timeout Seconds
group_fields.description: Exit after this many seconds with no new events (incremental batch mode)

group_fields.name: poll_timeout_seconds
group_fields.label: Poll Timeout Seconds
group_fields.description: Timeout for each poll request

group_fields.name: poll_limit
group_fields.label: Poll Limit
group_fields.description: Maximum number of events to fetch per poll

fields.name: incremental
fields.label: Incremental
fields.description: Incremental processing configuration (skip unchanged documents)

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Only process documents whose text content has changed (tracks content hash). Uses stateful ingestion when enabled. The state_file_path option is deprecated and ignored when stateful ingestion is enabled.

group_fields.name: state_file_path
group_fields.label: State File Path
group_fields.description: [DEPRECATED] Path to state file. This option is ignored when stateful ingestion is enabled. State is now managed through DataHub's stateful ingestion framework.

group_fields.name: force_reprocess
group_fields.label: Force Reprocess
group_fields.description: Force reprocess all documents regardless of content hash

fields.name: chunking
fields.label: Chunking
fields.description: Text chunking strategy configuration

group_fields.name: strategy
group_fields.label: Strategy
group_fields.description: Chunking strategy to use

group_fields.name: max_characters
group_fields.label: Max Characters
group_fields.description: Maximum characters per chunk

group_fields.name: overlap
group_fields.label: Overlap
group_fields.description: Character overlap between chunks

group_fields.name: combine_text_under_n_chars
group_fields.label: Combine Text Under N Chars
group_fields.description: Combine chunks smaller than this size

fields.name: max_documents
fields.label: Max Documents
fields.description: Maximum number of documents to process per ingestion run. The job will stop and fail with an error once this limit is reached. This is a runaway-cost guardrail; it counts documents actually indexed, so incremental runs rarely approach it and it mainly bounds initial bootstraps/backfills. Set to 0 or -1 to disable the limit.

fields.name: scroll_batch_size
fields.label: Scroll Batch Size
fields.description: Number of documents to fetch per scrollAcrossEntities page in batch mode. scrollAcrossEntities uses cursor-based pagination, so all documents are fetched across pages regardless of this value (no 10k Elasticsearch window limit). Tune for throughput vs. memory/load on GMS.

fields.name: scroll_delay_seconds
fields.label: Scroll Delay Seconds
fields.description: Delay in seconds between consecutive scroll page requests in batch mode. Use a small delay to reduce read load on GMS/Elasticsearch when scrolling large document sets; set to 0 to disable.

fields.name: index_delay_seconds
fields.label: Index Delay Seconds
fields.description: Delay in seconds after each document that is actually indexed (embeddings emitted). Skipped documents (unchanged, externally owned, empty, etc.) are not delayed. Defaults to 0.025s to smooth write load during bootstrap without unduly slowing large backfills (e.g. ~42 min of added pacing for 100k new documents); set to 0 to disable. Sub-second values are supported.

fields.name: skip_external_if_semantic_content_exists
fields.label: Skip External If Semantic Content Exists
fields.description: For EXTERNAL documents only: before processing, check whether the document already has a semanticContent aspect for the active embedding model and, if so, skip it and leave it out of our incremental state. Some ingestion sources (e.g. Notion, Confluence) perform their own semantic indexing, so an existing aspect means that source owns the document. This source then only picks up EXTERNAL documents the source did NOT index and takes ownership of them. NATIVE documents are never skipped this way. Adds one metadata read per candidate EXTERNAL document not already in state. Incremental content-hash skipping still applies once a document is owned. Set to False to always re-embed EXTERNAL documents.

fields.name: locking
fields.label: Locking
fields.description: Distributed lock configuration to prevent overlapping scheduled runs from duplicating scroll + indexing work.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Acquire a distributed lock before processing so overlapping scheduled runs do not duplicate scroll + indexing work. When the lock is held by another run, this run exits cleanly without processing.

group_fields.name: lock_id
group_fields.label: Lock Id
group_fields.description: Identifier for the lock's dataHubStepState entity. Defaults to 'datahub-documents-lock-{pipeline_name}'. Runs sharing a lock_id are mutually exclusive; give unrelated pipelines distinct ids.

group_fields.name: lock_ttl_seconds
group_fields.label: Lock Ttl Seconds
group_fields.description: Lease duration in seconds. The lock holder renews its lease periodically (see lock_renewal_interval_seconds) while it runs, so this only needs to exceed the renewal interval (with margin for transient write failures), NOT the total run duration. A lease not renewed within this window is treated as stale (e.g. from a crashed run) and may be taken over. After a crash, future runs are blocked for at most this long.

group_fields.name: lock_renewal_interval_seconds
group_fields.label: Lock Renewal Interval Seconds
group_fields.description: How often the lock holder renews (extends) its lease while running. Must be comfortably smaller than lock_ttl_seconds. This lets a job run for hours with a short TTL: the lease stays alive as long as the run is healthy.

fields.name: partition_strategy
fields.label: Partition Strategy
fields.description: Text partitioning strategy. Currently only 'markdown' is supported. This field is included in the document hash to trigger reprocessing if the strategy changes.

fields.name: skip_empty_text
fields.label: Skip Empty Text
fields.description: Skip documents with no text content

fields.name: min_text_length
fields.label: Min Text Length
fields.description: Minimum text length in characters to process (shorter documents are skipped)

fields.name: dry_run
fields.label: Dry Run
fields.description: Whether to perform a dry run or not. This is only supported for dataprocess cleanup and soft deleted entities cleanup.

fields.name: cleanup_expired_tokens
fields.label: Cleanup Expired Tokens
fields.description: Whether to clean up expired tokens or not

fields.name: truncate_indices
fields.label: Truncate Indices
fields.description: Whether to truncate elasticsearch indices or not which can be safely truncated

fields.name: truncate_index_older_than_days
fields.label: Truncate Index Older Than Days
fields.description: Indices older than this number of days will be truncated

fields.name: truncation_watch_until
fields.label: Truncation Watch Until
fields.description: Wait for truncation of indices until this number of documents are left

fields.name: truncation_sleep_between_seconds
fields.label: Truncation Sleep Between Seconds
fields.description: Sleep between truncation monitoring.

fields.name: dataprocess_cleanup
fields.label: Dataprocess Cleanup
fields.description: Configuration for data process cleanup

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do data process cleanup.

group_fields.name: retention_days
group_fields.label: Retention Days
group_fields.description: Number of days to retain metadata in DataHub

group_fields.name: aspects_to_clean
group_fields.label: Aspects To Clean
group_fields.description: List of aspect names to clean up

group_fields.name: keep_last_n
group_fields.label: Keep Last N
group_fields.description: Number of latest aspects to keep

group_fields.name: delete_empty_data_jobs
group_fields.label: Delete Empty Data Jobs
group_fields.description: Whether to delete Data Jobs without runs

group_fields.name: delete_empty_data_flows
group_fields.label: Delete Empty Data Flows
group_fields.description: Whether to delete Data Flows without runs

group_fields.name: hard_delete_entities
group_fields.label: Hard Delete Entities
group_fields.description: Whether to hard delete entities

group_fields.name: batch_size
group_fields.label: Batch Size
group_fields.description: The number of entities to get in a batch from API

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: The number of workers to use for deletion

group_fields.name: delay
group_fields.label: Delay
group_fields.description: Delay between each batch

fields.name: soft_deleted_entities_cleanup
fields.label: Soft Deleted Entities Cleanup
fields.description: Configuration for soft deleted entities cleanup

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do soft deletion cleanup.

group_fields.name: retention_days
group_fields.label: Retention Days
group_fields.description: Number of days to retain metadata in DataHub

group_fields.name: batch_size
group_fields.label: Batch Size
group_fields.description: The number of entities to get in a batch from GraphQL

group_fields.name: delay
group_fields.label: Delay
group_fields.description: Delay between each batch

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: The number of workers to use for deletion

group_fields.name: entity_types
group_fields.label: Entity Types
group_fields.description: List of entity types to cleanup

group_fields.name: platform
group_fields.label: Platform
group_fields.description: Platform to cleanup

group_fields.name: env
group_fields.label: Env
group_fields.description: Environment to cleanup

group_fields.name: query
group_fields.label: Query
group_fields.description: Query to filter entities

group_fields.name: limit_entities_delete
group_fields.label: Limit Entities Delete
group_fields.description: Max number of entities to delete.

group_fields.name: futures_max_at_time
group_fields.label: Futures Max At Time
group_fields.description: Max number of futures to have at a time.

group_fields.name: runtime_limit_seconds
group_fields.label: Runtime Limit Seconds
group_fields.description: Runtime limit in seconds

fields.name: execution_request_cleanup
fields.label: Execution Request Cleanup
fields.description: Configuration for execution request cleanup

group_fields.name: keep_history_min_count
group_fields.label: Keep History Min Count
group_fields.description: Minimum number of execution requests to keep, per ingestion source

group_fields.name: keep_history_max_count
group_fields.label: Keep History Max Count
group_fields.description: Maximum number of execution requests to keep, per ingestion source

group_fields.name: keep_history_max_days
group_fields.label: Keep History Max Days
group_fields.description: Maximum number of days to keep execution requests for, per ingestion source

group_fields.name: batch_read_size
group_fields.label: Batch Read Size
group_fields.description: Number of records per read operation

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Global switch for this cleanup task

group_fields.name: runtime_limit_seconds
group_fields.label: Runtime Limit Seconds
group_fields.description: Maximum runtime in seconds for the cleanup task

group_fields.name: limit_entities_delete
group_fields.label: Limit Entities Delete
group_fields.description: Max number of execution requests to hard delete.

group_fields.name: max_read_errors
group_fields.label: Max Read Errors
group_fields.description: Maximum number of read errors before aborting

fields.name: file
fields.label: File
fields.description: File path or URL to lineage file to ingest.

fields.name: preserve_upstream
fields.label: Preserve Upstream
fields.description: Whether we want to query datahub-gms for upstream data. False means it will hard replace upstream data for a given entity. True means it will query the backend for existing upstreams and include it in the ingestion run

fields.name: enabled
fields.label: Enabled
fields.description: Whether this source is enabled

fields.name: throw_uncaught_exceptions
fields.label: Throw Uncaught Exceptions
fields.description: Whether to throw an uncaught exception for testing

fields.name: num_errors
fields.label: Num Errors
fields.description: Number of errors to add in report for testing

fields.name: num_warnings
fields.label: Num Warnings
fields.description: Number of warnings to add in report for testing

fields.name: num_info
fields.label: Num Info
fields.description: Number of info to add in report for testing

fields.name: gen_1
fields.label: Gen 1
fields.description: Configuration for lineage data generation

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether this source is enabled

group_fields.name: table_name_prefix
group_fields.label: Table Name Prefix
group_fields.description: Prefix to add to the table name. This is useful for testing purposes.

group_fields.name: emit_lineage
group_fields.label: Emit Lineage
group_fields.description: Whether to emit lineage data for testing purposes. When False, no lineage data is generated regardless of other settings.

group_fields.name: emit_usage
group_fields.label: Emit Usage
group_fields.description: Whether to emit usage data for testing purposes. When False, no usage data is generated regardless of other settings.

group_fields.name: lineage_fan_out
group_fields.label: Lineage Fan Out
group_fields.description: Number of downstream tables that each upstream table connects to. This controls the 'width' of the lineage graph. Higher values create more parallel downstream tables per level.

group_fields.name: lineage_hops
group_fields.label: Lineage Hops
group_fields.description: Number of hops (levels) in the lineage graph. This controls the 'depth' of the lineage graph. Level 0 is the root table, and each subsequent level contains downstream tables. Higher values create deeper lineage chains.

group_fields.name: lineage_fan_out_after_first_hop
group_fields.label: Lineage Fan Out After First Hop
group_fields.description: Optional limit on fanout for hops after the first hop. When set, prevents exponential growth by limiting the number of downstream tables per upstream table at levels 2 and beyond. When None, uses the standard exponential growth (lineage_fan_out^level).

group_fields.name: subtype_pattern
group_fields.label: Subtype Pattern
group_fields.description: Pattern for determining SubTypes. Options: 'alternating', 'all_table', 'all_view', 'level_based'

group_fields.name: subtype_types
group_fields.label: Subtype Types
group_fields.description: List of types to use in alternating pattern. Defaults to ['Table', 'View'].

group_fields.name: level_subtypes
group_fields.label: Level Subtypes
group_fields.description: Mapping of level to subtype for level_based pattern

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project Id
fields.description: Project id to set the credentials

fields.name: private_key_id
fields.label: Private Key Id
fields.description: Private key id

fields.name: private_key
fields.label: Private Key
fields.description: Private key in a form of '<PEM block>\n'

fields.name: client_email
fields.label: Client Email
fields.description: Client email

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Client Id

fields.name: auth_uri
fields.label: Auth Uri
fields.description: Authentication uri

fields.name: token_uri
fields.label: Token Uri
fields.description: Token uri

fields.name: auth_provider_x509_cert_url
fields.label: Auth Provider X509 Cert Url
fields.description: Auth provider x509 certificate url

fields.name: type
fields.label: Type
fields.description: Authentication type

fields.name: client_x509_cert_url
fields.label: Client X509 Cert Url
fields.description: If not set it will be default to https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/client_email

fields.name: project_ids
fields.label: Project Ids
fields.description: Explicit list of GCP project ids to ingest. Overrides project_id_pattern.

fields.name: project_labels
fields.label: Project Labels
fields.description: Filter projects by labels in `key:value` format. Applied before project_id_pattern.

fields.name: project_id_pattern
fields.label: Project Id Pattern
fields.description: Regex allow/deny pattern for GCP project ids.

fields.name: include_schema
fields.label: Include Schema
fields.description: Whether to extract and ingest schema metadata (columns, types, descriptions). Set to False to skip schema extraction for faster ingestion when only basic dataset metadata is needed. Disabling schema extraction can improve performance for large deployments. Default: True.

fields.name: include_glossaries
fields.label: Include Glossaries
fields.description: Whether to ingest Dataplex Business Glossary entities as DataHub GlossaryNodes and GlossaryTerms. Glossaries, categories, and terms are emitted with correct parent hierarchy. Default: True.

fields.name: include_glossary_term_associations
fields.label: Include Glossary Term Associations
fields.description: Whether to ingest term-to-asset associations via the Dataplex lookupEntryLinks API. For each ingested term, all entries_locations are queried per project to find linked assets. Requires a role granting resourcemanager.projects.get (e.g. roles/browser) on all configured projects to resolve GCP project numbers needed by the lookupEntryLinks API.

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract lineage information using Dataplex Lineage API. Extracts table-level lineage relationships between entries. Lineage API calls automatically retry transient errors (timeouts, rate limits) with exponential backoff.

fields.name: lineage_locations
fields.label: Lineage Locations
fields.description: List of GCP regions to scan for Dataplex lineage data. By default, includes all supported multi-regions and regions. Narrowing this list from the default is critical for better performance because lineage API calls scale with configured project/location pairs. This list may include multi-regions and single regions. In practice, lineage often resides in job regions while entries may be in multi-regions, so entries_locations and lineage_locations are configured separately. Example: ['eu', 'us-central1', 'europe-west1'].

fields.name: lineage_max_retries
fields.label: Lineage Max Retries
fields.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for lineage API calls when encountering transient errors (timeouts, rate limits, service unavailable). Each attempt uses exponential backoff. Higher values increase resilience but may slow down ingestion. Default: 3.

fields.name: lineage_retry_backoff_multiplier
fields.label: Lineage Retry Backoff Multiplier
fields.description: Multiplier for exponential backoff between lineage API retry attempts (in seconds). Wait time formula: multiplier * (2 ^ attempt_number), capped between 2-10 seconds. Higher values reduce API load but increase ingestion time. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: max_workers_lineage
fields.label: Max Workers Lineage
fields.description: Number of parallel worker threads for lineage lookups (search_links API calls). Lineage lookup volume scales with entries × lineage_locations, so parallelism here has a large impact on total ingestion time. Increase for large entry × location matrices. Default: 10.

fields.name: enable_stateful_lineage_ingestion
fields.label: Enable Stateful Lineage Ingestion
fields.description: Enable stateful lineage ingestion. This will store lineage window timestamps after successful lineage ingestion. and will not run lineage ingestion for same timestamps in subsequent run. NOTE: This only works with use_queries_v2=False (legacy extraction path). For queries v2, use enable_stateful_time_window instead.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration for stale metadata removal.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: entries_locations
fields.label: Entries Locations
fields.description: List of GCP regions to scan for Universal Catalog entries extraction. This list may include multi-regions (for example 'us', 'eu', 'asia') and single regions (for example 'us-central1'). Entries scanning runs across all configured entries_locations. Default: ['us', 'eu', 'asia', 'global'].

fields.name: filter_config
fields.label: Filter Config
fields.description: Filters to control which Dataplex resources are ingested.

group_fields.name: entry_groups
group_fields.label: Entry Groups
group_fields.description: Filters for Dataplex entry group names.

group_fields.name: pattern
group_fields.label: Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for entry group resource names to include/exclude.

group_fields.name: entries
group_fields.label: Entries
group_fields.description: Filters specific to Dataplex Entries API (Universal Catalog).

group_fields.name: pattern
group_fields.label: Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for Dataplex entry names to filter in ingestion.

group_fields.name: fqn_pattern
group_fields.label: Fqn Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for Dataplex fully-qualified names to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: max_workers_entries
fields.label: Max Workers Entries
fields.description: Number of parallel worker threads for fetching entry details (get_entry API calls). Entry detail fetching is the main bottleneck in the entries stage because each entry requires one blocking RPC. Increasing this value reduces wall-clock time proportionally up to the API quota limit. Increase for large deployments (>1k entries). Default: 10.

fields.name: glossary_locations
fields.label: Glossary Locations
fields.description: GCP locations to scan for Dataplex Business Glossaries. Dataplex glossaries are typically created in 'global' but can exist in any location. Default: ['global'].

fields.name: max_workers_glossary
fields.label: Max Workers Glossary
fields.description: Number of parallel worker threads for glossary ingestion (fetching terms and categories per glossary) and term-asset association traversal (lookupEntryLinks calls). Default: 10.

fields.name: dataplex_url
fields.label: Dataplex Url
fields.description: Base URL for Dataplex console (for generating external links).

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: Db2 host URL.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The Db2 database to ingest from.

fields.name: scheme
fields.label: Scheme
fields.description: ibm_db_sa scheme to use (db2, db2+pyodbc, db2+pyodbc400).

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: include_stored_procedures
fields.label: Include Stored Procedures
fields.description: Ingest stored procedures.

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for stored procedures to filter in ingestion.Specify regex to match the entire procedure name in schema.procedure_name format.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: uri_args
fields.label: Uri Args
fields.description: Arguments to add to the URL when connecting.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: target_platform
fields.label: Target Platform
fields.description: The platform that dbt is loading onto. (e.g. bigquery / redshift / postgres etc.)

fields.name: manifest_path
fields.label: Manifest Path
fields.description: Path to dbt manifest JSON. See https://docs.getdbt.com/reference/artifacts/manifest-json. This can be a local file or a URI.

fields.name: aws_connection
fields.label: Aws Connection
fields.description: When fetching manifest files from s3, configuration for aws connection details

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: node_name_pattern
fields.label: Node Name Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for dbt model names to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: materialized_node_pattern
fields.label: Materialized Node Pattern
fields.description: Advanced filtering for materialized nodes based on their physical database location. Provides fine-grained control over database.schema.table patterns for catalog consistency.

group_fields.name: database_pattern
group_fields.label: Database Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for database names to filter materialized nodes.

group_fields.name: schema_pattern
group_fields.label: Schema Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for schema names in format '{database}.{schema}' to filter materialized nodes.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for table/view names in format '{database}.{schema}.{table}' to filter materialized nodes.

fields.name: include_env_in_assertion_guid
fields.label: Include Env In Assertion Guid
fields.description: Prior to version 0.9.4.2, the assertion GUIDs did not include the environment. If you're using multiple dbt ingestion that are only distinguished by env, then you should set this flag to True.

fields.name: include_compiled_code
fields.label: Include Compiled Code
fields.description: When enabled, includes the compiled code in the emitted metadata.

fields.name: include_database_name
fields.label: Include Database Name
fields.description: Whether to add database name to the table urn. Set to False to skip it for engines like AWS Athena where it's not required.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits incremental/patch lineage for non-dbt entities. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run. This would also require enabling 'incremental_lineage' in the counterpart warehouse ingestion (_e.g._ BigQuery, Redshift, etc).

fields.name: prefer_sql_parser_lineage
fields.label: Prefer Sql Parser Lineage
fields.description: Normally we use dbt's metadata to generate table lineage. When enabled, we prefer results from the SQL parser when generating lineage instead. This can be useful when dbt models reference tables directly, instead of using the ref() macro. This requires that `skip_sources_in_lineage` is enabled.

fields.name: skip_sources_in_lineage
fields.label: Skip Sources In Lineage
fields.description: [Experimental] When enabled, dbt sources will not be included in the lineage graph. Requires that `entities_enabled.sources` is set to `NO`. This is mainly useful when you have multiple, interdependent dbt projects.

fields.name: skip_missing_upstreams_in_lineage
fields.label: Skip Missing Upstreams In Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, upstream datasets that do not already exist in DataHub are excluded from lineage, preventing dangling graph edges from appearing in the lineage UI. Typically used together with `skip_sources_in_lineage` and `entities_enabled.sources: NO`. Important caveats: (1) if dbt is ingested before its upstream source systems, those lineage edges will be silently omitted until dbt is re-ingested after the upstreams are present; (2) adds one graph.exists() round-trip per unique upstream URN per run (cached within the run); (3) soft-deleted upstream entities are treated as present. Requires a DataHub graph connection.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, column-level lineage will be extracted from the dbt node definition. Requires `infer_dbt_schemas` to be enabled. If you run into issues where the column name casing does not match up with properly, providing a datahub_api or using the rest sink will improve accuracy.

fields.name: max_queries_per_model
fields.label: Max Queries Per Model
fields.description: Maximum number of Query entities to emit per dbt model. Prevents metadata explosion from malformed manifests. Set to 0 for unlimited.

fields.name: tag_prefix
fields.label: Tag Prefix
fields.description: Prefix added to tags during ingestion.

fields.name: query_tag_mapping
fields.label: Query Tag Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against dbt query_tag meta properties. Refer to the section below on dbt meta automated mappings.

fields.name: enable_query_tag_mapping
fields.label: Enable Query Tag Mapping
fields.description: When enabled, applies the mappings that are defined through the `query_tag_mapping` directives.

fields.name: enable_owner_extraction
fields.label: Enable Owner Extraction
fields.description: When enabled, ownership info will be extracted from the dbt meta

fields.name: owner_extraction_pattern
fields.label: Owner Extraction Pattern
fields.description: Regex string to extract owner from the dbt node using the `(?P<name>...) syntax` of the [match object](https://docs.python.org/3/library/re.html#match-objects), where the group name must be `owner`. Examples: (1)`r"(?P<owner>(.*)): (\w+) (\w+)"` will extract `jdoe` as the owner from `"jdoe: John Doe"` (2) `r"@(?P<owner>(.*))"` will extract `alice` as the owner from `"@alice"`.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: DBT Stateful Ingestion Config.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase. Default True to match historical dbt behavior. Set to False for case-sensitive platforms like BigQuery if you need to preserve original identifier casing in URNs.

fields.name: target_platform_instance
fields.label: Target Platform Instance
fields.description: The platform instance for the platform that dbt is operating on. Use this if you have multiple instances of the same platform (e.g. redshift) and need to distinguish between them.

fields.name: use_identifiers
fields.label: Use Identifiers
fields.description: Use model identifier instead of model name if defined (if not, default to model name).

fields.name: entities_enabled
fields.label: Entities Enabled
fields.description: Controls for enabling / disabling metadata emission for different dbt entities (models, test definitions, test results, etc.)

group_fields.name: models
group_fields.label: Models
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt models when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: sources
group_fields.label: Sources
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt sources when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: seeds
group_fields.label: Seeds
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt seeds when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: snapshots
group_fields.label: Snapshots
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt snapshots when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: test_definitions
group_fields.label: Test Definitions
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for test definitions when enabled when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: test_results
group_fields.label: Test Results
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for test results when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: model_performance
group_fields.label: Model Performance
group_fields.description: Emit model performance metadata when set to Yes or Only. Only supported with dbt core.

group_fields.name: exposures
group_fields.label: Exposures
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt exposures when set to Yes or Only. Exposures represent downstream consumers like dashboards, notebooks, or applications.

group_fields.name: semantic_models
group_fields.label: Semantic Models
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt semantic models when set to Yes or Only. Semantic models define entities, dimensions, and measures for the dbt semantic layer (dbt 1.6+).

group_fields.name: queries
group_fields.label: Queries
group_fields.description: Emit Query entities from meta.queries field when set to Yes or Only.

group_fields.name: catalog_stats
group_fields.label: Catalog Stats
group_fields.description: Emit DatasetProfile aspects with row counts and size from catalog.json stats when set to Yes. Requires catalog.json to be generated by `dbt docs generate`.

fields.name: meta_mapping
fields.label: Meta Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against dbt meta properties. Refer to the section below on dbt meta automated mappings.

fields.name: column_meta_mapping
fields.label: Column Meta Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against dbt column meta properties. Refer to the section below on dbt meta automated mappings.

fields.name: enable_meta_mapping
fields.label: Enable Meta Mapping
fields.description: When enabled, applies the mappings that are defined through the meta_mapping directives.

fields.name: write_semantics
fields.label: Write Semantics
fields.description: Whether the new tags, terms and owners to be added will override the existing ones added only by this source or not. Value for this config can be "PATCH" or "OVERRIDE"

fields.name: strip_user_ids_from_email
fields.label: Strip User Ids From Email
fields.description: Whether or not to strip email id while adding owners using dbt meta actions.

fields.name: convert_column_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Column Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: When enabled, converts column URNs to lowercase to ensure cross-platform compatibility. If `target_platform` is Snowflake, the default is True.

fields.name: test_warnings_are_errors
fields.label: Test Warnings Are Errors
fields.description: When enabled, dbt test warnings will be treated as failures (emitted as ``AssertionResult.type = FAILURE`` with ``severity = LOW``). The default will change to ``true`` in a future release once assertion result consumers can filter by severity; set ``true`` today to adopt the forthcoming behavior.

fields.name: infer_dbt_schemas
fields.label: Infer Dbt Schemas
fields.description: When enabled, schemas will be inferred from the dbt node definition.

fields.name: dbt_is_primary_sibling
fields.label: Dbt Is Primary Sibling
fields.description: Experimental: Controls sibling relationship primary designation between dbt entities and target platform entities. When True (default), dbt entities are primary and target platform entities are secondary. When False, target platform entities are primary and dbt entities are secondary. Uses aspect patches for precise control. Requires DataHub server 1.3.0+.

fields.name: drop_duplicate_sources
fields.label: Drop Duplicate Sources
fields.description: When enabled, drops sources that have the same name in the target platform as a model. This ensures that lineage is generated reliably, but will lose any documentation associated only with the source.

fields.name: catalog_path
fields.label: Catalog Path
fields.description: Path to dbt catalog JSON. See https://docs.getdbt.com/reference/artifacts/catalog-json. This file is optional, but highly recommended. Without it, some metadata like column info will be incomplete or missing. This can be a local file or a URI.

fields.name: sources_path
fields.label: Sources Path
fields.description: Path to dbt sources JSON. See https://docs.getdbt.com/reference/artifacts/sources-json. If not specified, last-modified fields will not be populated. This can be a local file or a URI.

fields.name: run_results_paths
fields.label: Run Results Paths
fields.description: Path to output of dbt test run as run_results files in JSON format. If not specified, test execution results and model performance metadata will not be populated in DataHub. If invoking dbt multiple times, you can provide paths to multiple run result files. Glob patterns are supported for S3, GCS, and local paths (e.g. 's3://bucket/results/*/run_results.json', 'gs://bucket/results/*/run_results.json', or '/path/to/results/*/run_results.json'). See https://docs.getdbt.com/reference/artifacts/run-results-json.

fields.name: only_include_if_in_catalog
fields.label: Only Include If In Catalog
fields.description: [experimental] If true, only include nodes that are also present in the catalog file. This is useful if you only want to include models that have been built by the associated run.

fields.name: gcs_connection
fields.label: Gcs Connection
fields.description: When fetching manifest files from gs://, GCS connection using HMAC credentials. See https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/authentication/hmackeys

group_fields.name: credential
group_fields.label: Credential
group_fields.description: GCS HMAC credentials. See https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/authentication/hmackeys

group_fields.name: hmac_access_id
group_fields.label: Hmac Access Id
group_fields.description: Access ID

group_fields.name: hmac_access_secret
group_fields.label: Hmac Access Secret
group_fields.description: Secret

group_fields.name: endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: GCS S3-compatible endpoint URL. Useful for testing with local S3-compatible servers.

fields.name: git_info
fields.label: Git Info
fields.description: Reference to your git location to enable easy navigation from DataHub to your dbt files.

group_fields.name: repo
group_fields.label: Repo
group_fields.description: Name of your Git repo e.g. https://github.com/datahub-project/datahub or https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab. If organization/repo is provided, we assume it is a GitHub repo.

group_fields.name: branch
group_fields.label: Branch
group_fields.description: Branch on which your files live by default. Typically main or master. This can also be a commit hash.

group_fields.name: url_subdir
group_fields.label: Url Subdir
group_fields.description: Prefix to prepend when generating URLs for files - useful when files are in a subdirectory. Only affects URL generation, not git operations.

group_fields.name: url_template
group_fields.label: Url Template
group_fields.description: Template for generating a URL to a file in the repo e.g. '{repo_url}/blob/{branch}/{file_path}'. We can infer this for GitHub and GitLab repos, and it is otherwise required.It supports the following variables: {repo_url}, {branch}, {file_path}

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: Environment to use in namespace when constructing URNs.

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: account_id
fields.label: Account Id
fields.description: The DBT Cloud account ID to use.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project Id
fields.description: The dbt Cloud project ID to use.

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.description: The API token to use to authenticate with DBT Cloud.

fields.name: target_platform
fields.label: Target Platform
fields.description: The platform that dbt is loading onto. (e.g. bigquery / redshift / postgres etc.)

fields.name: node_name_pattern
fields.label: Node Name Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for dbt model names to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: materialized_node_pattern
fields.label: Materialized Node Pattern
fields.description: Advanced filtering for materialized nodes based on their physical database location. Provides fine-grained control over database.schema.table patterns for catalog consistency.

group_fields.name: database_pattern
group_fields.label: Database Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for database names to filter materialized nodes.

group_fields.name: schema_pattern
group_fields.label: Schema Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for schema names in format '{database}.{schema}' to filter materialized nodes.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for table/view names in format '{database}.{schema}.{table}' to filter materialized nodes.

fields.name: include_env_in_assertion_guid
fields.label: Include Env In Assertion Guid
fields.description: Prior to version 0.9.4.2, the assertion GUIDs did not include the environment. If you're using multiple dbt ingestion that are only distinguished by env, then you should set this flag to True.

fields.name: include_compiled_code
fields.label: Include Compiled Code
fields.description: When enabled, includes the compiled code in the emitted metadata.

fields.name: include_database_name
fields.label: Include Database Name
fields.description: Whether to add database name to the table urn. Set to False to skip it for engines like AWS Athena where it's not required.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits incremental/patch lineage for non-dbt entities. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run. This would also require enabling 'incremental_lineage' in the counterpart warehouse ingestion (_e.g._ BigQuery, Redshift, etc).

fields.name: prefer_sql_parser_lineage
fields.label: Prefer Sql Parser Lineage
fields.description: Normally we use dbt's metadata to generate table lineage. When enabled, we prefer results from the SQL parser when generating lineage instead. This can be useful when dbt models reference tables directly, instead of using the ref() macro. This requires that `skip_sources_in_lineage` is enabled.

fields.name: skip_sources_in_lineage
fields.label: Skip Sources In Lineage
fields.description: [Experimental] When enabled, dbt sources will not be included in the lineage graph. Requires that `entities_enabled.sources` is set to `NO`. This is mainly useful when you have multiple, interdependent dbt projects.

fields.name: skip_missing_upstreams_in_lineage
fields.label: Skip Missing Upstreams In Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, upstream datasets that do not already exist in DataHub are excluded from lineage, preventing dangling graph edges from appearing in the lineage UI. Typically used together with `skip_sources_in_lineage` and `entities_enabled.sources: NO`. Important caveats: (1) if dbt is ingested before its upstream source systems, those lineage edges will be silently omitted until dbt is re-ingested after the upstreams are present; (2) adds one graph.exists() round-trip per unique upstream URN per run (cached within the run); (3) soft-deleted upstream entities are treated as present. Requires a DataHub graph connection.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, column-level lineage will be extracted from the dbt node definition. Requires `infer_dbt_schemas` to be enabled. If you run into issues where the column name casing does not match up with properly, providing a datahub_api or using the rest sink will improve accuracy.

fields.name: max_queries_per_model
fields.label: Max Queries Per Model
fields.description: Maximum number of Query entities to emit per dbt model. Prevents metadata explosion from malformed manifests. Set to 0 for unlimited.

fields.name: tag_prefix
fields.label: Tag Prefix
fields.description: Prefix added to tags during ingestion.

fields.name: query_tag_mapping
fields.label: Query Tag Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against dbt query_tag meta properties. Refer to the section below on dbt meta automated mappings.

fields.name: enable_query_tag_mapping
fields.label: Enable Query Tag Mapping
fields.description: When enabled, applies the mappings that are defined through the `query_tag_mapping` directives.

fields.name: enable_owner_extraction
fields.label: Enable Owner Extraction
fields.description: When enabled, ownership info will be extracted from the dbt meta

fields.name: owner_extraction_pattern
fields.label: Owner Extraction Pattern
