fields.description: Regex string to extract owner from the dbt node using the `(?P<name>...) syntax` of the [match object](https://docs.python.org/3/library/re.html#match-objects), where the group name must be `owner`. Examples: (1)`r"(?P<owner>(.*)): (\w+) (\w+)"` will extract `jdoe` as the owner from `"jdoe: John Doe"` (2) `r"@(?P<owner>(.*))"` will extract `alice` as the owner from `"@alice"`.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: DBT Stateful Ingestion Config.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase. Default True to match historical dbt behavior. Set to False for case-sensitive platforms like BigQuery if you need to preserve original identifier casing in URNs.

fields.name: target_platform_instance
fields.label: Target Platform Instance
fields.description: The platform instance for the platform that dbt is operating on. Use this if you have multiple instances of the same platform (e.g. redshift) and need to distinguish between them.

fields.name: use_identifiers
fields.label: Use Identifiers
fields.description: Use model identifier instead of model name if defined (if not, default to model name).

fields.name: entities_enabled
fields.label: Entities Enabled
fields.description: Controls for enabling / disabling metadata emission for different dbt entities (models, test definitions, test results, etc.)

group_fields.name: models
group_fields.label: Models
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt models when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: sources
group_fields.label: Sources
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt sources when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: seeds
group_fields.label: Seeds
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt seeds when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: snapshots
group_fields.label: Snapshots
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt snapshots when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: test_definitions
group_fields.label: Test Definitions
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for test definitions when enabled when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: test_results
group_fields.label: Test Results
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for test results when set to Yes or Only

group_fields.name: model_performance
group_fields.label: Model Performance
group_fields.description: Emit model performance metadata when set to Yes or Only. Only supported with dbt core.

group_fields.name: exposures
group_fields.label: Exposures
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt exposures when set to Yes or Only. Exposures represent downstream consumers like dashboards, notebooks, or applications.

group_fields.name: semantic_models
group_fields.label: Semantic Models
group_fields.description: Emit metadata for dbt semantic models when set to Yes or Only. Semantic models define entities, dimensions, and measures for the dbt semantic layer (dbt 1.6+).

group_fields.name: queries
group_fields.label: Queries
group_fields.description: Emit Query entities from meta.queries field when set to Yes or Only.

group_fields.name: catalog_stats
group_fields.label: Catalog Stats
group_fields.description: Emit DatasetProfile aspects with row counts and size from catalog.json stats when set to Yes. Requires catalog.json to be generated by `dbt docs generate`.

fields.name: meta_mapping
fields.label: Meta Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against dbt meta properties. Refer to the section below on dbt meta automated mappings.

fields.name: column_meta_mapping
fields.label: Column Meta Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against dbt column meta properties. Refer to the section below on dbt meta automated mappings.

fields.name: enable_meta_mapping
fields.label: Enable Meta Mapping
fields.description: When enabled, applies the mappings that are defined through the meta_mapping directives.

fields.name: write_semantics
fields.label: Write Semantics
fields.description: Whether the new tags, terms and owners to be added will override the existing ones added only by this source or not. Value for this config can be "PATCH" or "OVERRIDE"

fields.name: strip_user_ids_from_email
fields.label: Strip User Ids From Email
fields.description: Whether or not to strip email id while adding owners using dbt meta actions.

fields.name: convert_column_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Column Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: When enabled, converts column URNs to lowercase to ensure cross-platform compatibility. If `target_platform` is Snowflake, the default is True.

fields.name: test_warnings_are_errors
fields.label: Test Warnings Are Errors
fields.description: When enabled, dbt test warnings will be treated as failures (emitted as ``AssertionResult.type = FAILURE`` with ``severity = LOW``). The default will change to ``true`` in a future release once assertion result consumers can filter by severity; set ``true`` today to adopt the forthcoming behavior.

fields.name: infer_dbt_schemas
fields.label: Infer Dbt Schemas
fields.description: When enabled, schemas will be inferred from the dbt node definition.

fields.name: dbt_is_primary_sibling
fields.label: Dbt Is Primary Sibling
fields.description: Experimental: Controls sibling relationship primary designation between dbt entities and target platform entities. When True (default), dbt entities are primary and target platform entities are secondary. When False, target platform entities are primary and dbt entities are secondary. Uses aspect patches for precise control. Requires DataHub server 1.3.0+.

fields.name: drop_duplicate_sources
fields.label: Drop Duplicate Sources
fields.description: When enabled, drops sources that have the same name in the target platform as a model. This ensures that lineage is generated reliably, but will lose any documentation associated only with the source.

fields.name: access_url
fields.label: Access Url
fields.description: The base URL of the dbt Cloud instance to use. This should be the URL you use to access the dbt Cloud UI. It should include the scheme (http/https) and not include a trailing slash. See the access url for your dbt Cloud region here: https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/cloud/about-cloud/regions-ip-addresses

fields.name: metadata_endpoint
fields.label: Metadata Endpoint
fields.description: The dbt Cloud metadata API endpoint. If not provided, we will try to infer it from the access_url.

fields.name: job_id
fields.label: Job Id
fields.description: The ID of the job to ingest metadata from. Required in explicit mode (when auto_discovery is disabled).

fields.name: run_id
fields.label: Run Id
fields.description: The ID of the run to ingest metadata from. If not specified, defaults to the latest run. In auto-discovery mode, always uses the latest run for each job.

fields.name: auto_discovery
fields.label: Auto Discovery
fields.description: Auto-discovery configuration. When enabled, automatically discovers jobs for the specified project.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable/disable auto-discovery mode. When enabled, discovers production jobs for the specified project.

group_fields.name: require_generate_docs
group_fields.label: Require Generate Docs
group_fields.description: If True, only ingest jobs that have 'Generate docs on run' enabled in dbt Cloud. If False (default), ingest all production jobs regardless of the generate_docs setting.

group_fields.name: job_id_pattern
group_fields.label: Job Id Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter jobs by job_id when auto-discovering.

fields.name: external_url_mode
fields.label: External Url Mode
fields.description: Where should the "View in dbt" link point to - either the "Explore" UI or the dbt Cloud IDE

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: Environment to use in namespace when constructing URNs.

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: base_path
fields.label: Base Path
fields.description: Path to table (s3 or local file system). If path is not a delta table path then all subfolders will be scanned to detect and ingest delta tables.

fields.name: azure
fields.label: Azure
fields.description: Azure configuration for `abfss://`, `abfs://`, `az://`, `adl://`, and Azure HTTPS paths.

group_fields.name: account_name
group_fields.label: Account Name
group_fields.description: Azure storage account name. Required for `az://` and `adl://` style paths.

group_fields.name: account_key
group_fields.label: Account Key
group_fields.description: Azure storage account key.

group_fields.name: sas_token
group_fields.label: Sas Token
group_fields.description: Azure shared access signature (SAS) token.

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Azure service principal client id.

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Azure service principal client secret.

group_fields.name: tenant_id
group_fields.label: Tenant Id
group_fields.description: Azure service principal tenant id.

group_fields.name: credential
group_fields.label: Credential
group_fields.description: Unified Azure credential configuration. Mutually exclusive with `account_key`, `sas_token`, and service principal fields.

group_fields.name: authentication_method
group_fields.label: Authentication Method
group_fields.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'default' (auto-detects from environment), 'service_principal' (client ID + secret + tenant), 'managed_identity' (Azure Managed Identity), 'cli' (Azure CLI credential). Recommended: Use 'default' which tries multiple methods automatically.

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Azure Application (client) ID. Required for service_principal authentication. Find this in Azure Portal > App registrations > Your app > Overview.

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Azure client secret. Required for service_principal authentication. Create in Azure Portal > App registrations > Your app > Certificates & secrets.

group_fields.name: tenant_id
group_fields.label: Tenant Id
group_fields.description: Azure tenant (directory) ID. Required for service_principal authentication. Find this in Azure Portal > Microsoft Entra ID > Overview.

group_fields.name: managed_identity_client_id
group_fields.label: Managed Identity Client Id
group_fields.description: Client ID for user-assigned managed identity. Leave empty to use system-assigned managed identity. Only used when authentication_method is 'managed_identity'.

group_fields.name: exclude_cli_credential
group_fields.label: Exclude Cli Credential
group_fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude Azure CLI credential. Useful in production to avoid accidentally using developer credentials.

group_fields.name: exclude_environment_credential
group_fields.label: Exclude Environment Credential
group_fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude environment variables. Environment variables checked: AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_TENANT_ID.

group_fields.name: exclude_managed_identity_credential
group_fields.label: Exclude Managed Identity Credential
group_fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude managed identity. Useful during local development when managed identity is not available.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config with stale metadata removal

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: relative_path
fields.label: Relative Path
fields.description: If set, delta-tables will be searched at location '<base_path>/<relative_path>' and URNs will be created using relative_path only.

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: The platform that this source connects to

fields.name: version_history_lookback
fields.label: Version History Lookback
fields.description: Number of previous version histories to be ingested. Defaults to 1. If set to -1 all version history will be ingested.

fields.name: require_files
fields.label: Require Files
fields.description: Whether DeltaTable should track files. Consider setting this to `False` for large delta tables, resulting in significant memory reduction for ingestion process.When set to `False`, number_of_files in delta table can not be reported.

group_fields.name: aws_config
group_fields.label: Aws Config
group_fields.description: AWS configuration

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

group_fields.name: use_s3_bucket_tags
group_fields.label: Use S3 Bucket Tags
group_fields.description: Whether or not to create tags in datahub from the s3 bucket

group_fields.name: use_s3_object_tags
group_fields.label: Use S3 Object Tags
group_fields.description: # Whether or not to create tags in datahub from the s3 object

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to

fields.name: pack_name
fields.label: Pack Name
fields.description: Name of a data pack from the registry (e.g. 'bootstrap', 'showcase-ecommerce').

fields.name: pack_url
fields.label: Pack Url
fields.description: HTTP(S) URL to an MCP/MCE JSON file. Use instead of pack_name for custom packs.

fields.name: no_time_shift
fields.label: No Time Shift
fields.description: If true, load with original timestamps (no time-shifting).

fields.name: as_of
fields.label: As Of
fields.description: ISO 8601 datetime to use as the time-shift target (default: current time).

fields.name: trust_community
fields.label: Trust Community
fields.description: Allow loading community-contributed packs without warning.

fields.name: trust_custom
fields.label: Trust Custom
fields.description: Allow loading from unverified URLs without warning.

fields.name: no_cache
fields.label: No Cache
fields.description: Force re-download even if the pack is cached.

fields.name: pipeline_pattern
fields.label: Pipeline Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter pipelines by name. Matched against pipeline_name (the value passed to dlt.pipeline()). Example: allow ['^prod_.*'] to only ingest production pipelines.

fields.name: include_run_history
fields.label: Include Run History
fields.description: Whether to query the destination's _dlt_loads table and emit DataProcessInstance run history. Requires the destination (e.g. Postgres, BigQuery) to be accessible and dlt credentials to be configured in ~/.dlt/secrets.toml. Disabled by default to avoid requiring destination access.

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Whether to emit outlet lineage from dlt DataJobs to destination Dataset URNs. Constructs URNs using destination_platform_map. For lineage to stitch in DataHub, destination_platform_map env/instance must match the destination connector's configuration.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration. When enabled, automatically removes DataFlow/DataJob entities from DataHub if the corresponding dlt pipeline is deleted or no longer found in pipelines_dir.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: pipelines_dir
fields.label: Pipelines Dir
fields.description: Path to the dlt pipelines directory. dlt stores all pipeline state, schemas, and load packages here. Defaults to ~/.dlt/pipelines/ which is dlt's standard location. Override when pipelines are stored in a non-default location (e.g. /data/dlt-pipelines in a Docker environment).

fields.name: run_history_config
fields.label: Run History Config
fields.description: Time window for run history extraction. Uses standard DataHub BaseTimeWindowConfig — supports relative times like '-7 days' or absolute ISO timestamps.

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: destination_platform_map
fields.label: Destination Platform Map
fields.description: Maps dlt destination type names to DataHub platform configuration. Used to construct correct Dataset URNs for lineage. The destination type is the value passed to dlt.pipeline(destination='...'). Example: postgres: platform_instance: null env: DEV bigquery: platform_instance: my-gcp-project env: PROD

fields.name: source_dataset_urns
fields.label: Source Dataset Urns
fields.description: Optional: manually specify inlet (upstream) Dataset URNs per pipeline. All listed URNs are applied as inlets to every DataJob in the pipeline. Use for REST API sources where every task shares the same upstream source. For SQL sources where each task reads from exactly one table, use source_table_dataset_urns instead. Key is the pipeline_name; value is a list of Dataset URN strings. Example: crm_sync: - 'urn:li:dataset:(urn:li:dataPlatform:postgres,prod.crm.customers,PROD)'

fields.name: source_table_dataset_urns
fields.label: Source Table Dataset Urns
fields.description: Optional: manually specify inlet Dataset URNs per pipeline per table. Use for sql_database sources where each DataJob reads from exactly one source table and 1:1 lineage is desired. Outer key is pipeline_name; inner key is table_name; value is a list of URNs. Example: my_pipeline: my_table: - 'urn:li:dataset:(urn:li:dataPlatform:postgres,prod_db.public.my_table,PROD)' other_table: - 'urn:li:dataset:(urn:li:dataPlatform:postgres,prod_db.public.other_table,PROD)'

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: Doris FE (Frontend) host and port. Default port is 9030.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: auth_mode
fields.label: Auth Mode
fields.description: Authentication mode to use for the MySQL connection. Options are 'PASSWORD' (default) for standard username/password authentication, or 'AWS_IAM' for AWS RDS IAM authentication.

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: AWS configuration for RDS IAM authentication (only used when auth_mode is AWS_IAM). Provides full control over AWS credentials, region, profiles, role assumption, retry logic, and proxy settings. If not explicitly configured, boto3 will automatically use the default credential chain and region from environment variables (AWS_DEFAULT_REGION, AWS_REGION), AWS config files (~/.aws/config), or IAM role metadata.

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistics and query-based lineage from query history. The source of that history is controlled by `usage_source`.

fields.name: usage_source
fields.label: Usage Source
fields.description: Where to read query history from. `performance_schema` (default) reads normalized digests from `events_statements_summary_by_digest` (no setup, no per-user attribution). Its `COUNT_STAR` is cumulative since the last counter reset (server restart or table truncation), so the first ingestion after enabling usage can report a large one-day spike attributing all history-to-date to a single timestamp. `general_log` reads literal statements with user and timestamp from `mysql.general_log` (requires `general_log=ON` and `log_output=TABLE`).

fields.name: usage
fields.label: Usage
fields.description: Usage statistics config. Only used when `include_usage_statistics` is enabled.

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

group_fields.name: top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

group_fields.name: user_email_pattern
group_fields.label: User Email Pattern
group_fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

group_fields.name: include_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

group_fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

group_fields.name: format_sql_queries
group_fields.label: Format Sql Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

group_fields.name: include_top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Include Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Configuration for profiling Doris tables. Note: Doris types (HLL, BITMAP, QUANTILE_STATE, ARRAY, JSONB) are automatically excluded from field-level profiling as they don't support COUNT DISTINCT.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: email_domain
fields.label: Email Domain
fields.description: Email domain of your organisation, appended to `general_log` usernames (e.g. LDAP logins) so users display correctly. Ignored if the username already looks like an email. Only used with `usage_source: general_log`.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Dremio username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Dremio password or Personal Access Token

fields.name: hostname
fields.label: Hostname
fields.description: Hostname or IP Address of the Dremio server

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Port of the Dremio REST API

fields.name: authentication_method
fields.label: Authentication Method
fields.description: Authentication method: 'password' or 'PAT' (Personal Access Token)

fields.name: tls
fields.label: Tls
fields.description: Whether the Dremio REST API port is encrypted

fields.name: disable_certificate_verification
fields.label: Disable Certificate Verification
fields.description: Disable TLS certificate verification

fields.name: path_to_certificates
fields.label: Path To Certificates
fields.description: Path to SSL certificates

fields.name: is_dremio_cloud
fields.label: Is Dremio Cloud
fields.description: Whether this is a Dremio Cloud instance

fields.name: dremio_cloud_region
fields.label: Dremio Cloud Region
fields.description: Dremio Cloud region ('US' or 'EU')

fields.name: dremio_cloud_project_id
fields.label: Dremio Cloud Project Id
fields.description: ID of Dremio Cloud Project. Found in Project Settings in the Dremio Cloud UI

fields.name: include_system_tables
fields.label: Include System Tables
fields.description: Whether to include system tables and schemas (INFORMATION_SCHEMA, SYS) in ingestion.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter

fields.name: dataset_pattern
fields.label: Dataset Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables and views to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in dremio.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'dremio.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_query_lineage
fields.label: Include Query Lineage
fields.description: Whether to include query-based lineage information.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, lineage aspects are emitted as PATCH operations rather than full overwrites. This preserves any lineage edges that were manually added in DataHub between runs. Disable if you want each run to fully replace lineage. PATCH emission requires a GMS that supports patch aspects.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to profile

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Configuration for profiling

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

group_fields.name: query_timeout
group_fields.label: Query Timeout
group_fields.description: Time before cancelling Dremio profiling query

fields.name: ingest_owner
fields.label: Ingest Owner
fields.description: Ingest Owner from source. This will override Owner info entered from UI

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion config with stale-entity removal support.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: enable_stateful_time_window
fields.label: Enable Stateful Time Window
fields.description: Enable stateful time window tracking for query lineage/usage extraction. When enabled, subsequent runs will skip time windows already fully processed, avoiding redundant API calls. Requires stateful_ingestion to be configured.

fields.name: incremental_properties
fields.label: Incremental Properties
fields.description: When enabled, emits dataset properties as incremental to existing dataset properties in DataHub. When disabled, re-states dataset properties on each run.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Domain for all source objects.

fields.name: source_mappings
fields.label: Source Mappings
fields.description: Mappings from Dremio sources to DataHub platforms and datasets.

fields.name: source_type_mappings
fields.label: Source Type Mappings
fields.description: Register additional Dremio source-type strings on top of the built-in mapping, for Dremio ARP connectors or source types not yet known to DataHub. Keys are the Dremio `type` field (case-insensitive); values declare the DataHub platform and optional category. Example: `{"MYORG_KAFKA": {"platform": "kafka", "category": "database"}}`.

fields.name: max_workers
fields.label: Max Workers
fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for parallel processing

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: host URL

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: aws_access_key_id
fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_session_token
fields.label: Aws Session Token
fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_role
fields.label: Aws Role
fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

fields.name: aws_profile
fields.label: Aws Profile
fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: Aws Region
fields.description: AWS region code.

fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

fields.name: aws_proxy
fields.label: Aws Proxy
fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

fields.name: aws_retry_num
fields.label: Aws Retry Num
fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

fields.name: aws_retry_mode
fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

fields.name: read_timeout
