fields.label: Read Timeout
fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

fields.name: aws_advanced_config
fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. The table name format is 'region.table'

fields.name: include_table_item
fields.label: Include Table Item
fields.description: [Advanced] The primary keys of items of a table in dynamodb format the user would like to include in schema. Refer "Advanced Configurations" section for more details

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size

group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: extract_table_tags
fields.label: Extract Table Tags
fields.description: When enabled, tags associated with DynamoDB tables in AWS will be emitted as DataHub tags. Tags are applied using OVERWRITE mode, meaning any existing tags including tags manually added or modified from the UI will be replaced. Use with caution.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion

group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: max_schema_size

fields.description: Maximum number of fields to include in the schema.

fields.name: schema_sampling_size

fields.description: Number of items to sample from each table for schema inference. This controls how many items are scanned to determine the table's schema structure.

fields.name: username

fields.description: The username credential.

fields.name: password

fields.description: The password credential.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Api Key
fields.description: API Key authentication. Accepts either a list with id and api_key (UTF-8 representation), or a base64 encoded string of id and api_key combined by ':'.

fields.name: index_pattern
fields.label: Index Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for indexes to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: ingest_index_templates
fields.label: Ingest Index Templates
fields.description: Ingests ES index templates if enabled.

fields.name: index_template_pattern
fields.label: Index Template Pattern
fields.description: The regex patterns for filtering index templates to ingest.

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Configs to ingest data profiles from ElasticSearch.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to enable profiling for the elastic search source.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

fields.name: use_ssl
fields.label: Use Ssl
fields.description: Whether to use SSL for the connection or not.

fields.name: verify_certs
fields.label: Verify Certs
fields.description: Whether to verify SSL certificates.

fields.name: ca_certs
fields.label: Ca Certs
fields.description: Path to a certificate authority (CA) certificate.

fields.name: client_cert
fields.label: Client Cert
fields.description: Path to the file containing the private key and the certificate, or cert only if using client_key.

fields.name: client_key
fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: Path to the file containing the private key if using separate cert and key files.

fields.name: ssl_assert_hostname
fields.label: Ssl Assert Hostname
fields.description: Use hostname verification if not False.

fields.name: ssl_assert_fingerprint
fields.label: Ssl Assert Fingerprint
fields.description: Verify the supplied certificate fingerprint if not None.

fields.name: url_prefix
fields.label: Url Prefix
fields.description: There are cases where an enterprise would have multiple elastic search clusters. One way for them to manage is to have a single endpoint for all the elastic search clusters and use url_prefix for routing requests to different clusters.

fields.name: collapse_urns
fields.label: Collapse Urns
fields.description: List of regex patterns to remove from the name of the URN. All of the indices before removal of URNs are considered as the same dataset. These are applied in order for each URN. The main case where you would want to have multiple of these if the name where you are trying to remove suffix from have different formats. e.g. ending with -YYYY-MM-DD as well as ending -epochtime would require you to have 2 regex patterns to remove the suffixes across all URNs.

group_fields.name: urns_suffix_regex
group_fields.label: Urns Suffix Regex
group_fields.description: List of regex patterns to remove from the name of the URN. All of the indices before removal of URNs are considered as the same dataset. These are applied in order for each URN. The main case where you would want to have multiple of these if the name where you are trying to remove suffix from have different formats. e.g. ending with -YYYY-MM-DD as well as ending -epochtime would require you to have 2 regex patterns to remove the suffixes across all URNs.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: path_list
fields.label: Path List
fields.description: List of paths to Excel files or folders to ingest.

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: AWS configuration

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: azure_config
fields.label: Azure Config
fields.description: Azure configuration

group_fields.name: base_path
group_fields.label: Base Path
group_fields.description: Base folder in hierarchical namespaces to start from.

group_fields.name: container_name
group_fields.label: Container Name
group_fields.description: Azure storage account container name.

group_fields.name: account_name
group_fields.label: Account Name
group_fields.description: Name of the Azure storage account. See [Microsoft official documentation on how to create a storage account.](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/create-data-lake-storage-account)

group_fields.name: account_key
group_fields.label: Account Key
group_fields.description: Azure storage account access key that can be used as a credential. **An account key, a SAS token or a client secret is required for authentication.**

group_fields.name: sas_token
group_fields.label: Sas Token
group_fields.description: Azure storage account Shared Access Signature (SAS) token that can be used as a credential. **An account key, a SAS token or a client secret is required for authentication.**

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Azure client secret that can be used as a credential. **An account key, a SAS token or a client secret is required for authentication.**

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Azure client (Application) ID required when a `client_secret` is used as a credential.

group_fields.name: tenant_id
group_fields.label: Tenant Id
group_fields.description: Azure tenant (Directory) ID required when a `client_secret` is used as a credential.

fields.name: path_pattern
fields.label: Path Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for file paths to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: worksheet_pattern
fields.label: Worksheet Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for worksheets to ingest. Worksheets are specified as 'filename_without_extension.worksheet_name'. For example to allow the worksheet Sheet1 from file report.xlsx, use the pattern: 'report.Sheet1'.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for worksheets to profile. Worksheets are specified as 'filename_without_extension.worksheet_name'. For example to allow the worksheet Sheet1 from file report.xlsx, use the pattern: 'report.Sheet1'.

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Configuration for profiling

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: use_s3_bucket_tags
fields.label: Use S3 Bucket Tags
fields.description: Whether or not to create tags in datahub from the s3 bucket

fields.name: use_s3_object_tags
fields.label: Use S3 Object Tags
fields.description: Whether or not to create tags in datahub from the s3 object

fields.name: use_abs_blob_tags
fields.label: Use Abs Blob Tags
fields.description: Whether to create tags in datahub from the abs blob tags

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration for stateful ingestion and stale metadata removal.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Either a boolean, in which case it controls whether we verify the server's TLS certificate, or a string, in which case it must be a path to a CA bundle to use.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Enable to convert the Excel asset urns to lowercase

fields.name: active_sheet_only
fields.label: Active Sheet Only
fields.description: Enable to only ingest the active sheet of the workbook. If not set, all sheets will be ingested.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: authentication_method
fields.label: Authentication Method
fields.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'default' (auto-detects from environment), 'service_principal' (client ID + secret + tenant), 'managed_identity' (Azure Managed Identity), 'cli' (Azure CLI credential). Recommended: Use 'default' which tries multiple methods automatically.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Azure Application (client) ID. Required for service_principal authentication. Find this in Azure Portal > App registrations > Your app > Overview.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Azure client secret. Required for service_principal authentication. Create in Azure Portal > App registrations > Your app > Certificates & secrets.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant Id
fields.description: Azure tenant (directory) ID. Required for service_principal authentication. Find this in Azure Portal > Microsoft Entra ID > Overview.

fields.name: managed_identity_client_id
fields.label: Managed Identity Client Id
fields.description: Client ID for user-assigned managed identity. Leave empty to use system-assigned managed identity. Only used when authentication_method is 'managed_identity'.

fields.name: exclude_cli_credential
fields.label: Exclude Cli Credential
fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude Azure CLI credential. Useful in production to avoid accidentally using developer credentials.

fields.name: exclude_environment_credential
fields.label: Exclude Environment Credential
fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude environment variables. Environment variables checked: AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_TENANT_ID.

fields.name: exclude_managed_identity_credential
fields.label: Exclude Managed Identity Credential
fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude managed identity. Useful during local development when managed identity is not available.

fields.name: workspace_pattern
fields.label: Workspace Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter workspaces by name. Example: allow=['prod-.*'], deny=['.*-test']

fields.name: pipeline_pattern
fields.label: Pipeline Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter data pipelines by name. Applied to all workspaces matching workspace_pattern.

fields.name: include_execution_history
fields.label: Include Execution History
fields.description: Extract pipeline and activity execution history as DataProcessInstance. Includes run status, duration, and parameters. Enables lineage extraction from parameterized activities using actual runtime values.

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Extract lineage from activity inputs/outputs. Maps Fabric connections to DataHub datasets based on connection type.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration for stateful ingestion and stale entity removal. When enabled, tracks ingested entities and removes those that no longer exist in Fabric.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: extract_pipelines
fields.label: Extract Pipelines
fields.description: Whether to extract Data Pipelines and their activities.

fields.name: execution_history_days
fields.label: Execution History Days
fields.description: Number of days of execution history to extract. Only used when include_execution_history is True. Higher values increase ingestion time. Note: Fabric API returns at most 100 recently completed runs per pipeline.

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: Map connection names to DataHub platform instances. Example: {'my-snowflake-connection': 'prod_snowflake'}. Used for accurate lineage resolution to existing datasets.

fields.name: api_timeout
fields.label: Api Timeout
fields.description: Timeout for REST API calls in seconds.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: authentication_method
fields.label: Authentication Method
fields.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'default' (auto-detects from environment), 'service_principal' (client ID + secret + tenant), 'managed_identity' (Azure Managed Identity), 'cli' (Azure CLI credential). Recommended: Use 'default' which tries multiple methods automatically.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Azure Application (client) ID. Required for service_principal authentication. Find this in Azure Portal > App registrations > Your app > Overview.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Azure client secret. Required for service_principal authentication. Create in Azure Portal > App registrations > Your app > Certificates & secrets.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant Id
fields.description: Azure tenant (directory) ID. Required for service_principal authentication. Find this in Azure Portal > Microsoft Entra ID > Overview.

fields.name: managed_identity_client_id
fields.label: Managed Identity Client Id
fields.description: Client ID for user-assigned managed identity. Leave empty to use system-assigned managed identity. Only used when authentication_method is 'managed_identity'.

fields.name: exclude_cli_credential
fields.label: Exclude Cli Credential
fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude Azure CLI credential. Useful in production to avoid accidentally using developer credentials.

fields.name: exclude_environment_credential
fields.label: Exclude Environment Credential
fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude environment variables. Environment variables checked: AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, AZURE_TENANT_ID.

fields.name: exclude_managed_identity_credential
fields.label: Exclude Managed Identity Credential
fields.description: When using 'default' authentication, exclude managed identity. Useful during local development when managed identity is not available.

fields.name: workspace_pattern
fields.label: Workspace Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter workspaces by name. Example: allow=['prod-.*'], deny=['.*-test']

fields.name: lakehouse_pattern
fields.label: Lakehouse Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter lakehouses by name. Applied to all workspaces matching workspace_pattern.

fields.name: warehouse_pattern
fields.label: Warehouse Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter warehouses by name. Applied to all workspaces matching workspace_pattern.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables by name. Format: 'schema.table' or just 'table' for default schema.

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter views by name. Format: 'schema.view_name' or just 'view_name' for default schema.

fields.name: usage
fields.label: Usage
fields.description: Usage tracking configuration. Reads `queryinsights.exec_requests_history` on each Lakehouse/Warehouse SQL Analytics Endpoint. Requires a configured and enabled `sql_endpoint`.

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

group_fields.name: top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

group_fields.name: user_email_pattern
group_fields.label: User Email Pattern
group_fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

group_fields.name: include_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

group_fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

group_fields.name: format_sql_queries
group_fields.label: Format Sql Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

group_fields.name: include_top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Include Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

group_fields.name: include_usage_statistics
group_fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
group_fields.description: Master toggle for Fabric usage extraction. When False, no `datasetUsageStatistics` or `operation` aspects are emitted from queryinsights, regardless of `include_operational_stats`.

group_fields.name: skip_failed_queries
group_fields.label: Skip Failed Queries
group_fields.description: When True, the SQL filter excludes rows where status != 'Succeeded' (canceled / failed queries are skipped at the source).

group_fields.name: include_queries
group_fields.label: Include Queries
group_fields.description: If enabled, emit a `Query` entity for each unique parsed queryinsights row so the SQL becomes a first-class, searchable asset in DataHub (visible on the Queries tab and as standalone Query pages). Requires `include_usage_statistics=True`.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration for stateful ingestion and stale entity removal. When enabled, tracks ingested entities and removes those that no longer exist in Fabric.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: extract_lakehouses
fields.label: Extract Lakehouses
fields.description: Whether to extract lakehouses and their tables.

fields.name: extract_warehouses
fields.label: Extract Warehouses
fields.description: Whether to extract warehouses and their tables.

fields.name: extract_views
fields.label: Extract Views
fields.description: Whether to extract views and their definitions. Requires a configured sql_endpoint, because views are discovered via INFORMATION_SCHEMA.VIEWS over the SQL Analytics Endpoint.

fields.name: extract_schemas
fields.label: Extract Schemas
fields.description: Whether to extract schema containers. If False, tables will be directly under lakehouse/warehouse containers.

fields.name: api_timeout
fields.label: Api Timeout
fields.description: Timeout for REST API calls in seconds.

fields.name: extract_schema
fields.label: Extract Schema
fields.description: Configuration for schema extraction from tables.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable schema extraction

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Schema extraction method. Currently only 'sql_analytics_endpoint' is supported.

fields.name: sql_endpoint
fields.label: Sql Endpoint
fields.description: SQL Analytics Endpoint configuration. Required when extract_views=True or when extract_schema.enabled=True with method='sql_analytics_endpoint'.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable SQL Analytics Endpoint connection

group_fields.name: odbc_driver
group_fields.label: Odbc Driver
group_fields.description: ODBC driver name for SQL Server connections.

group_fields.name: encrypt
group_fields.label: Encrypt
group_fields.description: Enable encryption for SQL Server connections. Valid values: 'yes'/'mandatory' (enable encryption, default in ODBC Driver 18.0+), 'no'/'optional' (disable encryption), or 'strict' (ODBC Driver 18.0+, TDS 8.0 protocol only, always verifies server certificate). See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/connect/odbc/dsn-connection-string-attribute?view=sql-server-ver17#encrypt

group_fields.name: trust_server_certificate
group_fields.label: Trust Server Certificate
group_fields.description: Trust server certificate without validation. Set to 'yes' only if certificate validation fails. When 'encrypt=strict', this setting is ignored and certificate validation is always performed. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/connect/odbc/dsn-connection-string-attribute?view=sql-server-ver17

group_fields.name: query_timeout
group_fields.label: Query Timeout
group_fields.description: Timeout for SQL queries in seconds

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: path
fields.label: Path
fields.description: Path to Feast repository

fields.name: owner_mappings
fields.label: Owner Mappings
fields.description: Mapping of owner names to owner types

fields.name: enable_owner_extraction
fields.label: Enable Owner Extraction
fields.description: If this is disabled, then we NEVER try to map owners. If this is enabled, then owner_mappings is REQUIRED to extract ownership.

fields.name: enable_tag_extraction
fields.label: Enable Tag Extraction
fields.description: If this is disabled, then we NEVER try to extract tags.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

fields.name: fs_yaml_file
fields.label: Fs Yaml File
fields.description: Path to the `feature_store.yaml` file used to configure the feature store

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Environment to use when constructing URNs

fields.name: path
fields.label: Path
fields.description: File path to folder or file to ingest, or URL to a remote file. If pointed to a folder, all files with extension {file_extension} (default json) within that folder will be processed.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: file_extension
fields.label: File Extension
fields.description: When providing a folder to use to read files, set this field to control file extensions that you want the source to process. * is a special value that means process every file regardless of extension

fields.name: aspect
fields.label: Aspect
fields.description: Set to an aspect to only read this aspect for ingestion.

fields.name: count_all_before_starting
fields.label: Count All Before Starting
fields.description: When enabled, counts total number of records in the file before starting. Used for accurate estimation of completion time. Turn it off if startup time is too high.

fields.name: api_config
fields.label: Api Config
fields.description: Fivetran REST API configuration, used to provide wider support for connections.

group_fields.name: api_key
group_fields.label: Api Key
group_fields.description: Fivetran API key

group_fields.name: api_secret
group_fields.label: Api Secret
group_fields.description: Fivetran API secret

group_fields.name: base_url
group_fields.label: Base Url
group_fields.description: Fivetran API base URL

group_fields.name: request_timeout_sec
group_fields.label: Request Timeout Sec
group_fields.description: Request timeout in seconds

fields.name: connector_patterns
fields.label: Connector Patterns
fields.description: Filtering regex patterns for connector names.

fields.name: destination_patterns
fields.label: Destination Patterns
fields.description: Regex patterns for destination ids to filter in ingestion. Fivetran destination IDs are usually two word identifiers e.g. canyon_tolerable, and are not the same as the destination database name. They're visible in the Fivetran UI under Destinations -> Overview -> Destination Group ID.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates table->table column lineage.

fields.name: max_table_lineage_per_connector
fields.label: Max Table Lineage Per Connector
fields.description: Maximum number of table lineage entries to retrieve per connector.

fields.name: max_column_lineage_per_connector
fields.label: Max Column Lineage Per Connector
fields.description: Maximum number of column lineage entries to retrieve per connector.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Fivetran Stateful Ingestion Config.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: fivetran_log_config
fields.label: Fivetran Log Config
fields.description: Fivetran Platform Connector log destination configuration. Required for `log_database` mode (the inferred default whenever this block is present). Optional in `rest_api` mode — when supplied alongside `api_config`, the REST reader uses the DB log only for per-run sync history (which the REST API doesn't expose).

group_fields.name: destination_platform
group_fields.label: Destination Platform
group_fields.description: The destination platform where fivetran connector log tables are dumped. For Managed Data Lake destinations use `log_source: rest_api` instead (no `fivetran_log_config` block needed).

group_fields.name: snowflake_destination_config
group_fields.label: Snowflake Destination Config
group_fields.description: If destination platform is 'snowflake', provide snowflake configuration.

group_fields.name: options
group_fields.label: Options
group_fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

group_fields.name: username
group_fields.label: Username
group_fields.description: Snowflake username.

group_fields.name: password
group_fields.label: Password
group_fields.description: Snowflake password.

group_fields.name: private_key
group_fields.label: Private Key
group_fields.description: Private key in a form of '<PEM block>\n' if using key pair authentication. Encrypted version of private key will be in a form of '<PEM block>\n' See: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth.html

group_fields.name: private_key_path
group_fields.label: Private Key Path
group_fields.description: The path to the private key if using key pair authentication. Ignored if `private_key` is set. See: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth.html

group_fields.name: private_key_password
group_fields.label: Private Key Password
group_fields.description: Password for your private key. Required if using key pair authentication with encrypted private key.

group_fields.name: oauth_config
group_fields.label: OAuth
group_fields.description: oauth configuration - https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/python-connector-example.html#connecting-with-oauth

group_fields.name: provider
group_fields.label: Provider
group_fields.description: Identity provider for oauth.Supported providers are microsoft and okta.

group_fields.name: authority_url
group_fields.label: Authority Url
group_fields.description: Authority url of your identity provider

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: client id of your registered application

group_fields.name: scopes
group_fields.label: Scopes
group_fields.description: scopes required to connect to snowflake

group_fields.name: use_certificate
group_fields.label: Use Certificate
group_fields.description: Do you want to use certificate and private key to authenticate using oauth

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: client secret of the application if use_certificate = false

group_fields.name: encoded_oauth_public_key
group_fields.label: Encoded Oauth Public Key
group_fields.description: base64 encoded certificate content if use_certificate = true

group_fields.name: encoded_oauth_private_key
group_fields.label: Encoded Oauth Private Key
group_fields.description: base64 encoded private key content if use_certificate = true

group_fields.name: authentication_type
group_fields.label: Authentication Type
group_fields.description: The type of authenticator to use when connecting to Snowflake. Supports "DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATOR", "OAUTH_AUTHENTICATOR", "EXTERNAL_BROWSER_AUTHENTICATOR" and "KEY_PAIR_AUTHENTICATOR".

group_fields.name: account_id
group_fields.label: Account Id
group_fields.description: Snowflake account identifier. e.g. xy12345, xy12345.us-east-2.aws, xy12345.us-central1.gcp, xy12345.central-us.azure, xy12345.us-west-2.privatelink. Refer [Account Identifiers](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-account-identifier.html#format-2-legacy-account-locator-in-a-region) for more details.

group_fields.name: warehouse
group_fields.label: Warehouse
group_fields.description: Snowflake warehouse.

group_fields.name: role
group_fields.label: Role
group_fields.description: Snowflake role.

group_fields.name: connect_args
group_fields.label: Connect Args
group_fields.description: Connect args to pass to Snowflake SqlAlchemy driver

group_fields.name: token
group_fields.label: Token
group_fields.description: OAuth token from external identity provider. Not recommended for most use cases because it will not be able to refresh once expired.

group_fields.name: snowflake_domain
group_fields.label: Snowflake Domain
group_fields.description: Snowflake domain. Use 'snowflakecomputing.com' for most regions or 'snowflakecomputing.cn' for China (cn-northwest-1) region.

group_fields.name: database
group_fields.label: Database
group_fields.description: The fivetran connector log database.

group_fields.name: log_schema
group_fields.label: Log Schema
group_fields.description: The fivetran connector log schema.

group_fields.name: preserve_case
group_fields.label: Preserve Case
group_fields.description: Pass `database` and `log_schema` identifiers verbatim when issuing `USE DATABASE` / `USE SCHEMA`, instead of Snowflake's default uppercasing of unquoted identifiers. Useful when the log lives in a Snowflake schema created with quoted lowercase names, or any other case-preserving setup where the uppercasing path would query identifiers that don't exist. **For Managed Data Lake destinations specifically: prefer `log_source: rest_api` over a Snowflake catalog-linked database (CLD) — REST mode reads the log directly via API and avoids the identifier-casing issue altogether.**

group_fields.name: bigquery_destination_config
group_fields.label: Bigquery Destination Config
group_fields.description: If destination platform is 'bigquery', provide bigquery configuration.

group_fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration
group_fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration
group_fields.description: Path to the GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration JSON file. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration_json and gcp_wif_configuration_json_string.

group_fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration_json
group_fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration Json
group_fields.description: GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration as a dict or a JSON string. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration and gcp_wif_configuration_json_string.

group_fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration_json_string
group_fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration Json String
group_fields.description: GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration as a JSON string (contents of the configuration file). Useful for injecting configuration from secrets managers. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration and gcp_wif_configuration_json. Note: WIF configuration typically contains public endpoint URLs rather than private keys, so SecretStr masking is not applied. If your WIF config contains sensitive material, ensure it is not logged at DEBUG level.

group_fields.name: auth_type
group_fields.label: Auth Type
group_fields.description: Authentication type to use. Defaults to 'service_account'. Set to 'workload_identity_federation' to authenticate via [Workload Identity Federation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/workload-identity-federation), which avoids long-lived service account keys.

group_fields.name: credential
group_fields.label: Credential
group_fields.description: BigQuery service account credential. Required when auth_type is 'service_account' unless Application Default Credentials are available (e.g. running on GCE/GKE).

group_fields.name: project_id
group_fields.label: Project Id
group_fields.description: Project id to set the credentials

group_fields.name: private_key_id
group_fields.label: Private Key Id
group_fields.description: Private key id

group_fields.name: private_key
group_fields.label: Private Key
group_fields.description: Private key in a form of '<PEM block>\n'

group_fields.name: client_email
group_fields.label: Client Email
group_fields.description: Client email

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Client Id

group_fields.name: auth_uri
group_fields.label: Auth Uri
group_fields.description: Authentication uri

group_fields.name: token_uri
group_fields.label: Token Uri
group_fields.description: Token uri

group_fields.name: auth_provider_x509_cert_url
group_fields.label: Auth Provider X509 Cert Url
group_fields.description: Auth provider x509 certificate url

group_fields.name: type
group_fields.label: Type
group_fields.description: Authentication type

group_fields.name: client_x509_cert_url
group_fields.label: Client X509 Cert Url
group_fields.description: If not set it will be default to https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/client_email

group_fields.name: extra_client_options
group_fields.label: Extra Client Options
group_fields.description: Additional keyword arguments passed to GCP client constructors (bigquery.Client, GCPLoggingClient, etc.).

group_fields.name: project_on_behalf
group_fields.label: Project On Behalf
group_fields.description: [Advanced] The BigQuery project in which queries are executed. Will be passed when creating a job. If not passed, falls back to the project associated with the service account.

group_fields.name: dataset
group_fields.label: Dataset
group_fields.description: The fivetran connector log dataset.

group_fields.name: databricks_destination_config
group_fields.label: Databricks Destination Config
group_fields.description: If destination platform is 'databricks', provide databricks configuration.

group_fields.name: token
group_fields.label: Token
group_fields.description: Databricks personal access token

group_fields.name: azure_auth
group_fields.label: Azure Auth
group_fields.description: Azure configuration

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Azure application client secret used for authentication. This is a confidential credential that should be kept secure.

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Azure application (client) ID. This is the unique identifier for the registered Azure AD application.

group_fields.name: tenant_id
group_fields.label: Tenant Id
group_fields.description: Azure tenant (directory) ID. This identifies the Azure AD tenant where the application is registered.

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Databricks service principal client ID

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Databricks service principal client secret

group_fields.name: workspace_url
group_fields.label: Workspace Url
group_fields.description: Databricks workspace url. e.g. https://my-workspace.cloud.databricks.com

group_fields.name: warehouse_id
group_fields.label: Warehouse Id
group_fields.description: SQL Warehouse id, for running queries. Must be explicitly provided to enable SQL-based features. Required for the following features that need SQL access: 1) Tag extraction (include_tags=True) - queries system.information_schema.tags 2) Hive Metastore catalog (include_hive_metastore=True) - queries legacy hive_metastore catalog 3) System table lineage (lineage_data_source=SYSTEM_TABLES) - queries system.access.table_lineage/column_lineage 4) Data profiling (profiling.enabled=True) - runs SELECT/ANALYZE queries on tables. When warehouse_id is missing, these features will be automatically disabled (with warnings) to allow ingestion to continue.

group_fields.name: extra_client_options
group_fields.label: Extra Client Options
group_fields.description: Additional options to pass to Databricks SQLAlchemy client.

group_fields.name: catalog
group_fields.label: Catalog
group_fields.description: The fivetran connector log catalog.

group_fields.name: log_schema
group_fields.label: Log Schema
group_fields.description: The fivetran connector log schema.

fields.name: log_source
fields.label: Log Source
fields.description: Where to read the Fivetran log from. Leave unset to let the connector infer this from which credential blocks you provide: - Only `fivetran_log_config` → `log_database`. - Only `api_config` → `rest_api`. - Both → `log_database` (DB-primary; REST still owns destination routing and Google Sheets details). Set this explicitly to override the default routing — e.g. `rest_api` with a `fivetran_log_config` block also present runs REST-primary with the DB log only providing per-run sync history.

fields.name: sources_to_platform_instance
fields.label: Sources To Platform Instance
fields.description: A mapping from connector id to its platform/instance/env/database details.

fields.name: destination_to_platform_instance
fields.label: Destination To Platform Instance
fields.description: A mapping of destination id to its platform/instance/env details.

fields.name: history_sync_lookback_period
fields.label: History Sync Lookback Period
fields.description: The number of days to look back when extracting connectors' sync history.

fields.name: max_jobs_per_connector
fields.label: Max Jobs Per Connector
fields.description: Maximum number of sync jobs to retrieve per connector.

fields.name: rest_api_max_workers
fields.label: Rest Api Max Workers
fields.description: Number of worker threads used to fetch per-connector data (schemas + sync history) in parallel when `log_source: rest_api`. Values >1 issue concurrent HTTP calls to the Fivetran REST API and meaningfully speed up ingestion for accounts with hundreds of connectors. Set to 1 for fully sequential behaviour. Lower this (not raise it) if you hit Fivetran rate limits. Ignored in `log_database` mode.

fields.name: rest_api_per_connector_timeout_sec
fields.label: Rest Api Per Connector Timeout Sec
fields.description: Hard wall-clock timeout (seconds) for fetching a single connector's schema + sync history when `log_source: rest_api`. If exceeded, that connector is emitted without lineage / run history and a warning is recorded — the rest of the ingest continues. Guards against a single hung HTTP call stalling the whole run. Healthy connectors finish in seconds; bump only if you have very large connectors that legitimately need more.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: rest_api_url
fields.label: Rest Api Url
fields.description: JobManager REST API endpoint (e.g., http://localhost:8081).

fields.name: sql_gateway_url
fields.label: Sql Gateway Url
fields.description: SQL Gateway REST API endpoint (e.g., http://localhost:8083). Enables platform resolution for SQL/Table API lineage. When provided, the connector resolves table references to their actual platform (kafka, postgres, iceberg, etc.) via catalog introspection.

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.description: Bearer token for authentication. Mutually exclusive with username/password.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Username for HTTP Basic authentication. Must be paired with 'password'.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for HTTP Basic authentication. Must be paired with 'username'.

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.label: Timeout Seconds
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds.

fields.name: max_retries
fields.label: Max Retries
fields.description: Maximum total attempts (initial + retries) for failed HTTP requests with exponential backoff. Default of 3 means 1 initial attempt plus up to 2 retries.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Verify SSL certificates for HTTPS connections.

fields.name: sql_gateway_operation_timeout_seconds
fields.label: Sql Gateway Operation Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for a SQL Gateway operation (SHOW CATALOGS, DESCRIBE CATALOG, etc.) to complete. Increase for slow catalog backends.

fields.name: job_name_pattern
fields.label: Job Name Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter Flink jobs by name.

fields.name: include_job_states
fields.label: Include Job States
fields.description: Flink job states to include in ingestion.

fields.name: include_run_history
fields.label: Include Run History
fields.description: Emit DataProcessInstance entities for job execution tracking.

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform_instance mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Extract source/sink lineage from Flink execution plans.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion for soft-deleting stale entities.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: catalog_platform_map
fields.label: Catalog Platform Map
fields.description: Platform overrides for Flink catalogs, keyed by catalog name. Values take priority over SQL Gateway auto-detection. Example: {'ice_catalog': {'platform': 'iceberg', 'platform_instance': 'prod-iceberg'}, 'pg_catalog': {'platform_instance': 'prod-postgres'}}. The 'platform' field overrides auto-detection. Required for Iceberg/Paimon catalogs on Flink < 1.20 (DESCRIBE CATALOG unavailable). On Flink 1.20+, platform is auto-detected via SQL Gateway unless overridden here. The 'platform_instance' field takes priority over the inherited platform_instance_map (platform -> platform_instance) for catalogs listed here.

fields.name: operator_granularity
fields.label: Operator Granularity
fields.description: DataJob granularity: 'job' emits one coalesced DataJob per flow, 'vertex' emits one DataJob per operator/vertex in the execution plan.

fields.name: max_workers
fields.label: Max Workers
fields.description: Max parallel threads for fetching job details from the Flink REST API.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: hmac_access_id
fields.label: Hmac Access Id
fields.description: Access ID

fields.name: hmac_access_secret
fields.label: Hmac Access Secret
fields.description: Secret

fields.name: path_specs
fields.label: Path Specs
fields.description: List of PathSpec. See [below](#path-spec) the details about PathSpec

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration
fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration
fields.description: Path to the GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration JSON file. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration_json and gcp_wif_configuration_json_string.

fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration_json
fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration Json
fields.description: GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration as a dict or a JSON string. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration and gcp_wif_configuration_json_string.

fields.name: gcp_wif_configuration_json_string
fields.label: Gcp Wif Configuration Json String
fields.description: GCP Workload Identity Federation configuration as a JSON string (contents of the configuration file). Useful for injecting configuration from secrets managers. Mutually exclusive with gcp_wif_configuration and gcp_wif_configuration_json. Note: WIF configuration typically contains public endpoint URLs rather than private keys, so SecretStr masking is not applied. If your WIF config contains sensitive material, ensure it is not logged at DEBUG level.

fields.name: auth_type
fields.label: Auth Type
fields.description: Authentication type to use. Defaults to 'hmac'. Set to 'workload_identity_federation' to authenticate via a WIF configuration file (for external workloads). Set to 'workload_identity' to use Application Default Credentials — the recommended option when running inside GKE with Workload Identity enabled.

fields.name: max_rows
fields.label: Max Rows
fields.description: Maximum number of rows to use when inferring schemas for TSV and CSV files.

fields.name: number_of_files_to_sample
fields.label: Number Of Files To Sample
fields.description: Number of files to list to sample for schema inference. This will be ignored if sample_files is set to False in the pathspec.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: github_token
fields.label: Github Token
fields.description: GitHub access token (PAT or GitHub App installation token).

fields.name: repository
fields.label: Repository
fields.description: Repository to ingest, as 'owner/repo' or 'https://github.com/owner/repo'.

fields.name: include_repository_in_browse_path
fields.label: Include Repository In Browse Path
fields.description: Include the repository name as a top-level entry in the browse path. When a repository root document is created, that document already provides the repository entry; this adds it for configurations that skip the root document or nest under a configured parent document.

fields.name: include_organization_in_browse_path
fields.label: Include Organization In Browse Path
fields.description: Include the GitHub organization/owner name as the top-most entry in the browse path, above the repository name.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: branch
fields.label: Branch
fields.description: Branch to read files from.

fields.name: path_prefix
fields.label: Path Prefix
fields.description: Only ingest files under this path (e.g. 'docs').

fields.name: file_extensions
fields.label: File Extensions
fields.description: File extensions to include (include the leading dot).

fields.name: max_files
fields.label: Max Files
fields.description: Maximum number of matching files to import per run. Additional matches are skipped with a report warning.

fields.name: create_repo_root_document
fields.label: Create Repo Root Document
fields.description: When True, create a folder document named after the repository and nest imported files beneath it.

fields.name: parent_document_urn
fields.label: Parent Document Urn
fields.description: Optional parent document URN. Top-level imported items are nested beneath this document while preserving the GitHub folder hierarchy.

fields.name: document_import_mode
fields.label: Document Import Mode
fields.description: NATIVE imports editable documents in DataHub. EXTERNAL imports read-only references that link back to GitHub.

fields.name: show_in_global_context
fields.label: Show In Global Context
fields.description: Whether imported documents appear in global search and navigation.

fields.name: document_mapping
fields.label: Document Mapping
fields.description: Document entity mapping configuration.

group_fields.name: id_pattern
group_fields.label: Id Pattern
group_fields.description: Pattern for generating document IDs

group_fields.name: id_normalization
group_fields.label: Id Normalization
group_fields.description: ID normalization rules

group_fields.name: lowercase
group_fields.label: Lowercase
group_fields.description: Convert to lowercase

group_fields.name: replace_spaces_with
group_fields.label: Replace Spaces With
group_fields.description: Replace spaces with this character

group_fields.name: remove_special_chars
group_fields.label: Remove Special Chars
group_fields.description: Remove special characters except _ and -

group_fields.name: max_length
group_fields.label: Max Length
group_fields.description: Maximum ID length

group_fields.name: title
group_fields.label: Title
group_fields.description: Title extraction configuration

group_fields.name: extract_from_content
group_fields.label: Extract From Content
group_fields.description: Try to extract title from document content

group_fields.name: fallback_to_filename
group_fields.label: Fallback To Filename
group_fields.description: Use filename as title if not found in content

group_fields.name: max_length
group_fields.label: Max Length
group_fields.description: Maximum title length

group_fields.name: source
group_fields.label: Source
group_fields.description: Source configuration

group_fields.name: type
group_fields.label: Type
group_fields.description: Document source type: NATIVE for editable DataHub documents, EXTERNAL for read-only references.

group_fields.label: Include External Url
group_fields.description: Include external URL in DocumentSource

group_fields.name: include_external_id
group_fields.label: Include External Id
group_fields.description: Include external ID in DocumentSource

group_fields.name: status

group_fields.description: Default publication status
