fields.name: aws_access_key_id
fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_session_token
fields.label: Aws Session Token
fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_role
fields.label: Aws Role
fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

fields.name: aws_profile
fields.label: Aws Profile
fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: Aws Region
fields.description: AWS region code.

fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

fields.name: aws_proxy
fields.label: Aws Proxy
fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

fields.name: aws_retry_num
fields.label: Aws Retry Num
fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

fields.name: aws_retry_mode
fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

fields.name: read_timeout
fields.label: Read Timeout
fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

fields.name: aws_advanced_config
fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: emit_storage_lineage
fields.label: Emit Storage Lineage
fields.description: Whether to emit storage-to-Glue lineage. When enabled, creates lineage relationships between Glue tables and their underlying storage locations (S3 or Iceberg).

fields.name: glue_storage_lineage_direction
fields.label: Glue Storage Lineage Direction
fields.description: If `upstream`, storage locations are upstream to Glue. If `downstream`, they are downstream to Glue.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, column-level lineage will be extracted between Glue table columns and storage location fields.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, the SQL definition of each Glue view (TableType VIRTUAL_VIEW, e.g. Athena/Presto views) is parsed to extract upstream table/view lineage, including column-level lineage. Referenced tables must also be ingested for the lineage to resolve to DataHub entities.

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Configs to ingest data profiles from glue table

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: row_count
group_fields.label: Row Count
group_fields.description: The parameter name for row count in glue table.

group_fields.name: column_count
group_fields.label: Column Count
group_fields.description: The parameter name for column count in glue table.

group_fields.name: unique_count
group_fields.label: Unique Count
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the count of unique value in a column.

group_fields.name: unique_proportion
group_fields.label: Unique Proportion
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the proportion of unique values in a column.

group_fields.name: null_count
group_fields.label: Null Count
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the count of null values in a column.

group_fields.name: null_proportion
group_fields.label: Null Proportion
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the proportion of null values in a column.

group_fields.name: min
group_fields.label: Min
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the min value of a column.

group_fields.name: max
group_fields.label: Max
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the max value of a column.

group_fields.name: mean
group_fields.label: Mean
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the mean value of a column.

group_fields.name: median
group_fields.label: Median
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the median value of a column.

group_fields.name: stdev
group_fields.label: Stdev
group_fields.description: The parameter name for the standard deviation of a column.

group_fields.name: partition_patterns
group_fields.label: Partition Patterns
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering partitions for profile. The pattern should be a string like: "{'key':'value'}".

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

fields.name: extract_owners
fields.label: Extract Owners
fields.description: When enabled, extracts ownership from Glue table property and overwrites existing owners (DATAOWNER). When disabled, ownership is left empty for datasets. Expects a corpGroup urn, a corpuser urn or only the identifier part for the latter. Not used in the normal course of AWS Glue operations.

fields.name: use_s3_bucket_tags
fields.label: Use S3 Bucket Tags
fields.description: If an S3 Buckets Tags should be created for the Tables ingested by Glue. Please Note that this will not apply tags to any folders ingested, only the files.

fields.name: use_s3_object_tags
fields.label: Use S3 Object Tags
fields.description: If an S3 Objects Tags should be created for the Tables ingested by Glue.

fields.name: extract_lakeformation_tags
fields.label: Extract Lakeformation Tags
fields.description: When True, extracts Lake Formation tags directly assigned to Glue tables/databases. Tags inherited from databases are excluded by AWS from the per-table response; enable `propagate_lakeformation_tags` to apply database tags to their tables as well.

fields.name: propagate_lakeformation_tags
fields.label: Propagate Lakeformation Tags
fields.description: When True (requires `extract_lakeformation_tags`), Lake Formation tags assigned to a Glue database are inherited by every table in that database, and (when `extract_lakeformation_column_tags` is also enabled) by every column as well — matching Lake Formation's own inheritance. AWS returns the database's tags alongside each table's own tags, so no extra API calls are made. A more-specific assignment overrides an inherited value for the same key (table over database, column over table/database).

fields.name: extract_lakeformation_column_tags
fields.label: Extract Lakeformation Column Tags
fields.description: When True (requires `extract_lakeformation_tags`), Lake Formation tags assigned directly to columns are applied to the corresponding schema fields. If `propagate_lakeformation_tags` is also enabled, the table's and database's tags are inherited by every column as well (matching Lake Formation's inheritance), with a column's own tag taking precedence for the same key.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: incremental_properties
fields.label: Incremental Properties
fields.description: When enabled, emits dataset properties as incremental to existing dataset properties in DataHub. When disabled, re-states dataset properties on each run.

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: The platform to use for the dataset URNs. Must be one of ['glue', 'athena'].

fields.name: extract_transforms
fields.label: Extract Transforms
fields.description: Whether to extract Glue transform jobs.

fields.name: ignore_unsupported_connectors
fields.label: Ignore Unsupported Connectors
fields.description: Whether to ignore unsupported connectors. If disabled, an error will be raised.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: regex patterns for tables to filter to assign domain_key.

fields.name: catalog_id
fields.label: Catalog Id
fields.description: The aws account id where the target glue catalog lives. If None, datahub will ingest glue in aws caller's account.

fields.name: ignore_resource_links
fields.label: Ignore Resource Links
fields.description: If set to True, ignore database resource links.

fields.name: extract_delta_schema_from_parameters
fields.label: Extract Delta Schema From Parameters
fields.description: If enabled, delta schemas can be alternatively fetched from table parameters.

fields.name: extract_column_parameters
fields.label: Extract Column Parameters
fields.description: When enabled, column-level Parameters from Glue are ingested as structured properties on each schemaField entity. A StructuredPropertyDefinition is upserted once per unique parameter key per recipe run; subsequent columns reuse the cached definition.

fields.name: target_platform_configs
fields.label: Target Platform Configs
fields.description: Optional per-platform config for aligning dataset URNs with separately ingested platforms. Keys are DataHub platform names (e.g. 'postgres', 'mysql', 'redshift'). When provided, the platform_instance and env are applied to dataset URNs so they match the URNs produced by the platform's own connector. Only needed when the target platform's connector uses a platform_instance or a different env.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: url
fields.label: Url
fields.description: Grafana URL in the format http://your-grafana-instance with no trailing slash

fields.name: service_account_token
fields.label: Service Account Token
fields.description: Service account token for Grafana

fields.name: dashboard_pattern
fields.label: Dashboard Pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern to filter dashboards for ingestion

fields.name: folder_pattern
fields.label: Folder Pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern to filter folders for ingestion

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform_instance mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract lineage between charts and data sources. When enabled, the source will parse SQL queries and datasource configurations to build lineage relationships.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract column-level lineage from SQL queries. Only applicable when include_lineage is enabled.

fields.name: ingest_tags
fields.label: Ingest Tags
fields.description: Whether to ingest dashboard and chart tags

fields.name: ingest_owners
fields.label: Ingest Owners
fields.description: Whether to ingest dashboard ownership information

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Whether to verify SSL certificates when connecting to Grafana

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: Number of items to fetch per API call when paginating through folders and dashboards

fields.name: basic_mode
fields.label: Basic Mode
fields.description: Enable basic extraction mode for users with limited permissions. In basic mode, only dashboard metadata is extracted without detailed panel information, lineage, or folder hierarchy. This requires only basic dashboard read permissions.

fields.name: remove_email_suffix
fields.label: Remove Email Suffix
fields.description: Remove Grafana user email suffix for example, @acryl.io, when assigning ownership.

fields.name: skip_text_panels
fields.label: Skip Text Panels
fields.description: Whether to skip text panels during ingestion. Text panels don't contain data visualizations and may not be relevant for data lineage.

fields.name: connection_to_platform_map
fields.label: Connection To Platform Map
fields.description: Map of Grafana datasource types/UIDs to platform connection configs for lineage extraction

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: SAP HANA host and port, e.g. `myhost.example.com:30015`. HANA Cloud requires TLS — pass `encrypt=true` via `options.connect_args` if the driver does not enable it.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. SAP-managed `_SYS_*` schemas (except `_SYS_BIC`) are denied by default.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: include_calculation_views
fields.label: Include Calculation Views
fields.description: If true, ingest SAP HANA calculation views from `_SYS_REPO.ACTIVE_OBJECT` with column-level lineage parsed from their XML. On-premise / self-managed HANA only.

fields.name: calculation_view_pattern
fields.label: Calculation View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns matched against `<package_id>.<view_name>` (e.g. `acme.analytics.SalesOverview`) to filter calculation views. Use this instead of `view_pattern` for calculation views.

fields.name: include_stored_procedures
fields.label: Include Stored Procedures
fields.description: If true, ingest stored procedures as `DataJob` entities with table-level lineage parsed from their bodies.

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns matched against `<schema>.<procedure_name>` to filter stored procedures.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: include_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

fields.name: format_sql_queries
fields.label: Format Sql Queries
fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

fields.name: include_top_n_queries
fields.label: Include Top N Queries
fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: include_query_usage
fields.label: Include Query Usage
fields.description: If true, mine query history from `_SYS_STATISTICS.HOST_SQL_PLAN_CACHE`. Requires the `MONITORING` role or `CATALOG READ` system privilege.

fields.name: include_usage_stats
fields.label: Include Usage Stats
fields.description: If true, emit `DatasetUsageStatistics` aspects. Requires `include_query_usage` and the same `MONITORING` / `CATALOG READ` privilege.

fields.name: usage_max_queries
fields.label: Usage Max Queries
fields.description: Maximum number of distinct `(statement_hash, last_execution_timestamp)` rows pulled from `HOST_SQL_PLAN_CACHE` per ingestion run. Distinct from `top_n_queries`, which caps the per-bucket rollup.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Base Url
fields.description: Hex API base URL. For most Hex users, this will be https://app.hex.tech/api/v1. Single-tenant app users should replace this with the URL they use to access Hex.

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.description: Hex Workspace Token with the 'Read projects' scope. Create one at Settings → API → Workspace tokens. The 'Read projects' scope is required to access project cells for lineage; tokens without it can enumerate projects but not read their content. See https://learn.hex.tech/docs/api-integrations/api/overview for token types.

fields.name: workspace_name
fields.label: Workspace Name
fields.description: Hex workspace name. Find it in the workspace switcher dropdown in the top-left corner of the Hex app.

fields.name: include_components
fields.label: Include Components
fields.description: Include Hex Components in the ingestion

fields.name: project_title_pattern
fields.label: Project Title Pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern for project titles to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: component_title_pattern
fields.label: Component Title Pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern for component titles to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: include_run_history
fields.label: Include Run History
fields.description: Emit the most recent COMPLETED run as a DashboardInfo PATCH setting lastRefreshed.

fields.name: include_context_documents
fields.label: Include Context Documents
fields.description: Emit a DataHub Document per Project and per Component containing SQL sources, visualisation metadata, and notebook documentation. Documents are hidden from global search and linked to the Dashboard/Chart for AI agent retrieval.

fields.name: category_pattern
fields.label: Category Pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern for categories to filter in ingestion. This will exclude any project or component that has any category denied or not explicitly allowed.

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Extract upstream lineage. Uses queriedTables API (Hex Enterprise workspaces) or falls back to parsing SQL from cells (all workspaces). No warehouse ingestion dependency required.

fields.name: use_queried_tables_lineage
fields.label: Use Queried Tables Lineage
fields.description: Use Hex's queriedTables API (Hex Enterprise workspaces only) as the primary lineage source for published projects and components. Unpublished entities always fall back to SQL-cell parsing since queriedTables is only populated for published runs. Set to False to force SQL-cell parsing for everything.

fields.name: collections_as_tags
fields.label: Collections As Tags
fields.description: Emit Hex Collections as tags

fields.name: status_as_tag
fields.label: Status As Tag
fields.description: Emit Hex Status as tags

fields.name: categories_as_tags
fields.label: Categories As Tags
fields.description: Emit Hex Category as tags

fields.name: set_ownership_from_email
fields.label: Set Ownership From Email
fields.description: Set ownership identity from owner/creator email

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration for stateful ingestion and stale metadata removal.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: workspace_id
fields.label: Workspace Id
fields.description: Hex workspace (org) UUID, used to build external URLs to the Hex app (e.g. https://app.hex.tech/<workspace_id>/hex/<project_id>). If left unset, the connector calls /users/me to auto-discover it — which requires the token to have 'Users → Read access'. Set this explicitly to avoid granting that scope. Find the UUID in any Hex project URL.

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: Number of items to fetch per Hex API call.

fields.name: patch_metadata
fields.label: Patch Metadata
fields.description: Emit metadata as patch events

fields.name: connection_platform_map
fields.label: Connection Platform Map
fields.description: Per-connection lineage configuration, keyed by Hex dataConnectionId (UUID). Pins platform and platform_instance so upstream URNs match the warehouse's ingestion. Example: {"<uuid>": {"platform": "snowflake", "platform_instance": "prod_snowflake"}}

fields.name: max_projects
fields.label: Max Projects
fields.description: Maximum number of projects to process. Useful for testing or staged rollouts. Components discovered during project processing are not counted. Defaults to None (process all projects). WARNING: with stateful ingestion enabled, projects beyond this limit are soft-deleted on the next run.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: host URL

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: emit_storage_lineage
fields.label: Emit Storage Lineage
fields.description: Whether to emit storage-to-Hive lineage. When enabled, creates lineage relationships between Hive tables and their underlying storage locations (S3, Azure, GCS, HDFS, etc.).

fields.name: hive_storage_lineage_direction
fields.label: Hive Storage Lineage Direction
fields.description: Direction of storage lineage. If 'upstream', storage is treated as upstream to Hive (data flows from storage to Hive). If 'downstream', storage is downstream to Hive (data flows from Hive to storage).

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: When enabled along with emit_storage_lineage, column-level lineage will be extracted between Hive table columns and storage location fields.

fields.name: storage_platform_instance
fields.label: Storage Platform Instance
fields.description: Platform instance for the storage system (e.g., 'my-s3-instance'). Used when generating URNs for storage datasets.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count

group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count

group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile

group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days

group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: Host and port. For SQL: database port (3306/5432). For Thrift: HMS Thrift port (9083).

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter.

fields.name: include_catalog_name_in_ids
fields.label: Include Catalog Name In Ids
fields.description: Add catalog name to dataset URNs. Example: urn:li:dataset:(urn:li:dataPlatform:hive,catalog.db.table,PROD)

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: emit_storage_lineage
fields.label: Emit Storage Lineage
fields.description: Whether to emit storage-to-Hive lineage. When enabled, creates lineage relationships between Hive tables and their underlying storage locations (S3, Azure, GCS, HDFS, etc.).

fields.name: hive_storage_lineage_direction
fields.label: Hive Storage Lineage Direction
fields.description: Direction of storage lineage. If 'upstream', storage is treated as upstream to Hive (data flows from storage to Hive). If 'downstream', storage is downstream to Hive (data flows from Hive to storage).

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: When enabled along with emit_storage_lineage, column-level lineage will be extracted between Hive table columns and storage location fields.

fields.name: storage_platform_instance
fields.label: Storage Platform Instance
fields.description: Platform instance for the storage system (e.g., 'my-s3-instance'). Used when generating URNs for storage datasets.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Extract lineage from Hive views by parsing view definitions.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern

group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration for stateful ingestion and stale entity removal.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: connection_type
fields.label: Connection Type
fields.description: Connection method: 'sql' for direct database access (MySQL/PostgreSQL), 'thrift' for HMS Thrift API with optional Kerberos support.

fields.name: views_where_clause_suffix
fields.label: Views Where Clause Suffix
fields.description: DEPRECATED: This option has been deprecated for security reasons and will be removed in a future release. Use 'database_pattern' instead.

fields.name: tables_where_clause_suffix
fields.label: Tables Where Clause Suffix
fields.description: DEPRECATED: This option has been deprecated for security reasons and will be removed in a future release. Use 'database_pattern' instead.

fields.name: schemas_where_clause_suffix
fields.label: Schemas Where Clause Suffix
fields.description: DEPRECATED: This option has been deprecated for security reasons and will be removed in a future release. Use 'database_pattern' instead.

fields.name: metastore_db_name
fields.label: Metastore Db Name
fields.description: Name of the Hive metastore's database (usually: metastore). For backward compatibility, if not provided, the database field will be used. If both 'database' and 'metastore_db_name' are set, 'database' is used for filtering.

fields.name: use_kerberos
fields.label: Use Kerberos
fields.description: Whether to use Kerberos/SASL authentication. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: kerberos_service_name
fields.label: Kerberos Service Name
fields.description: Kerberos service name for the HMS principal. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: kerberos_hostname_override
fields.label: Kerberos Hostname Override
fields.description: Override hostname for Kerberos principal construction. Use when connecting through a load balancer. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: kerberos_qop
fields.label: Kerberos Qop
fields.description: Kerberos Quality of Protection (QOP) for SASL authentication. Options: 'auth' (authentication only), 'auth-int' (authentication + integrity), 'auth-conf' (authentication + confidentiality/encryption). Must match the server's hadoop.rpc.protection setting. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.label: Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Connection timeout in seconds. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: catalog_name
fields.label: Catalog Name
fields.description: Catalog name for HMS 3.x multi-catalog deployments. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: mode
fields.label: Mode
fields.description: Platform mode for metadata. Valid options: ['hive', 'presto', 'presto-on-hive', 'trino']

fields.name: use_catalog_subtype
fields.label: Use Catalog Subtype
fields.description: Use 'Catalog' (True) or 'Database' (False) as container subtype.

fields.name: use_dataset_pascalcase_subtype
fields.label: Use Dataset Pascalcase Subtype
fields.description: Use 'Table'/'View' (True) or 'table'/'view' (False) as dataset subtype.

fields.name: enable_properties_merge
fields.label: Enable Properties Merge
fields.description: Merge properties with existing server data instead of overwriting.

fields.name: simplify_nested_field_paths
fields.label: Simplify Nested Field Paths
fields.description: Simplify v2 field paths to v1. Falls back to v2 for Union/Array types.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: catalog
fields.label: Catalog
fields.description: Catalog configuration where to find Iceberg tables. Only one catalog specification is supported. The format is the same as [pyiceberg's catalog configuration](https://py.iceberg.apache.org/configuration/), where the catalog name is specified as the object name and attributes are set as key-value pairs.

fields.name: namespace_pattern
fields.label: Namespace Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for namespaces to filter in ingestion.

group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

fields.name: user_ownership_property
fields.label: User Ownership Property
fields.description: Iceberg table property to look for a `CorpUser` owner. Can only hold a single user value. If property has no value, no owner information will be emitted.

fields.name: group_ownership_property
fields.label: Group Ownership Property
fields.description: Iceberg table property to look for a `CorpGroup` owner. Can only hold a single group value. If property has no value, no owner information will be emitted.

fields.description: Iceberg Stateful Ingestion Config.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: A map of domain names to allow/deny patterns for tables and namespaces. Domain key can be a guid like "urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba" or a string like "Engineering". If multiple patterns match, at most one domain is assigned to the entity.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Informatica Cloud username (email or service account name).

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Informatica Cloud password.

fields.name: project_pattern
fields.label: Project Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter IDMC projects by name. Only projects matching these patterns will be ingested. Example: allow: ['Production.*'], deny: ['.*_sandbox']

fields.name: folder_pattern
fields.label: Folder Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter IDMC folders by name. Only folders matching these patterns will be ingested.

fields.name: mapping_task_pattern
fields.label: Mapping Task Pattern
