fields.description: Regex patterns to filter Mapping Tasks. Matched against '<folder_path>/<name>' so same-named tasks in different folders can be targeted independently (e.g. allow: ['.*/ProjectA/MyTask']).

fields.name: taskflow_pattern
fields.label: Taskflow Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter Taskflows. Matched against '<folder_path>/<name>' so same-named taskflows in different folders can be targeted independently (e.g. allow: ['.*/ProjectA/MyFlow']).

fields.name: extract_lineage
fields.label: Extract Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract table-level lineage from mapping definitions. Requires the 'Asset - export' privilege on the service account. When enabled, uses the v3 Export API to fetch full mapping definitions.

fields.name: tag_filter_names
fields.label: Tag Filter Names
fields.description: List of literal IDMC tag names. When set, only objects tagged with at least one of these tags will be ingested. Tags are matched exactly (not regex). This is the recommended filtering approach for large orgs — IDMC admins tag objects in the UI and the connector picks them up.

fields.name: extract_ownership
fields.label: Extract Ownership
fields.description: Whether to extract ownership from IDMC object createdBy/updatedBy fields.

fields.name: extract_tags
fields.label: Extract Tags
fields.description: Emit IDMC object tags as DataHub GlobalTags on Projects, Folders, Taskflows, and Mapping Tasks. Set to False to skip tag extraction.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration for stateful ingestion and stale entity removal.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: login_url
fields.label: Login Url
fields.description: Informatica Cloud login URL. This is the regional pod URL, not the runtime serverUrl. After login, the connector discovers the actual API base URL from the login response. Common values: https://dm-us.informaticacloud.com (US), https://dm2-us.informaticacloud.com (US2), https://dm-em.informaticacloud.com (EMEA), https://dm-ap.informaticacloud.com (APAC).

fields.name: strip_user_email_domain
fields.label: Strip User Email Domain
fields.description: Strip the domain from IDMC user identifiers before forming the CorpUser URN (e.g. ``alice@acme.com`` → ``urn:li:corpuser:alice``). Enable when your Okta/AzureAD source ingests users without the email domain so ownership edges align with existing CorpUser URNs.

fields.name: connection_type_overrides
fields.label: Connection Type Overrides
fields.description: Per-connection-ID override mapping IDMC connection id → DataHub platform name. Use when a single connection can't be auto-resolved (e.g., a one-off custom connector). Example: {'01DM180B000000000008': 'snowflake'}. Takes priority over `connection_type_platform_map` and the built-in CONNECTION_TYPE_MAP.

fields.name: connection_type_platform_map
fields.label: Connection Type Platform Map
fields.description: Extend the built-in connection-type → DataHub-platform map with custom entries. Keys are the IDMC `connParams["Connection Type"]` string (or the connection's top-level `type` as a fallback), values are DataHub platform names. Useful for new IDMC marketplace connectors that aren't in the built-in map yet. Example: {'MyCustomConnector_v3': 'snowflake', 'CompanyDW': 'postgres'}. Entries here are merged with (and override) CONNECTION_TYPE_MAP.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Lowercase the dataset qualifier in emitted upstream URNs to match the default behavior of the Snowflake, Postgres, and BigQuery sources (which lowercase by default). Set to False only if you've disabled lowercasing on every source this connector produces lineage to.

fields.name: connection_to_platform_instance
fields.label: Connection To Platform Instance
fields.description: Map IDMC connection ID → DataHub `platform_instance` to use when building upstream/downstream dataset URNs. Required whenever the target source was ingested with a non-default platform_instance; otherwise lineage edges will point at URNs that don't exist in DataHub. Example: {'01DM180B000000000008': 'prod_sf'}.

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: Number of objects to fetch per API page (max 200 for v3 objects).

fields.name: export_batch_size
fields.label: Export Batch Size
fields.description: Number of mappings per v3 export batch job (max 1000).

fields.name: export_poll_timeout_secs
fields.label: Export Poll Timeout Secs
fields.description: Timeout in seconds for polling export job completion.

fields.name: export_poll_interval_secs
fields.label: Export Poll Interval Secs
fields.description: Interval in seconds between export job status polls.

fields.name: request_timeout_secs
fields.label: Request Timeout Secs
fields.description: HTTP timeout in seconds for IDMC API requests. Raise this for large deployments where /api/v2/mapping or /api/v2/connection returns many records and the default 60s is insufficient.

fields.name: max_concurrent_export_jobs
fields.label: Max Concurrent Export Jobs
fields.description: Maximum number of v3 export jobs to run concurrently. Each job covers one batch of mappings. Increase to reduce lineage wall-clock time on large orgs; decrease if hitting IDMC rate limits.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: path
fields.label: Path
fields.description: Set this to a single file-path or a directory-path (for recursive traversal) or a remote url. e.g. https://json.schemastore.org/petstore-v1.0.json

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: Set this to a platform that you want all schemas to live under. e.g. schemaregistry / schemarepo etc.

fields.name: uri_replace_pattern
fields.label: Uri Replace Pattern
fields.description: Use this if URI-s need to be modified during reference resolution. Simple string match - replace capabilities are supported.

group_fields.name: match
group_fields.label: Match
group_fields.description: Pattern to match on uri-s as part of reference resolution. See replace field

group_fields.name: replace
group_fields.label: Replace
group_fields.description: Pattern to replace with as part of reference resolution. See match field

fields.name: dataset_name_replace_pattern
fields.label: Dataset Name Replace Pattern
fields.description: Apply a string match-replace on the computed display name. Runs after dataset_name_strategy is applied.

group_fields.name: match
group_fields.label: Match
group_fields.description: Pattern to match on uri-s as part of reference resolution. See replace field

group_fields.name: replace
group_fields.label: Replace
group_fields.description: Pattern to replace with as part of reference resolution. See match field

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: use_id_as_base_uri
fields.label: Use Id As Base Uri
fields.description: When enabled, uses the `$id` field in the json schema as the base uri for following references.

fields.name: dataset_name_strategy
fields.label: Dataset Name Strategy
fields.description: Controls how the display name for datasets is computed. BASENAME (default) uses the last path segment. SCHEMA_ID uses the full $id URI. TITLE uses the 'title' field from the schema.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: schema_registry_url
fields.label: Schema Registry Url
fields.description: Schema registry URL. Can be overridden with KAFKA_SCHEMAREGISTRY_URL environment variable, or will use DATAHUB_GMS_BASE_PATH if not set.

fields.name: schema_registry_config
fields.label: Schema Registry Config
fields.description: Extra schema registry config serialized as JSON. These options will be passed into Kafka's SchemaRegistryClient. https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/clients/confluent-kafka-python/html/index.html?#schemaregistryclient

fields.name: client_timeout_seconds
fields.label: Client Timeout Seconds
fields.description: The request timeout used when interacting with the Kafka APIs.

fields.name: consumer_config
fields.label: Consumer Config
fields.description: Extra consumer config serialized as JSON. These options will be passed into Kafka's DeserializingConsumer. See https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/clients/confluent-kafka-python/html/index.html#deserializingconsumer and https://github.com/edenhill/librdkafka/blob/master/CONFIGURATION.md .

fields.name: topic_patterns
fields.label: Topic Patterns
fields.description: A class to store allow deny regexes

fields.name: ingest_schemas_as_entities
fields.label: Ingest Schemas As Entities
fields.description: Enables ingesting schemas from schema registry as separate entities, in addition to the topics

fields.name: schema_tags_field
fields.label: Schema Tags Field
fields.description: The field name in the schema metadata that contains the tags to be added to the dataset.

fields.name: tag_prefix
fields.label: Tag Prefix
fields.description: Prefix added to tags during ingestion.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: A map of domain names to allow deny patterns. Domains can be urn-based (`urn:li:domain:13ae4d85-d955-49fc-8474-9004c663a810`) or bare (`13ae4d85-d955-49fc-8474-9004c663a810`).

fields.name: topic_subject_map
fields.label: Topic Subject Map
fields.description: Provides the mapping for the `key` and the `value` schemas of a topic to the corresponding schema registry subject name. Each entry of this map has the form `<topic_name>-key`:`<schema_registry_subject_name_for_key_schema>` and `<topic_name>-value`:`<schema_registry_subject_name_for_value_schema>` for the key and the value schemas associated with the topic, respectively. This parameter is mandatory when the [RecordNameStrategy](https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/schema-registry/serdes-develop/index.html#how-the-naming-strategies-work) is used as the subject naming strategy in the kafka schema registry. NOTE: When provided, this overrides the default subject name resolution even when the `TopicNameStrategy` or the `TopicRecordNameStrategy` are used.

fields.name: schema_registry_class
fields.label: Schema Registry Class
fields.description: The fully qualified implementation class(custom) that implements the KafkaSchemaRegistryBase interface.

fields.name: enable_meta_mapping
fields.label: Enable Meta Mapping
fields.description: When enabled, applies the mappings that are defined through the meta_mapping directives.

fields.name: meta_mapping
fields.label: Meta Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against top-level schema properties. Refer to the section below on meta automated mappings.

fields.name: field_meta_mapping
fields.label: Field Meta Mapping
fields.description: mapping rules that will be executed against field-level schema properties. Refer to the section below on meta automated mappings.

fields.name: strip_user_ids_from_email
fields.label: Strip User Ids From Email
fields.description: Whether or not to strip email id while adding owners using meta mappings.

fields.name: ignore_warnings_on_schema_type
fields.label: Ignore Warnings On Schema Type
fields.description: Disables warnings reported for non-AVRO/Protobuf value or key schemas if set.

fields.name: disable_topic_record_naming_strategy
fields.label: Disable Topic Record Naming Strategy
fields.description: Disables the utilization of the TopicRecordNameStrategy for Schema Registry subjects. For more information, visit: https://docs.confluent.io/platform/current/schema-registry/serdes-develop/index.html#handling-differences-between-preregistered-and-client-derived-schemas:~:text=io.confluent.kafka.serializers.subject.TopicRecordNameStrategy

fields.name: external_url_base
fields.label: External Url Base
fields.description: Base URL for external platform (e.g. Aiven) where topics can be viewed. The topic name will be appended to this base URL.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: connect_uri
fields.label: Connect Uri
fields.description: URI to connect to.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Kafka Connect username.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Kafka Connect password.

fields.name: kafka_api_key
fields.label: Kafka Api Key
fields.description: Optional: Confluent Cloud Kafka API key for authenticating with Kafka REST API v3. If not specified, DataHub will reuse the Connect credentials (username/password) for Kafka API authentication. Only needed if you want to use separate credentials for the Kafka API.

fields.name: kafka_api_secret
fields.label: Kafka Api Secret
fields.description: Optional: Confluent Cloud Kafka API secret for authenticating with Kafka REST API v3. If not specified, DataHub will reuse the Connect credentials (username/password) for Kafka API authentication. Only needed if you want to use separate credentials for the Kafka API.

fields.name: connector_patterns
fields.label: Connector Patterns
fields.description: regex patterns for connectors to filter for ingestion.

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: Platform instance mapping to use when constructing URNs. e.g.`platform_instance_map: { "hive": "warehouse" }`

fields.name: convert_lineage_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Lineage Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert the urns of ingested lineage dataset to lowercase

fields.name: schema_resolver_finegrained_lineage
fields.label: Schema Resolver Finegrained Lineage
fields.description: Enable fine-grained (column-level) lineage extraction using DataHub schema metadata. When use_schema_resolver=True, this controls whether to generate column-level lineage by matching schemas between source tables and Kafka topics. Only applies when use_schema_resolver is enabled. Defaults to True when use_schema_resolver is enabled.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: cluster_name
fields.label: Cluster Name
fields.description: Cluster to ingest from.

fields.name: provided_configs
fields.label: Provided Configs
fields.description: Provided Configurations

fields.name: connect_to_platform_map
fields.label: Connect To Platform Map
fields.description: Platform instance mapping when multiple instances for a platform is available. Entry for a platform should be in either `platform_instance_map` or `connect_to_platform_map`. e.g.`connect_to_platform_map: { "postgres-connector-finance-db": "postgres": "core_finance_instance" }`

fields.name: generic_connectors
fields.label: Generic Connectors
fields.description: Provide lineage graph for sources connectors other than Confluent JDBC Source Connector, Debezium Source Connector, and Mongo Source Connector

fields.name: use_connect_topics_api
fields.label: Use Connect Topics Api
fields.description: Whether to use Kafka Connect API for topic retrieval and validation. This flag controls the environment-specific topic retrieval strategy: **When True (default):** - **Self-hosted environments:** Uses runtime `/connectors/{name}/topics` API for accurate topic information - **Confluent Cloud:** Uses comprehensive Kafka REST API v3 to get all topics for transform pipeline, with config-based fallback **When False:** Disables all API-based topic retrieval for both environments. Returns empty topic lists. Useful for air-gapped environments or when topic validation isn't needed for performance optimization.

fields.name: kafka_rest_endpoint
fields.label: Kafka Rest Endpoint
fields.description: Optional: Confluent Cloud Kafka REST API endpoint for comprehensive topic retrieval. Format: https://pkc-xxxxx.region.provider.confluent.cloud If not specified, DataHub automatically derives the endpoint from connector configurations (kafka.endpoint). When available, enables getting all topics from Kafka cluster for improved transform pipeline accuracy.

fields.name: confluent_cloud_environment_id
fields.label: Confluent Cloud Environment Id
fields.description: Confluent Cloud environment ID (e.g., 'env-xyz123'). When specified along with confluent_cloud_cluster_id, the connect_uri will be automatically constructed. This is the recommended approach for Confluent Cloud instead of manually constructing the full URI.

fields.name: confluent_cloud_cluster_id
fields.label: Confluent Cloud Cluster Id
fields.description: Confluent Cloud Kafka Connect cluster ID (e.g., 'lkc-abc123'). When specified along with confluent_cloud_environment_id, the connect_uri will be automatically constructed. This is the recommended approach for Confluent Cloud instead of manually constructing the full URI.

fields.name: use_schema_resolver
fields.label: Use Schema Resolver
fields.description: Use DataHub's schema metadata to enhance Kafka Connect connector lineage. When enabled (requires DataHub graph connection): 1) Expands table patterns (e.g., 'database.*') to actual tables using DataHub metadata 2) Generates fine-grained column-level lineage for Kafka Connect sources/sinks. **Auto-enabled for Confluent Cloud:** This feature is automatically enabled for Confluent Cloud environments where DataHub graph connection is required. Set `use_schema_resolver: false` to disable. **Prerequisite:** Source database tables must be ingested into DataHub before Kafka Connect ingestion for this feature to work. Without prior database ingestion, schema resolver will not find table metadata.

fields.name: schema_resolver_expand_patterns
fields.label: Schema Resolver Expand Patterns
fields.description: Enable table pattern expansion using DataHub schema metadata. When use_schema_resolver=True, this controls whether to expand patterns like 'database.*' to actual table names by querying DataHub. Only applies when use_schema_resolver is enabled. Defaults to True when use_schema_resolver is enabled.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: Standard DataHub AWS connection config (creds, region, role assumption, endpoint override). Optional — when omitted, boto3 resolves everything from the standard credential chain (env vars, shared credentials file, IAM role, SSO profile).

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: include_streams
fields.label: Include Streams
fields.description: Extract Kinesis Data Streams (KDS).

fields.name: stream_pattern
fields.label: Stream Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for which Kinesis streams to include.

fields.name: include_firehose
fields.label: Include Firehose
fields.description: Extract Amazon Data Firehose streams.

fields.name: firehose_stream_pattern
fields.label: Firehose Stream Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for which Firehose streams to include.

fields.name: include_table_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Lineage
fields.description: Emit Firehose-stream → destination lineage edges.

fields.name: extract_tags
fields.label: Extract Tags
fields.description: Extract AWS resource tags as DataHub globalTags. To derive ownership from a tag, apply the `extract_ownership_from_tags` transformer to the emitted tags (see the connector docs).

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration for stale entity removal.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: glue_schema_registry
fields.label: Glue Schema Registry
fields.description: Glue Schema Registry integration (disabled by default).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: When True, fetch Glue Schema Registry schemas for streams. Requires the additional `glue:Get*` / `glue:List*` IAM permissions listed in the connector prerequisites.

group_fields.name: registry_name
group_fields.label: Registry Name
group_fields.description: Glue Schema Registry registry name to query.

group_fields.name: stream_schema_map
group_fields.label: Stream Schema Map
group_fields.description: Explicit override map: stream name → schema name within registry_name. Wins over use_naming_convention.

group_fields.name: use_naming_convention
group_fields.label: Use Naming Convention
group_fields.description: Heuristic fallback: when `stream_schema_map` has no entry for a stream, look up a Glue schema whose name matches the stream name. Disabled by default — unlike Kafka + Confluent Schema Registry (which has a documented `TopicNameStrategy`), AWS Glue Schema Registry has NO AWS-defined relationship between a Kinesis Data Stream and a schema: schemas are chosen per-record by producers, and one stream may carry multiple schemas. Enable only if your organization has adopted schema-name == stream-name as an internal convention.

fields.name: destination_platform_map
fields.label: Destination Platform Map
fields.description: Map destination platform name (one of 's3', 'redshift', 'snowflake', 'iceberg', 'mongodb', 'elasticsearch', 'glue') to platform_instance + env overrides. REQUIRED when the destination platform was ingested with a platform_instance — without this, lineage edges reference non-existent URNs (silent failure mode). Unknown platform keys are rejected at parse time by the DestinationPlatform Literal.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: ldap_server
fields.label: Ldap Server
fields.description: LDAP server URL.

fields.name: ldap_user
fields.label: Ldap User
fields.description: LDAP user.

fields.name: ldap_password
fields.label: Ldap Password
fields.description: LDAP password.

fields.name: base_dn
fields.label: Base Dn
fields.description: LDAP DN.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: filter
fields.label: Filter
fields.description: LDAP extractor filter.

fields.name: attrs_list
fields.label: Attrs List
fields.description: Retrieved attributes list

fields.name: custom_props_list
fields.label: Custom Props List
fields.description: A list of custom attributes to extract from the LDAP provider.

fields.name: drop_missing_first_last_name
fields.label: Drop Missing First Last Name
fields.description: If set to true, any users without first and last names will be dropped.

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: Size of each page to fetch when extracting metadata.

fields.name: manager_filter_enabled
fields.label: Manager Filter Enabled
fields.description: Use LDAP extractor filter to search managers.

fields.name: manager_pagination_enabled
fields.label: Manager Pagination Enabled
fields.description: [deprecated] Use pagination_enabled

fields.name: pagination_enabled
fields.label: Pagination Enabled
fields.description: Use pagination while do search query (enabled by default).

fields.name: use_email_as_username
fields.label: Use Email As Username
fields.description: Use email for users' usernames instead of username (disabled by default). If enabled, the user and group urn would be having email as the id part of the urn.

fields.name: tls_verify
fields.label: Tls Verify
fields.description: Verify server TLS certificates for LDAPS connections. Disabling in production exposes connections to Man-in-the-Middle attacks (CWE-295).

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Base Url
fields.description: Url to your Looker instance: `https://company.looker.com:19999` or `https://looker.company.com`, or similar. Used for making API calls to Looker and constructing clickable dashboard and chart urls.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Looker API client id.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Looker API client secret.

fields.name: explore_naming_pattern
fields.label: Explore Naming Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing dataset names to explores. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name']

fields.name: explore_browse_pattern
fields.label: Explore Browse Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing browse paths to explores. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name']

fields.name: view_naming_pattern
fields.label: View Naming Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing dataset names to views. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name', 'file_path', 'folder_path']

fields.name: view_browse_pattern
fields.label: View Browse Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing browse paths to views. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name', 'file_path', 'folder_path']

fields.name: dashboard_pattern
fields.label: Dashboard Pattern
fields.description: Patterns for selecting dashboard ids that are to be included

fields.name: chart_pattern
fields.label: Chart Pattern
fields.description: Patterns for selecting chart ids that are to be included

fields.name: include_deleted
fields.label: Include Deleted
fields.description: Whether to include deleted dashboards and looks.

fields.name: include_platform_instance_in_urns
fields.label: Include Platform Instance In Urns
fields.description: When enabled, platform instance will be added in dashboard and chart urn.

fields.name: folder_path_pattern
fields.label: Folder Path Pattern
fields.description: Allow or deny dashboards from specific folders using their fully qualified paths. For example: deny: - Shared/deprecated This pattern will deny the ingestion of all dashboards and looks within the Shared/deprecated folder. allow: - Shared/sales This pattern will allow only the ingestion of dashboards within the Shared/sales folder. To get the correct path from Looker, take the folder hierarchy shown in the UI and join it with slashes. For example, Shared -> Customer Reports -> Sales becomes Shared/Customer Reports/Sales. Dashboards will only be ingested if they're allowed by both this config and dashboard_pattern.

fields.name: extract_column_level_lineage
fields.label: Extract Column Level Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, extracts column-level lineage from Views and Explores

fields.name: extract_usage_history
fields.label: Extract Usage History
fields.description: Whether to ingest usage statistics for dashboards. Setting this to True will query looker system activity explores to fetch historical dashboard usage.

fields.name: extract_usage_history_for_interval
fields.label: Extract Usage History For Interval
fields.description: Used only if extract_usage_history is set to True. Interval to extract looker dashboard usage history for. See https://docs.looker.com/reference/filter-expressions#date_and_time.

fields.name: tag_measures_and_dimensions
fields.label: Tag Measures And Dimensions
fields.description: When enabled, attaches tags to measures, dimensions and dimension groups to make them more discoverable. When disabled, adds this information to the description of the column.

fields.name: extract_owners
fields.label: Extract Owners
fields.description: When enabled, extracts ownership from Looker directly. When disabled, ownership is left empty for dashboards and charts.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: transport_options
fields.label: Transport Options
fields.description: Populates the [TransportOptions](https://github.com/looker-open-source/sdk-codegen/blob/94d6047a0d52912ac082eb91616c1e7c379ab262/python/looker_sdk/rtl/transport.py#L70) struct for looker client

fields.name: max_retries
fields.label: Max Retries
fields.description: Number of retries for Looker API calls

fields.name: max_threads
fields.label: Max Threads
fields.description: Max parallelism for Looker API calls. Defaults to cpuCount or 40

fields.name: strip_user_ids_from_email
fields.label: Strip User Ids From Email
fields.description: When enabled, converts Looker user emails of the form name@domain.com to urn:li:corpuser:name when assigning ownership

fields.name: skip_personal_folders
fields.label: Skip Personal Folders
fields.description: Whether to skip ingestion of dashboards in personal folders. Setting this to True will only ingest dashboards in the Shared folder space.

fields.name: external_base_url
fields.label: External Base Url
fields.description: Optional URL to use when constructing external URLs to Looker if the `base_url` is not the correct one to use. For example, `https://looker-public.company.com`. If not provided, the external base URL will default to `base_url`.

fields.name: extract_embed_urls
fields.label: Extract Embed Urls
fields.description: Produce URLs used to render Looker Explores as Previews inside of DataHub UI. Embeds must be enabled inside of Looker to use this feature.

fields.name: extract_independent_looks
fields.label: Extract Independent Looks
fields.description: Extract looks which are not part of any Dashboard. To enable this flag the stateful_ingestion should also be enabled.

fields.name: emit_used_explores_only
fields.label: Emit Used Explores Only
fields.description: When enabled, only explores that are used by a Dashboard/Look will be ingested.

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: git_info
fields.label: Git Info
fields.description: Reference to your git location. If present, supplies handy links to your lookml on the dataset entity page.

group_fields.name: repo
group_fields.label: Repo
group_fields.description: Name of your Git repo e.g. https://github.com/datahub-project/datahub or https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab. If organization/repo is provided, we assume it is a GitHub repo.

group_fields.name: branch
group_fields.label: Branch
group_fields.description: Branch on which your files live by default. Typically main or master. This can also be a commit hash.

group_fields.name: url_subdir
group_fields.label: Url Subdir
group_fields.description: Prefix to prepend when generating URLs for files - useful when files are in a subdirectory. Only affects URL generation, not git operations.

group_fields.name: url_template
group_fields.label: Url Template
group_fields.description: Template for generating a URL to a file in the repo e.g. '{repo_url}/blob/{branch}/{file_path}'. We can infer this for GitHub and GitLab repos, and it is otherwise required.It supports the following variables: {repo_url}, {branch}, {file_path}

group_fields.name: deploy_key_file
group_fields.label: Deploy Key File
group_fields.description: A private key file that contains an ssh key that has been configured as a deploy key for this repository. Use a file where possible, else see deploy_key for a config field that accepts a raw string. We expect the key not have a passphrase.

group_fields.name: deploy_key
group_fields.label: Deploy Key
group_fields.description: A private key that contains an ssh key that has been configured as a deploy key for this repository. See deploy_key_file if you want to use a file that contains this key.

group_fields.name: repo_ssh_locator
group_fields.label: Repo Ssh Locator
group_fields.description: The url to call `git clone` on. We infer this for github and gitlab repos, but it is required for other hosts.

group_fields.name: clone_timeout
group_fields.label: Clone Timeout
group_fields.description: Timeout in seconds for git clone operations. Set to None to disable the timeout.

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Looker API client id.

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Looker API client secret.

group_fields.name: base_url
group_fields.label: Base Url
group_fields.description: Url to your Looker instance: `https://company.looker.com:19999` or `https://looker.company.com`, or similar. Used for making API calls to Looker and constructing clickable dashboard and chart urls.

group_fields.name: transport_options
group_fields.label: Transport Options
group_fields.description: Populates the [TransportOptions](https://github.com/looker-open-source/sdk-codegen/blob/94d6047a0d52912ac082eb91616c1e7c379ab262/python/looker_sdk/rtl/transport.py#L70) struct for looker client

group_fields.name: max_retries
group_fields.label: Max Retries
group_fields.description: Number of retries for Looker API calls

group_fields.name: max_threads
group_fields.label: Max Threads
group_fields.description: Max parallelism for Looker API calls. Defaults to cpuCount or 40

fields.name: explore_naming_pattern
fields.label: Explore Naming Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing dataset names to explores. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name']

fields.name: explore_browse_pattern
fields.label: Explore Browse Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing browse paths to explores. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name']

fields.name: view_naming_pattern
fields.label: View Naming Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing dataset names to views. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name', 'file_path', 'folder_path']

fields.name: view_browse_pattern
fields.label: View Browse Pattern
fields.description: Pattern for providing browse paths to views. Allowed variables are ['platform', 'env', 'project', 'model', 'name', 'file_path', 'folder_path']

fields.name: model_pattern
fields.label: Model Pattern
fields.description: List of regex patterns for LookML models to include in the extraction.

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: List of regex patterns for LookML views to include in the extraction.

fields.name: extract_column_level_lineage
fields.label: Extract Column Level Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, extracts column-level lineage from Views and Explores

fields.name: use_api_for_view_lineage
fields.label: Use Api For View Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, uses Looker API to get SQL representation of views for lineage parsing instead of parsing LookML files directly. Requires 'api' configuration to be provided.Coverage of regex based lineage extraction has limitations, it only supportes ${TABLE}.column_name syntax, See (https://cloud.google.com/looker/docs/reference/param-field-sql#sql_for_dimensions) tounderstand the other substitutions and cross-references allowed in LookML.

fields.name: use_api_cache_for_view_lineage
fields.label: Use Api Cache For View Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, uses Looker API server-side caching for query execution. Requires 'api' configuration to be provided.

fields.name: allow_partial_lineage_results
fields.label: Allow Partial Lineage Results
fields.description: When enabled, allows partial lineage results to be returned even when some field chunks fail or when there are SQL parsing errors. This provides better resilience for large field sets and ensures some lineage information is available rather than complete failure.

fields.name: tag_measures_and_dimensions
fields.label: Tag Measures And Dimensions
fields.description: When enabled, attaches tags to measures, dimensions and dimension groups to make them more discoverable. When disabled, adds this information to the description of the column.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: base_folder
fields.label: Base Folder
fields.description: Required if not providing github configuration and deploy keys. A pointer to a local directory (accessible to the ingestion system) where the root of the LookML repo has been checked out (typically via a git clone). This is typically the root folder where the `*.model.lkml` and `*.view.lkml` files are stored. e.g. If you have checked out your LookML repo under `/Users/jdoe/workspace/my-lookml-repo`, then set `base_folder` to `/Users/jdoe/workspace/my-lookml-repo`.

fields.name: project_dependencies
fields.label: Project Dependencies
fields.description: A map of project_name to local directory (accessible to the ingestion system) or Git credentials. Every local_dependencies or private remote_dependency listed in the main project's manifest.lkml file should have a corresponding entry here. If a deploy key is not provided, the ingestion system will use the same deploy key as the main project. When providing a local directory path (string), the directory must exist at config validation time.

fields.name: connection_to_platform_map
fields.label: Connection To Platform Map
fields.description: A mapping of [Looker connection names](https://docs.looker.com/reference/model-params/connection-for-model) to DataHub platform, database, and schema values.

fields.name: parse_table_names_from_sql
fields.label: Parse Table Names From Sql
fields.description: See note below.

fields.name: project_name
fields.label: Project Name
fields.description: Required if you don't specify the `api` section. The project name within which all the model files live. See (https://docs.looker.com/data-modeling/getting-started/how-project-works) to understand what the Looker project name should be. The simplest way to see your projects is to click on `Develop` followed by `Manage LookML Projects` in the Looker application.

fields.name: transport_options
fields.label: Transport Options
fields.description: Populates the [TransportOptions](https://github.com/looker-open-source/sdk-codegen/blob/94d6047a0d52912ac082eb91616c1e7c379ab262/python/looker_sdk/rtl/transport.py#L70) struct for looker client

fields.name: max_file_snippet_length
fields.label: Max File Snippet Length
fields.description: When extracting the view definition from a lookml file, the maximum number of characters to extract.

fields.name: emit_reachable_views_only
fields.label: Emit Reachable Views Only
fields.description: When enabled, only views that are reachable from explores defined in the model files are emitted. If set to False, all views imported in model files are emitted. Views that are unreachable i.e. not explicitly defined in the model files are currently not emitted however reported as warning for debugging purposes.

fields.name: populate_sql_logic_for_missing_descriptions
fields.label: Populate Sql Logic For Missing Descriptions
fields.description: When enabled, field descriptions will include the sql logic for computed fields if descriptions are missing

fields.name: process_isolation_for_sql_parsing
fields.label: Process Isolation For Sql Parsing
fields.description: When enabled, sql parsing will be executed in a separate process to prevent memory leaks.

fields.name: process_refinements
fields.label: Process Refinements
fields.description: When enabled, looker refinement will be processed to adapt an existing view.

fields.name: liquid_variables
fields.label: Liquid Variables
fields.description: A dictionary containing Liquid variables with their corresponding values, utilized in SQL-defined derived views. The Liquid template will be resolved in view.derived_table.sql and view.sql_table_name. Defaults to an empty dictionary.

fields.name: lookml_constants
fields.label: Lookml Constants
fields.description: A dictionary containing LookML constants (`@{constant_name}`) and their values. If a constant is defined in the `manifest.lkml` file, its value will be used. If not found in the manifest, the value from this config will be used instead. Defaults to an empty dictionary.

fields.name: looker_environment
fields.label: Looker Environment
fields.description: A looker prod or dev environment. It helps to evaluate looker if comments i.e. -- if prod --. All if comments are evaluated to true for configured looker_environment value

fields.name: field_threshold_for_splitting
fields.label: Field Threshold For Splitting
fields.description: When the total number of fields returned by Looker API exceeds this threshold, the fields will be split into multiple API calls to avoid SQL parsing failures. This helps provide partial column and table lineage when dealing with large field sets.

fields.name: enable_individual_field_fallback
fields.label: Enable Individual Field Fallback
fields.description: When enabled, if a field chunk fails, the system will attempt to process each field individually to maximize information and isolate problematic fields. This helps identify which specific fields are causing issues while still getting lineage for working fields.

fields.name: max_workers_for_parallel_processing
fields.label: Max Workers For Parallel Processing
fields.description: Maximum number of worker threads to use for parallel processing of field chunks and individual fields. Set to 1 to process everything sequentially. Higher values can improve performance but may increase memory usage. Maximum allowed value is 100 to prevent resource exhaustion.

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: MySQL host URL.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: auth_mode
fields.label: Auth Mode
fields.description: Authentication mode to use for the MySQL connection. Options are 'PASSWORD' (default) for standard username/password authentication, or 'AWS_IAM' for AWS RDS IAM authentication.

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: AWS configuration for RDS IAM authentication (only used when auth_mode is AWS_IAM). Provides full control over AWS credentials, region, profiles, role assumption, retry logic, and proxy settings. If not explicitly configured, boto3 will automatically use the default credential chain and region from environment variables (AWS_DEFAULT_REGION, AWS_REGION), AWS config files (~/.aws/config), or IAM role metadata.

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: include_stored_procedures
fields.label: Include Stored Procedures
fields.description: Include ingest of stored procedures.

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for stored procedures to filter in ingestion.Specify regex to match the entire procedure name in database.schema.procedure_name format. e.g. to match all procedures starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistics and query-based lineage from query history. The source of that history is controlled by `usage_source`.

fields.name: usage_source
fields.label: Usage Source
fields.description: Where to read query history from. `performance_schema` (default) reads normalized digests from `events_statements_summary_by_digest` (no setup, no per-user attribution). Its `COUNT_STAR` is cumulative since the last counter reset (server restart or table truncation), so the first ingestion after enabling usage can report a large one-day spike attributing all history-to-date to a single timestamp. `general_log` reads literal statements with user and timestamp from `mysql.general_log` (requires `general_log=ON` and `log_output=TABLE`).

fields.name: usage
fields.label: Usage
fields.description: Usage statistics config. Only used when `include_usage_statistics` is enabled.

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

group_fields.name: top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

group_fields.name: user_email_pattern
group_fields.label: User Email Pattern
group_fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

group_fields.name: include_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

group_fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

group_fields.name: format_sql_queries
group_fields.label: Format Sql Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

group_fields.name: include_top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Include Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: email_domain
fields.label: Email Domain
fields.description: Email domain of your organisation, appended to `general_log` usernames (e.g. LDAP logins) so users display correctly. Ignored if the username already looks like an email. Only used with `usage_source: general_log`.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: api_config
fields.label: Api Config
fields.description: Matillion API configuration

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Matillion API Client ID for OAuth2 authentication.

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Matillion API Client Secret for OAuth2 authentication.

group_fields.name: region
group_fields.label: Region
group_fields.description: Matillion Data Productivity Cloud region (EU1, US1, or AU1)

group_fields.name: custom_base_url
group_fields.label: Custom Base Url
group_fields.description: Custom API base URL for VPC endpoints or on-premise installations.

group_fields.name: custom_oauth_token_url
group_fields.label: Custom Oauth Token Url
group_fields.description: Custom OAuth2 token endpoint URL for VPC endpoints or on-premise installations.

group_fields.name: request_timeout_sec
group_fields.label: Request Timeout Sec
group_fields.description: Per-request timeout in seconds. On timeouts, prefer narrowing start_time/end_time or enabling stateful ingestion over a large timeout.

fields.name: include_streaming_pipelines
fields.label: Include Streaming Pipelines
fields.description: Whether to ingest Matillion streaming pipelines (CDC pipelines). Streaming pipelines are emitted as separate DataFlows with pipeline_type='streaming'.

fields.name: streaming_pipeline_patterns
fields.label: Streaming Pipeline Patterns
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering Matillion streaming pipelines to ingest.

fields.name: pipeline_patterns
fields.label: Pipeline Patterns
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering Matillion pipelines to ingest.

fields.name: project_patterns
fields.label: Project Patterns
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering Matillion projects to ingest.

fields.name: environment_patterns
fields.label: Environment Patterns
fields.description: Regex patterns for filtering Matillion environments to ingest.

fields.name: include_unpublished_pipelines
fields.label: Include Unpublished Pipelines
fields.description: Whether to discover and ingest unpublished pipelines from recent execution history. When enabled, the connector will discover pipelines that have been executed but not yet published. Disable this to only ingest published pipelines from the published-pipelines API.

fields.name: include_dependent_pipelines
fields.label: Include Dependent Pipelines
fields.description: Whether to ingest dependent (child) pipelines that are only referenced via lineage events and were not discovered as project pipelines (published or via execution history). When disabled, lineage is still emitted for discovered pipelines, but these lineage-only dependencies are not created as their own DataFlows/DataJobs.

fields.name: include_external_urls
fields.label: Include External Urls
fields.description: Whether to emit links back to the Matillion console (project, run, and pipeline). Off by default because the pipeline link searches the observability dashboard by file name and only resolves for pipelines that ran recently and whose editor name matches their file name. Enable to add all Matillion external links.

fields.name: parse_sql_for_lineage
fields.label: Parse Sql For Lineage
fields.description: Whether to parse SQL from OpenLineage events to extract additional column-level lineage. Requires DataHub graph access. When enabled, SQL queries are parsed to infer lineage beyond what's explicitly provided in OpenLineage column mappings.

fields.name: lineage_platform_mapping
fields.label: Lineage Platform Mapping
