fields.description: Override platform name mappings from OpenLineage namespaces to DataHub platforms. Only needed for non-standard platforms. See documentation for list of pre-mapped platforms. Example: {"customdb": "postgres", "mywarehouse": "snowflake"}

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: max_executions_per_pipeline
fields.label: Max Executions Per Pipeline
fields.description: Maximum number of recent pipeline executions to ingest per pipeline. Set to 0 to disable execution ingestion.

fields.name: namespace_to_platform_instance
fields.label: Namespace To Platform Instance
fields.description: Maps OpenLineage namespace prefixes to platform instance/environment using longest prefix matching. Unmapped namespaces extract platform from URI with defaults (env=PROD). Example: {"snowflake://prod-account": {"platform_instance": "snowflake_prod", "env": "PROD"}}

fields.name: extract_projects_to_containers
fields.label: Extract Projects To Containers
fields.description: Whether to add the top-level Matillion project as a DataHub container. Environment and folder containers are always created; this only controls whether they are nested under a project container or hang at the root.

fields.name: extract_run_history
fields.label: Extract Run History
fields.description: Emit run history (DataProcessInstances) for published pipelines by fetching their executions in the time window. Implied when include_unpublished_pipelines is enabled.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by DataHub platform ingestion source belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to

fields.name: connect_uri
fields.label: Connect Uri
fields.description: Metabase host URL.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Metabase username, used when an API key is not provided.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Metabase password, used when an API key is not provided.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Api Key
fields.description: Metabase API key. If provided, the username and password will be ignored. Recommended method.

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform_instance mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: display_uri
fields.label: Display Uri
fields.description: optional URL to use in links (if `connect_uri` is only for ingestion)

fields.name: database_alias_map
fields.label: Database Alias Map
fields.description: Database name map to use when constructing dataset URN.

fields.name: engine_platform_map
fields.label: Engine Platform Map
fields.description: Custom mappings between metabase database engines and DataHub platforms

fields.name: database_id_to_instance_map
fields.label: Database Id To Instance Map
fields.description: Custom mappings between metabase database id and DataHub platform instance

fields.name: default_schema
fields.label: Default Schema
fields.description: Default schema name to use when schema is not provided in an SQL query

fields.name: exclude_other_user_collections
fields.label: Exclude Other User Collections
fields.description: Flag that if true, exclude other user collections

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Base Url
fields.description: MicroStrategy Library base URL, for example `https://your-company.example.com/MicroStrategyLibrary`.

fields.name: project_pattern
fields.label: Project Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter MicroStrategy projects by name.

fields.name: dashboard_pattern
fields.label: Dashboard Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter MicroStrategy dossiers/dashboards by name.

fields.name: report_pattern
fields.label: Report Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter MicroStrategy reports by name.

fields.name: folder_pattern
fields.label: Folder Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter folder containers by name. When an intermediate folder is denied, its children re-parent to the nearest allowed ancestor rather than being dropped.

fields.name: include_hidden
fields.label: Include Hidden
fields.description: Whether to include hidden MicroStrategy objects when APIs support it.

fields.name: extract_lineage
fields.label: Extract Lineage
fields.description: Whether to emit dataset-to-chart lineage when resolved from definitions.

fields.name: extract_model_lineage
fields.label: Extract Model Lineage
fields.description: Whether to attempt modeling/table API access needed for logical table and source warehouse lineage. If privileges are missing, the connector reports the failure and continues.

fields.name: extract_warehouse_lineage
fields.label: Extract Warehouse Lineage
fields.description: Whether to execute dashboard/dossier SQL-view APIs and emit upstream coarse table-level lineage from MicroStrategy datasets to source warehouse datasets parsed from SQL. Disabled by default because this is not field-level metric, attribute, or fact lineage. The connector discovers the warehouse platform from MicroStrategy datasource metadata and does not store raw SQL.

fields.name: extract_report_sql_lineage
fields.label: Extract Report Sql Lineage
fields.description: Whether to execute report SQL-view APIs and emit coarse table-level lineage from report source datasets to source warehouse datasets. Disabled by default for the same reason as `extract_warehouse_lineage`. Field-level model lineage for report source datasets also requires this flag, because model lineage only attaches to datasets with known warehouse upstreams.

fields.name: warehouse_lineage_sql_timeout_seconds
fields.label: Warehouse Lineage Sql Timeout Seconds
fields.description: HTTP timeout in seconds for SQL-view APIs. These calls can be slower than metadata definition APIs because MicroStrategy must create and resolve a dashboard instance.

fields.name: extract_usage_statistics
fields.label: Extract Usage Statistics
fields.description: Whether to extract dashboard and report usage statistics (view counts, unique users, per-user counts) by querying the MicroStrategy Platform Analytics telemetry cube. Requires the Platform Analytics project to be enabled on the environment (standard on MicroStrategy Cloud) and readable by the ingestion principal. Disabled by default.

fields.name: usage_lookback_days
fields.label: Usage Lookback Days
fields.description: How many days of usage history to request from the Platform Analytics cube. The shipped aggregate cube typically retains a 14-day rolling window, so larger values only help when the environment retains more history.

fields.name: usage_cube_name
fields.label: Usage Cube Name
fields.description: Name of the Platform Analytics cube to query for usage. The default is the aggregate telemetry cube shipped with Platform Analytics; a custom cube works as long as it exposes Date, Project, Object, and User attributes and an executions metric.

fields.name: usage_query_timeout_seconds
fields.label: Usage Query Timeout Seconds
fields.description: HTTP timeout in seconds for Platform Analytics cube query calls. Cube execution is server-side work and can be slower than metadata definition APIs.

fields.name: tag_measures_and_dimensions
fields.label: Tag Measures And Dimensions
fields.description: Tag metric fields as Measure, attribute fields as Dimension, and date/time attribute forms as Temporal.

fields.name: ingest_owner
fields.label: Ingest Owner
fields.description: Whether to map API owner fields to DataHub ownership aspects.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion config with stale entity removal support.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: auth
fields.label: Auth
fields.description: Authentication mode. Use `type: guest` for public demo-style access or `type: password` with username/password for authenticated tenants.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Whether to verify SSL certificates for MicroStrategy API calls.

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.label: Timeout Seconds
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds.

fields.name: max_retries
fields.label: Max Retries
fields.description: Maximum retry attempts for transient API failures.

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: Number of objects requested per paginated metadata search call.

fields.name: extract_dashboards
fields.label: Extract Dashboards
fields.description: Whether to extract dossiers/documents as DataHub dashboards.

fields.name: extract_charts
fields.label: Extract Charts
fields.description: Whether to extract visualizations as DataHub charts.

fields.name: extract_reports
fields.label: Extract Reports
fields.description: Whether to extract MicroStrategy reports as DataHub charts. Disabled by default because reports can be numerous and are independent from dossier visualization extraction.

fields.name: extract_report_definitions
fields.label: Extract Report Definitions
fields.description: Whether to fetch report definitions for source dataset, metric, and attribute details when `extract_reports` is enabled.

fields.name: extract_independent_reports
fields.label: Extract Independent Reports
fields.description: Whether to extract reports not referenced by any ingested dashboard. By default only dashboard-linked reports are ingested (`report_pattern` still applies), so scoping dashboards also scopes reports. The linkage comes from `extract_dashboard_dependencies`; when dashboards or dependencies are not extracted, all matching reports are ingested.

fields.name: extract_cubes
fields.label: Extract Cubes
fields.description: Whether to extract embedded dashboard datasets as DataHub datasets.

fields.name: extract_visualization_details
fields.label: Extract Visualization Details
fields.description: Whether to execute dashboards and fetch per-visualization runtime definitions to resolve dataset-to-visualization lineage when the static dashboard definition does not include dataset IDs.

fields.name: extract_source_warehouses
fields.label: Extract Source Warehouses
fields.description: Whether to call the MicroStrategy datasource management APIs to discover project source warehouse names, source types, database versions, DBMS names, and connection metadata.

fields.name: extract_dashboard_dependencies
fields.label: Extract Dashboard Dependencies
fields.description: Whether to call metadata search lineage APIs for direct dashboard components such as metrics, attributes, filters, and functions.

fields.name: extract_metric_expressions
fields.label: Extract Metric Expressions
fields.description: Whether to fetch metric model definitions with expression tokens and attach expression metadata to metric schema fields when the MicroStrategy principal has access.

fields.name: emit_dashboard_dataset_edges
fields.label: Emit Dashboard Dataset Edges
fields.description: Emit DashboardInfo.datasetEdges as a fallback. Disabled by default because BI dashboards with many datasets make lineage views noisy.

fields.name: platform_analytics_project_name
fields.label: Platform Analytics Project Name
fields.description: Name of the MicroStrategy project that hosts Platform Analytics telemetry. Only change this if the environment renamed the standard project.

fields.name: datasource_platform_mapping
fields.label: Datasource Platform Mapping
fields.description: Optional mapping from MicroStrategy datasource or connection name to the platform, platform instance, environment, and URN casing that warehouse was ingested under. MicroStrategy can hold several connections to the same warehouse platform (for example a prod and a dev Snowflake account), so keying by connection name lets each resolve to the right instance. Entries are matched against the datasource's connection name first, then its datasource name. Datasources with no entry auto-detect their platform and use this connector's `env`, no platform instance, and lowercase URNs.

fields.name: metric_glossary_term_mapping
fields.label: Metric Glossary Term Mapping
fields.description: Optional explicit mapping from MicroStrategy metric ID or name to DataHub glossary term URN.

fields.name: attribute_glossary_term_mapping
fields.label: Attribute Glossary Term Mapping
fields.description: Optional explicit mapping from MicroStrategy attribute/form ID or name to DataHub glossary term URN.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Username for MLflow authentication

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for MLflow authentication

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: tracking_uri
fields.label: Tracking Uri
fields.description: Tracking server URI. If not set, an MLflow default tracking_uri is used (local `mlruns/` directory or `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI` environment variable)

fields.name: registry_uri
fields.label: Registry Uri
fields.description: Registry server URI. If not set, an MLflow default registry_uri is used (value of tracking_uri or `MLFLOW_REGISTRY_URI` environment variable)

fields.name: model_name_separator
fields.label: Model Name Separator
fields.description: A string which separates model name from its version (e.g. model_1 or model-1)

fields.name: base_external_url
fields.label: Base External Url
fields.description: Base URL to use when constructing external URLs to MLflow. If not set, tracking_uri is used if it's an HTTP URL. If neither is set, external URLs are not generated.

fields.name: materialize_dataset_inputs
fields.label: Materialize Dataset Inputs
fields.description: Whether to materialize dataset inputs for each run

fields.name: source_mapping_to_platform
fields.label: Source Mapping To Platform
fields.description: Mapping of source type to datahub platform

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: connect_uri
fields.label: Connect Uri
fields.description: Mode host URL.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: When creating workspace API key this is the 'Secret'.

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.description: When creating workspace API key this is the 'Key ID'.

fields.name: workspace
fields.label: Workspace
fields.description: The Mode workspace username. If you navigate to Workspace Settings > Details, the url will be `https://app.mode.com/organizations/<workspace-username>`. This is distinct from the workspace's display name, and should be all lowercase.

fields.name: space_pattern
fields.label: Space Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for mode spaces to filter in ingestion (Spaces named as 'Personal' are filtered by default.) Specify regex to only match the space name. e.g. to only ingest space named analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: report_pattern
fields.label: Report Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for Mode reports to filter in ingestion. Matched against the report name. e.g. to exclude a report named 'slow_report', use deny=['slow_report'].

fields.name: ingest_embed_url
fields.label: Ingest Embed Url
fields.description: Whether to Ingest embed URL for Reports

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform_instance mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: owner_username_instead_of_email
fields.label: Owner Username Instead Of Email
fields.description: Use username for owner URN instead of Email

fields.name: tag_measures_and_dimensions
fields.label: Tag Measures And Dimensions
fields.description: Tag measures and dimensions in the schema

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: exclude_restricted
fields.label: Exclude Restricted
fields.description: Exclude restricted collections

fields.name: exclude_personal_collections
fields.label: Exclude Personal Collections
fields.description: Exclude personal collections from ingestion using Mode's server-side filter (?filter=custom). When True, only shared/custom collections are fetched from the API. When False, all collections are fetched (space_pattern still applies for client-side filtering).

fields.name: api_options
fields.label: Api Options
fields.description: Retry/Wait settings for Mode API to avoid "Too many Requests" error. See Mode API Options below

group_fields.name: retry_backoff_multiplier
group_fields.label: Retry Backoff Multiplier
group_fields.description: Multiplier for exponential backoff when waiting to retry

group_fields.name: max_retry_interval
group_fields.label: Max Retry Interval
group_fields.description: Maximum interval to wait when retrying

group_fields.name: max_attempts
group_fields.label: Max Attempts
group_fields.description: Maximum number of attempts to retry before failing

group_fields.name: timeout
group_fields.label: Timeout
group_fields.description: Timeout setting, how long to wait for the Mode rest api to send data before giving up

group_fields.name: requests_per_minute
group_fields.label: Requests Per Minute
group_fields.description: Maximum API requests per minute across all threads. Mode's API limit is ~240 req/min (4 req/s). Default of 180 leaves headroom to avoid 429 errors.

fields.name: exclude_archived
fields.label: Exclude Archived
fields.description: Exclude archived reports

fields.name: max_threads
fields.label: Max Threads
fields.description: Maximum number of threads to use for parallel API requests. Increase to speed up ingestion for large workspaces. Setting too high may trigger Mode API rate limiting (429 errors).

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: connect_uri
fields.label: Connect Uri
fields.description: MongoDB connection URI.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: MongoDB username.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: MongoDB password.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: collection_pattern
fields.label: Collection Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for collections to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: authMechanism
fields.label: Authmechanism
fields.description: MongoDB authentication mechanism. Supported values: DEFAULT, SCRAM-SHA-1, SCRAM-SHA-256, MONGODB-AWS, MONGODB-X509. When using MONGODB-AWS, credentials are resolved via boto3. See https://pymongo.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples/authentication.html

fields.name: enableSchemaInference
fields.label: Enableschemainference
fields.description: Whether to infer schemas.

fields.name: schemaSamplingSize
fields.label: Schemasamplingsize
fields.description: Number of documents to use when inferring schema size. If set to `null`, all documents will be scanned.

fields.name: useRandomSampling
fields.label: Userandomsampling
fields.description: If documents for schema inference should be randomly selected. If `False`, documents will be selected from start.

fields.name: maxSchemaSize
fields.label: Maxschemasize
fields.description: Maximum number of fields to include in the schema.

fields.name: hostingEnvironment
fields.label: Hostingenvironment
fields.description: Hosting environment of MongoDB, default is SELF_HOSTED, currently support `SELF_HOSTED`, `ATLAS`, `AWS_DOCUMENTDB`

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: Data platform to emit entities under. Use `documentdb` to surface AWS DocumentDB clusters as their own platform instead of `mongodb`. Requires `hostingEnvironment` to be `AWS_DOCUMENTDB`.

fields.name: excludeSystemCollections
fields.label: Excludesystemcollections
fields.description: Whether to exclude MongoDB system collections (those starting with 'system.') from ingestion. System collections such as system.profile and system.views are internal MongoDB collections that do not contain user data. Ingesting them produces noisy or incorrect metadata. Set to False only if you explicitly need to ingest system collections and have granted the appropriate database roles (dbAdmin for system.profile). Default: True.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Additional options to pass to `pymongo.MongoClient()`.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: MSSQL host URL.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog). If set to Null, all databases will be considered for ingestion.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: include_stored_procedures
fields.label: Include Stored Procedures
fields.description: Include ingest of stored procedures. Requires access to the 'sys' schema.

fields.name: include_stored_procedures_code
fields.label: Include Stored Procedures Code
fields.description: Include information about object code.

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for stored procedures to filter in ingestion.Specify regex to match the entire procedure name in database.schema.procedure_name format. e.g. to match all procedures starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_jobs
fields.label: Include Jobs
fields.description: Include ingest of MSSQL Jobs. Requires access to the 'msdb' and 'sys' schema.

fields.name: include_descriptions
fields.label: Include Descriptions
fields.description: Include table descriptions information.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: include_containers_for_pipelines
fields.label: Include Containers For Pipelines
fields.description: Enable the container aspects ingestion for both pipelines and tasks. Note that this feature requires the corresponding model support in the backend, which was introduced in version 0.15.0.1.

fields.name: temporary_tables_pattern
fields.label: Temporary Tables Pattern
fields.description: [Advanced] Regex patterns for temporary tables to filter in lineage ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. Defaults are to set in such a way to ignore the temporary staging tables created by known ETL tools.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_lineage
fields.label: Include Lineage
fields.description: Enable lineage extraction for stored procedures

fields.name: include_query_lineage
fields.label: Include Query Lineage
fields.description: Enable query-based lineage extraction from Query Store or DMVs. Query Store is preferred (SQL Server 2016+) and must be enabled on the database. Falls back to DMV-based extraction (sys.dm_exec_cached_plans) for older versions. Requires VIEW SERVER STATE permission. See documentation for setup instructions.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: include_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

fields.name: format_sql_queries
fields.label: Format Sql Queries
fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

fields.name: include_top_n_queries
fields.label: Include Top N Queries
fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: max_queries_to_extract
fields.label: Max Queries To Extract
fields.description: Maximum number of queries to extract for lineage analysis. Queries are prioritized by execution time and frequency.

fields.name: min_query_calls
fields.label: Min Query Calls
fields.description: Minimum number of executions required for a query to be included. Set higher to focus on frequently-used queries.

fields.name: query_exclude_patterns
fields.label: Query Exclude Patterns
fields.description: SQL LIKE patterns to exclude from query extraction. Example: ['%sys.%', '%temp_%'] to exclude system and temp tables.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistics from query history. Requires include_query_lineage to be enabled. Collects metrics like unique user counts, query frequencies, and column access patterns. Statistics appear in DataHub UI under the Dataset Profile > Usage tab.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Enable to convert the SQL Server assets urns to lowercase

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: uri_args
fields.label: Uri Args
fields.description: Arguments to URL-encode when connecting. See https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/connect/odbc/dsn-connection-string-attribute?view=sql-server-ver15.

fields.name: convert_column_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Column Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: When enabled, converts column URNs to lowercase to ensure cross-platform compatibility.

fields.name: quote_schemas
fields.label: Quote Schemas
fields.description: Represent a schema identifiers combined with quoting preferences. See [sqlalchemy quoted_name docs](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/sqlelement.html#sqlalchemy.sql.expression.quoted_name).

fields.name: is_aws_rds
fields.label: Is Aws Rds
fields.description: Indicates if the SQL Server instance is running on AWS RDS. When None (default), automatic detection will be attempted using server name analysis.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: MySQL host URL.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: auth_mode
fields.label: Auth Mode
fields.description: Authentication mode to use for the MySQL connection. Options are 'PASSWORD' (default) for standard username/password authentication, or 'AWS_IAM' for AWS RDS IAM authentication.

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: AWS configuration for RDS IAM authentication (only used when auth_mode is AWS_IAM). Provides full control over AWS credentials, region, profiles, role assumption, retry logic, and proxy settings. If not explicitly configured, boto3 will automatically use the default credential chain and region from environment variables (AWS_DEFAULT_REGION, AWS_REGION), AWS config files (~/.aws/config), or IAM role metadata.

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: include_stored_procedures
fields.label: Include Stored Procedures
fields.description: Include ingest of stored procedures.

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for stored procedures to filter in ingestion.Specify regex to match the entire procedure name in database.schema.procedure_name format. e.g. to match all procedures starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistics and query-based lineage from query history. The source of that history is controlled by `usage_source`.

fields.name: usage_source
fields.label: Usage Source
fields.description: Where to read query history from. `performance_schema` (default) reads normalized digests from `events_statements_summary_by_digest` (no setup, no per-user attribution). Its `COUNT_STAR` is cumulative since the last counter reset (server restart or table truncation), so the first ingestion after enabling usage can report a large one-day spike attributing all history-to-date to a single timestamp. `general_log` reads literal statements with user and timestamp from `mysql.general_log` (requires `general_log=ON` and `log_output=TABLE`).

fields.name: usage
fields.label: Usage
fields.description: Usage statistics config. Only used when `include_usage_statistics` is enabled.

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

group_fields.name: top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

group_fields.name: user_email_pattern
group_fields.label: User Email Pattern
group_fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

group_fields.name: include_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

group_fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

group_fields.name: format_sql_queries
group_fields.label: Format Sql Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

group_fields.name: include_top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Include Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: email_domain
fields.label: Email Domain
fields.description: Email domain of your organisation, appended to `general_log` usernames (e.g. LDAP logins) so users display correctly. Ignored if the username already looks like an email. Only used with `usage_source: general_log`.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: uri
fields.label: Uri
fields.description: The URI for the Neo4j server

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Neo4j Username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Neo4j Password

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Nifi username, must be set for auth = "SINGLE_USER"

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Nifi password, must be set for auth = "SINGLE_USER"

fields.name: site_url
fields.label: Site Url
fields.description: URL for Nifi, ending with /nifi/. e.g. https://mynifi.domain/nifi/

fields.name: client_key_password
fields.label: Client Key Password
fields.description: The password to decrypt the client_key_file

fields.name: process_group_pattern
fields.label: Process Group Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for filtering process groups

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits incremental/patch lineage for Nifi processors. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

fields.name: auth
fields.label: Auth
fields.description: Nifi authentication. must be one of : NO_AUTH, SINGLE_USER, CLIENT_CERT, KERBEROS

fields.name: provenance_days
fields.label: Provenance Days
fields.description: time window to analyze provenance events for external datasets

fields.name: site_name
fields.label: Site Name
fields.description: Site name to identify this site with, useful when using input and output ports receiving remote connections

fields.name: site_url_to_site_name
fields.label: Site Url To Site Name
fields.description: Lookup to find site_name for site_url ending with /nifi/, required if using remote process groups in nifi flow

fields.name: client_cert_file
fields.label: Client Cert File
fields.description: Path to PEM file containing the public certificates for the user/client identity, must be set for auth = "CLIENT_CERT"

fields.name: client_key_file
fields.label: Client Key File
fields.description: Path to PEM file containing the client’s secret key

fields.name: ca_file
fields.label: Ca File
fields.description: Path to PEM file containing certs for the root CA(s) for the NiFi.Set to False to disable SSL verification.

fields.name: emit_process_group_as_container
fields.label: Emit Process Group As Container
fields.description: Whether to emit Nifi process groups as container entities.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Api Key
fields.description: Notion internal integration token. Create one at https://www.notion.so/my-integrations

fields.name: datahub
fields.label: Datahub
fields.description: DataHub connection configuration (for querying server-side embedding config)

group_fields.name: server
group_fields.label: Server
group_fields.description: DataHub GMS server URL (defaults to DATAHUB_GMS_URL env var, ~/.datahubenv, or localhost:8080)

group_fields.name: token
group_fields.label: Token
group_fields.description: DataHub API token for authentication (defaults to DATAHUB_GMS_TOKEN env var or ~/.datahubenv)

fields.name: embedding
fields.label: Embedding
fields.description: Embedding generation configuration (Bedrock, Cohere, OpenAI, or Vertex AI)

group_fields.name: provider
group_fields.label: Provider
group_fields.description: Embedding provider. 'local' calls a locally-running OpenAI-compatible server (e.g. Ollama). 'vertex_ai' uses GCP. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: endpoint
group_fields.label: Endpoint
group_fields.description: Endpoint URL for local embedding server (e.g., http://localhost:11434/v1/embeddings). Only used when provider='local'. Falls back to LOCAL_EMBEDDING_ENDPOINT env var.

group_fields.name: model
group_fields.label: Model
group_fields.description: Model name. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: model_embedding_key
group_fields.label: Model Embedding Key
group_fields.description: Storage key for embeddings (e.g., 'cohere_embed_v3'). Required if overriding server config. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region for Bedrock. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: vertex_project_id
group_fields.label: Vertex Project Id
group_fields.description: GCP project ID for Vertex AI. Required when provider is 'vertex_ai' and using local config. If not set, loads from server.

group_fields.name: vertex_location
group_fields.label: Vertex Location
group_fields.description: GCP region for the Vertex AI endpoint (e.g., 'us-east1', 'us-central1'). Matches the Java server default.

group_fields.name: api_key
group_fields.label: Api Key
group_fields.description: API key for Cohere (not needed for Bedrock with IAM roles)

group_fields.name: batch_size
group_fields.label: Batch Size
group_fields.description: Batch size for embedding API calls

group_fields.name: request_timeout
group_fields.label: Request Timeout
group_fields.description: Per-call HTTP timeout in seconds for embedding providers.

group_fields.name: input_type
group_fields.label: Input Type
group_fields.description: Input type for Cohere embeddings

group_fields.name: rate_limit
group_fields.label: Rate Limit
group_fields.description: Enable rate limiting for embedding API calls.

group_fields.name: documents_per_minute
group_fields.label: Documents Per Minute
group_fields.description: Maximum number of documents to embed per minute when rate_limit is enabled.

group_fields.name: allow_local_embedding_config
group_fields.label: Allow Local Embedding Config
group_fields.description: BREAK-GLASS: Allow local config without server validation. NOT RECOMMENDED - may break semantic search.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
