fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: connection_type
fields.label: Connection Type
fields.description: Connection method: 'sql' for direct database access (MySQL/PostgreSQL), 'thrift' for HMS Thrift API with optional Kerberos support.

fields.name: views_where_clause_suffix
fields.label: Views Where Clause Suffix
fields.description: DEPRECATED: This option has been deprecated for security reasons and will be removed in a future release. Use 'database_pattern' instead.

fields.name: tables_where_clause_suffix
fields.label: Tables Where Clause Suffix
fields.description: DEPRECATED: This option has been deprecated for security reasons and will be removed in a future release. Use 'database_pattern' instead.

fields.name: schemas_where_clause_suffix
fields.label: Schemas Where Clause Suffix
fields.description: DEPRECATED: This option has been deprecated for security reasons and will be removed in a future release. Use 'database_pattern' instead.

fields.name: metastore_db_name
fields.label: Metastore Db Name
fields.description: Name of the Hive metastore's database (usually: metastore). For backward compatibility, if not provided, the database field will be used. If both 'database' and 'metastore_db_name' are set, 'database' is used for filtering.

fields.name: use_kerberos
fields.label: Use Kerberos
fields.description: Whether to use Kerberos/SASL authentication. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: kerberos_service_name
fields.label: Kerberos Service Name
fields.description: Kerberos service name for the HMS principal. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: kerberos_hostname_override
fields.label: Kerberos Hostname Override
fields.description: Override hostname for Kerberos principal construction. Use when connecting through a load balancer. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: kerberos_qop
fields.label: Kerberos Qop
fields.description: Kerberos Quality of Protection (QOP) for SASL authentication. Options: 'auth' (authentication only), 'auth-int' (authentication + integrity), 'auth-conf' (authentication + confidentiality/encryption). Must match the server's hadoop.rpc.protection setting. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.label: Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Connection timeout in seconds. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: catalog_name
fields.label: Catalog Name
fields.description: Catalog name for HMS 3.x multi-catalog deployments. Only for connection_type='thrift'.

fields.name: mode
fields.label: Mode
fields.description: Platform mode for metadata. Valid options: ['hive', 'presto', 'presto-on-hive', 'trino']

fields.name: use_catalog_subtype
fields.label: Use Catalog Subtype
fields.description: Use 'Catalog' (True) or 'Database' (False) as container subtype.

fields.name: use_dataset_pascalcase_subtype
fields.label: Use Dataset Pascalcase Subtype
fields.description: Use 'Table'/'View' (True) or 'table'/'view' (False) as dataset subtype.

fields.name: enable_properties_merge
fields.label: Enable Properties Merge
fields.description: Merge properties with existing server data instead of overwriting.

fields.name: simplify_nested_field_paths
fields.label: Simplify Nested Field Paths
fields.description: Simplify v2 field paths to v1. Falls back to v2 for Union/Array types.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: The application's client ID

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The application's client secret

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.description: The access token for the application. Mandatory for token based authentication.

fields.name: tenant_patterns
fields.label: Tenant Patterns
fields.description: List of regex patterns for tenants to include/exclude from ingestion. By default all tenants are allowed.

fields.name: namespace_patterns
fields.label: Namespace Patterns
fields.description: List of regex patterns for namespaces to include/exclude from ingestion. By default the functions namespace is denied.

fields.name: topic_patterns
fields.label: Topic Patterns
fields.description: List of regex patterns for topics to include/exclude from ingestion. By default the Pulsar system topics are denied.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: see Stateful Ingestion

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: web_service_url
fields.label: Web Service Url
fields.description: The web URL for the cluster.

fields.name: timeout
fields.label: Timeout
fields.description: Timout setting, how long to wait for the Pulsar rest api to send data before giving up

fields.name: issuer_url
fields.label: Issuer Url
fields.description: The complete URL for a Custom Authorization Server. Mandatory for OAuth based authentication.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Either a boolean, in which case it controls whether we verify the server's TLS certificate, or a string, in which case it must be a path to a CA bundle to use.

fields.name: exclude_individual_partitions
fields.label: Exclude Individual Partitions
fields.description: Extract each individual partitioned topic. e.g. when turned off a topic with 100 partitions will result in 100 Datasets.

fields.name: tenants
fields.label: Tenants
fields.description: Listing all tenants requires superUser role, alternative you can set a list of tenants you want to scrape using the tenant admin role

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Domain patterns

fields.name: oid_config
fields.label: Oid Config
fields.description: Placeholder for OpenId discovery document

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Api Key
fields.description: Qlik API Key

fields.name: tenant_hostname
fields.label: Tenant Hostname
fields.description: Qlik Tenant hostname

fields.name: space_pattern
fields.label: Space Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter Qlik spaces in ingestion.Mention 'personal_space' if entities of personal space need to ingest

fields.name: ingest_owner
fields.label: Ingest Owner
fields.description: Ingest Owner from source. This will override Owner info entered from UI

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

fields.name: data_connection_to_platform_instance
fields.label: Data Connection To Platform Instance
fields.description: A mapping of the Qlik app dataset upstream tables from data connection to platform instance.Use 'data_connection_name' as key.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: aws_access_key_id
fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_session_token
fields.label: Aws Session Token
fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

fields.name: aws_role
fields.label: Aws Role
fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

fields.name: aws_profile
fields.label: Aws Profile
fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: Aws Region
fields.description: AWS region code.

fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

fields.name: aws_proxy
fields.label: Aws Proxy
fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

fields.name: aws_retry_num
fields.label: Aws Retry Num
fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

fields.name: aws_retry_mode
fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
fields.description: Retry mode for failed AWS requests. Defaults to `adaptive` for QuickSight, whose per-API TPS throttling benefits from client-side rate limiting. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs.

fields.name: read_timeout
fields.label: Read Timeout
fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

fields.name: aws_advanced_config
fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: namespace_pattern
fields.label: Namespace Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter QuickSight namespaces (only relevant when `add_namespace_container` is enabled).

fields.name: folder_pattern
fields.label: Folder Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter QuickSight folders.

fields.name: dashboard_pattern
fields.label: Dashboard Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter QuickSight dashboards by name.

fields.name: analysis_pattern
fields.label: Analysis Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter QuickSight analyses by name.

fields.name: dataset_pattern
fields.label: Dataset Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter QuickSight datasets by name.

fields.name: data_source_pattern
fields.label: Data Source Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter QuickSight data sources by name. Data sources are not emitted as entities; denying one suppresses upstream lineage resolution for the datasets backed by it.

fields.name: extract_lineage
fields.label: Extract Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract upstream lineage from QuickSight Datasets to their backing warehouse/database tables.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract column-level lineage for CustomSql datasets via sqlglot. Requires `extract_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: extract_ownership
fields.label: Extract Ownership
fields.description: Whether to extract ownership from QuickSight resource permissions.

fields.name: extract_tags
fields.label: Extract Tags
fields.description: Whether to extract AWS resource tags on QuickSight assets.

fields.name: tag_pattern
fields.label: Tag Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter assets by their AWS resource tags (formatted as `key=value`).

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration (enables stale entity removal).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: aws_account_id
fields.label: Aws Account Id
fields.description: AWS account ID that owns the QuickSight assets. Auto-detected via `sts:GetCallerIdentity` when not provided.

fields.name: strip_user_ids_from_email
fields.label: Strip User Ids From Email
fields.description: When extracting ownership, strip the email domain from user identities so the CorpUser URN uses the bare username (e.g. `jane@acme.com` -> `jane`). Matches Looker's `strip_user_ids_from_email`. For IAM/SSO-federated principals the QuickSight identity is the role-session name (typically the email), which is used regardless of this flag.

fields.name: extract_dashboard_definitions
fields.label: Extract Dashboard Definitions
fields.description: Whether to fetch full dashboard definitions (sheets/visuals). These payloads are large; disabling this skips Chart entity emission.

fields.name: extract_analysis_definitions
fields.label: Extract Analysis Definitions
fields.description: Whether to fetch full analysis definitions (sheets/visuals). These payloads are large.

fields.name: extract_users_and_groups
fields.label: Extract Users And Groups
fields.description: Whether to extract QuickSight users and groups (opt-in; often noisy and requires additional permissions).

fields.name: add_shared_folders_container
fields.label: Add Shared Folders Container
fields.description: When enabled, a synthetic `Shared folders` container is emitted as the root of the folder hierarchy and every top-level QuickSight folder is nested beneath it — mirroring the `Shared folders` section in the QuickSight left-nav. (QuickSight's `Shared folders` is a UI category for SHARED-type folders, not a real folder, so it is only synthesized when this is on.) Off by default since we only ingest shared folders, making the level a constant prefix. Loose assets that belong to no folder are unaffected.

fields.name: add_namespace_container
fields.label: Add Namespace Container
fields.description: When enabled, QuickSight namespaces are emitted as containers and appear as a level in the browse hierarchy. Most accounts have only the single built-in `default` namespace, so this is off by default (assets sit directly under the platform / platform_instance, or under their folder). Enable it for Enterprise accounts that use multiple namespaces. Mirrors Tableau's `add_site_container`. Account separation is handled via `platform_instance`, not a container — matching the Glue / Redshift / PowerBI / Informatica convention.

fields.name: external_data_sources
fields.label: External Data Sources
fields.description: Per-data-source overrides for cross-platform lineage stitching, keyed by QuickSight `DataSourceId` (UUID, preferred) or display name (fallback). Provides the upstream platform_instance, env, URN casing, and SQL-parser default database/schema.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: source
fields.label: Source
fields.description: Primary: Web URL to .ttl or .zip file (e.g. https://example.org/glossary.ttl, https://example.org/data.zip). Also supports web folder URLs. Local file/folder paths work only when running via CLI. Examples: 'https://example.org/glossary.ttl', 'https://example.org/data.zip', 'https://example.org/folder/', '/path/to/file.ttl' (CLI-only)

fields.name: include_provisional
fields.label: Include Provisional
fields.description: Include terms with provisional/work-in-progress status (default: False). When False, only terms that have been fully approved/released are included. Many ontologies use workflow status properties (e.g., maturity level) to mark terms that are in the pipeline but not yet fully approved. Setting this to False helps reduce noise from unapproved or draft terms.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration. See https://datahubproject.io/docs/stateful-ingestion for more details.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: format
fields.label: Format
fields.description: RDF format (auto-detected if not specified). Examples: turtle, xml, n3, nt

fields.name: extensions
fields.label: Extensions
fields.description: File extensions to process when source is a folder

fields.name: recursive
fields.label: Recursive
fields.description: Enable recursive folder processing (default: true)

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: DataHub environment (PROD, DEV, TEST, etc.)

fields.name: parent_glossary_node
fields.label: Parent Glossary Node
fields.description: Optional parent Term Group (glossary node) to place the loaded hierarchy under. Use a name (e.g. 'ExternalOntologies') or full URN (urn:li:glossaryNode:ExternalOntologies). If a name is provided, the parent node is created if it does not exist. When omitted, terms are placed at the top level.

fields.name: dialect
fields.label: Dialect
fields.description: Force a specific RDF dialect (default: auto-detect). Options: default, fibo, generic

fields.name: export_only
fields.label: Export Only
fields.description: Export only specified entity types. Options are dynamically determined from registered entity types.

fields.name: skip_export
fields.label: Skip Export
fields.description: Skip exporting specified entity types. Options are dynamically determined from registered entity types.

fields.name: sparql_filter
fields.label: Sparql Filter
fields.description: Optional SPARQL CONSTRUCT query to filter the RDF graph before ingestion. Useful for filtering by namespace, module, or custom patterns. The query should use CONSTRUCT to build a filtered graph. Example: Filter to specific FIBO modules: 'CONSTRUCT { ?s ?p ?o } WHERE { ?s ?p ?o . FILTER(STRSTARTS(STR(?s), "https://spec.edmcouncil.org/fibo/ontology/FBC/")) }'

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: connect_uri
fields.label: Connect Uri
fields.description: Redash base URL.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Api Key
fields.description: Redash user API key.

fields.name: dashboard_patterns
fields.label: Dashboard Patterns
fields.description: regex patterns for dashboards to filter for ingestion.

fields.name: chart_patterns
fields.label: Chart Patterns
fields.description: regex patterns for charts to filter for ingestion.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful Ingestion Config

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

fields.name: skip_draft
fields.label: Skip Draft
fields.description: Only ingest published dashboards and charts.

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: Limit on number of items to be queried at once.

fields.name: api_page_limit
fields.label: Api Page Limit
fields.description: Limit on number of pages queried for ingesting dashboards and charts API during pagination.

fields.name: parallelism
fields.label: Parallelism
fields.description: Parallelism to use while processing.

fields.name: parse_table_names_from_sql
fields.label: Parse Table Names From Sql
fields.description: See note below.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: Environment to use in namespace when constructing URNs.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: Host and port where Redshift is running.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: s3_lineage_config
fields.label: S3 Lineage Config
fields.description: Common config for S3 lineage generation

group_fields.name: path_specs
group_fields.label: Path Specs
group_fields.description: List of PathSpec. See below the details about PathSpec

group_fields.name: strip_urls
group_fields.label: Strip Urls
group_fields.description: Strip filename from s3 url. It only applies if path_specs are not specified.

group_fields.name: ignore_non_path_spec_path
group_fields.label: Ignore Non Path Spec Path
group_fields.description: Ignore paths that are not match in path_specs. It only applies if path_specs are specified.

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform_instance mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: lineage_generate_queries
fields.label: Lineage Generate Queries
fields.description: Whether to generate queries entities for the SQL-based lineage collector.

fields.name: include_table_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Lineage
fields.description: Whether table lineage should be ingested.

fields.name: include_copy_lineage
fields.label: Include Copy Lineage
fields.description: Whether lineage should be collected from copy commands

fields.name: include_share_lineage
fields.label: Include Share Lineage
fields.description: Whether lineage should be collected from datashares

fields.name: include_unload_lineage
fields.label: Include Unload Lineage
fields.description: Whether lineage should be collected from unload commands

fields.name: include_table_rename_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Rename Lineage
fields.description: Whether we should follow `alter table ... rename to` statements when computing lineage.

fields.name: table_lineage_mode
fields.label: Table Lineage Mode
fields.description: Which table lineage collector mode to use. Available modes are: [stl_scan_based, sql_based, mixed]

fields.name: extract_column_level_lineage
fields.label: Extract Column Level Lineage
fields.description: Whether to extract column level lineage. This config works with rest-sink only.

fields.name: resolve_temp_table_in_lineage
fields.label: Resolve Temp Table In Lineage
fields.description: Whether to resolve temp table appear in lineage to upstream permanent tables.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: include_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

fields.name: format_sql_queries
fields.label: Format Sql Queries
fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

fields.name: include_top_n_queries
fields.label: Include Top N Queries
fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: email_domain
fields.label: Email Domain
fields.description: Email domain of your organisation so users can be displayed on UI appropriately.

fields.name: include_query_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Query Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate per-query popularity statistics (queryUsageStatistics) for the Query entities emitted by the SQL-based lineage collector. This is a sub-flag of include_usage_statistics: it only takes effect when include_usage_statistics is enabled, and is independent of include_column_usage_stats. Requires lineage_generate_queries (default True) so Query entities exist to attach stats to.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistic. email_domain config parameter needs to be set if enabled

fields.name: include_column_usage_stats
fields.label: Include Column Usage Stats
fields.description: Generate column-level usage statistics (`fieldCounts`) by parsing the SQL query text instead of attributing reads to the tables reported by Redshift's `stl_scan` system table. This is slower (every read query is parsed) but adds per-column usage. The full query text is reconstructed from `STL_QUERYTEXT` / `SYS_QUERY_TEXT` (the same source used for lineage), not the truncated `stl_query.querytxt`. Only applies when `include_usage_statistics` is enabled.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: extract_ownership
fields.label: Extract Ownership
fields.description: When enabled, extracts table and view owners from the Redshift catalog (pg_catalog.pg_user) and emits them as TECHNICAL_OWNER in DataHub. Ownership is applied using OVERWRITE mode, meaning any existing ownership information (including manually added or modified owners from the UI) will be replaced.

fields.name: enable_stateful_profiling
fields.label: Enable Stateful Profiling
fields.description: Enable stateful profiling. This will store profiling timestamps per dataset after successful profiling. and will not run profiling again in subsequent run if table has not been updated.

fields.name: enable_stateful_lineage_ingestion
fields.label: Enable Stateful Lineage Ingestion
fields.description: Enable stateful lineage ingestion. This will store lineage window timestamps after successful lineage ingestion. and will not run lineage ingestion for same timestamps in subsequent run. NOTE: This only works with use_queries_v2=False (legacy extraction path). For queries v2, use enable_stateful_time_window instead.

fields.name: enable_stateful_usage_ingestion
fields.label: Enable Stateful Usage Ingestion
fields.description: Enable stateful lineage ingestion. This will store usage window timestamps after successful usage ingestion. and will not run usage ingestion for same timestamps in subsequent run. NOTE: This only works with use_queries_v2=False (legacy extraction path). For queries v2, use enable_stateful_time_window instead.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: default_schema
fields.label: Default Schema
fields.description: The default schema to use if the SQL parser fails to parse the schema with the `sql_based` lineage collector. Set to `None` (or override at runtime) to leave unqualified table references without a schema qualifier in the resulting URN, instead of forcing them under the default schema.

fields.name: is_serverless
fields.label: Is Serverless
fields.description: Whether target Redshift instance is serverless (alternative is provisioned cluster)

fields.name: match_fully_qualified_names
fields.label: Match Fully Qualified Names
fields.description: Whether `schema_pattern` is matched against fully qualified schema name `<database>.<schema>`.

fields.name: patch_custom_properties
fields.label: Patch Custom Properties
fields.description: Whether to patch custom properties on existing datasets rather than replace.

fields.name: skip_external_tables
fields.label: Skip External Tables
fields.description: Whether to skip EXTERNAL tables.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: path_specs
fields.label: Path Specs
fields.description: List of PathSpec. See [below](#path-spec) the details about PathSpec

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: AWS configuration

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: profile_patterns
fields.label: Profile Patterns
fields.description: regex patterns for tables to profile

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Data profiling configuration

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

fields.name: use_s3_bucket_tags
fields.label: Use S3 Bucket Tags
fields.description: Whether or not to create tags in datahub from the s3 bucket

fields.name: use_s3_object_tags
fields.label: Use S3 Object Tags
fields.description: Whether or not to create tags in datahub from the s3 object

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: The platform that this source connects to (either 's3' or 'file'). If not specified, the platform will be inferred from the path_specs.

fields.name: use_s3_content_type
fields.label: Use S3 Content Type
fields.description: If enabled, use S3 Object metadata to determine content type over file extension, if set. Warning: this requires a separate query to S3 for each object, which can be slow for large datasets.

fields.name: spark_driver_memory
fields.label: Spark Driver Memory
fields.description: Max amount of memory to grant Spark.

fields.name: spark_config
fields.label: Spark Config
fields.description: Spark configuration properties to set on the SparkSession. Put config property names into quotes. For example: '"spark.executor.memory": "2g"'

fields.name: max_rows
fields.label: Max Rows
fields.description: Maximum number of rows to use when inferring schemas for TSV and CSV files.

fields.name: add_partition_columns_to_schema
fields.label: Add Partition Columns To Schema
fields.description: Whether to add partition fields to the schema.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify Ssl
fields.description: Either a boolean, in which case it controls whether we verify the server's TLS certificate, or a string, in which case it must be a path to a CA bundle to use.

fields.name: number_of_files_to_sample
fields.label: Number Of Files To Sample
fields.description: Number of files to list to sample for schema inference. This will be ignored if sample_files is set to False in the pathspec.

fields.name: sort_schema_fields
fields.label: Sort Schema Fields
fields.description: Whether to sort schema fields by fieldPath when inferring schemas.

fields.name: generate_partition_aspects
fields.label: Generate Partition Aspects
fields.description: Whether to generate partition aspects for partitioned tables. On older servers for backward compatibility, this should be set to False. This flag will be removed in future versions.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Client ID for the OAuth authentication

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Client secret for the OAuth authentication

fields.name: tenant_url
fields.label: Tenant Url
fields.description: URL of the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant

fields.name: token_url
fields.label: Token Url
fields.description: URL of the OAuth token endpoint of the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant

fields.name: ingest_stories
fields.label: Ingest Stories
fields.description: Controls whether Stories should be ingested

fields.name: ingest_applications
fields.label: Ingest Applications
fields.description: Controls whether Analytic Applications should be ingested

fields.name: ingest_import_data_model_schema_metadata
fields.label: Ingest Import Data Model Schema Metadata
fields.description: Controls whether schema metadata of Import Data Models should be ingested (ingesting schema metadata of Import Data Models significantly increases overall ingestion time)

fields.name: resource_id_pattern
fields.label: Resource Id Pattern
fields.description: Patterns for selecting resource ids that are to be included

fields.name: resource_name_pattern
fields.label: Resource Name Pattern
fields.description: Patterns for selecting resource names that are to be included

fields.name: folder_pattern
fields.label: Folder Pattern
fields.description: Patterns for selecting folders that are to be included

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: query_name_template
fields.label: Query Name Template
fields.description: Template for generating dataset urns of consumed queries, the placeholder {query} can be used within the template for inserting the name of the query

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion related configs

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: connection_mapping

fields.name: ingest_data_models
fields.label: Ingest Data Models
fields.description: Whether to ingest Sigma Data Models. Each Data Model is emitted as a Container with one Dataset per element inside it (plus per-element ``SchemaMetadata`` and, when ``extract_lineage`` is also enabled, ``UpstreamLineage``). Enabling this issues ``/dataModels/{id}/elements`` and ``/columns`` calls per Data Model unconditionally; the ``/lineage`` call is only issued when ``extract_lineage`` is also ``True`` (so users who opt out of lineage at the workbook surface don't get a lineage endpoint hit under a different flag).

fields.description: Whether to extract lineage of workbook's elements and datasets or not.

fields.description: Regex patterns to filter workbook's elements and datasets lineage in ingestion.Requires extract_lineage to be enabled.

fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Sigma Stateful Ingestion Config.

group_fields.name: enabled

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: api_url
fields.label: Api Url
fields.description: Sigma API hosted URL.

fields.name: chart_sources_platform_mapping
fields.label: Chart Sources Platform Mapping
fields.description: A mapping of the sigma workspace/workbook/chart folder path to all chart's data sources platform details present inside that folder path.

fields.name: connection_to_platform_map
fields.label: Connection To Platform Map
fields.description: Per-connection env / platform_instance overrides for warehouse URN construction from DM element lineage. Keys are Sigma connectionIds (visible in the Sigma admin UI or /v2/connections response). When a connection is not listed, the Sigma source's own env is used and platform_instance defaults to None, which is correct for single-env, single-instance deployments. For multi-env or multi-instance warehouse setups, add an entry here so the emitted UpstreamLineage edge points at the URN the warehouse connector actually produced.

fields.name: max_personal_dm_discovery_rounds
fields.label: Max Personal Dm Discovery Rounds
fields.description: Belt-and-braces safety cap on the number of passes the personal-space Data Model discovery loop is allowed to make. Each pass fetches ``/v2/dataModels/{urlId}`` for every newly-seen cross-DM ``<urlId>`` prefix; the loop terminates naturally when ``unresolved_seen`` plateaus (monotonically growing set), so under a well-behaved API this cap is never hit. Must be ``>= 1`` -- set ``ingest_shared_entities: False`` (or leave it at the default) if the goal is to disable personal-space discovery entirely; ``0`` / negative values are rejected because the first pass is required to prepopulate the bridge maps for listed DMs. Exists to protect against pathological Sigma payloads (e.g. a chain of personal-space DMs that keep referencing newly-discovered personal-space DMs) by breaking with a ``SourceReport.warning`` instead of looping unbounded.

Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'
