fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: stream_pattern
fields.label: Stream Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for streams to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for procedures to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire procedure name in database.schema.procedure format. e.g. to match all procedures starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: streamlit_pattern
fields.label: Streamlit Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for Streamlit app to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire Streamlit app name in database.schema.streamlit format. e.g. to match all Streamlit apps starting with dashboard in Analytics database and public schema, use the regex 'Analytics.public.dashboard.*'

fields.name: semantic_view_pattern
fields.label: Semantic View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for semantic views to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire semantic view name in database.schema.semantic_view format. e.g. to match all semantic views starting with sales in Analytics database and public schema, use the regex 'Analytics.public.sales.*'

fields.name: stage_pattern
fields.label: Stage Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for stages to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire stage name in database.schema.stage format.

fields.name: task_pattern
fields.label: Task Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tasks to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire task name in database.schema.task format.

fields.name: pipe_pattern
fields.label: Pipe Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for pipes to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire pipe name in database.schema.pipe format.

fields.name: match_fully_qualified_names
fields.label: Match Fully Qualified Names
fields.description: Whether `schema_pattern` is matched against fully qualified schema name `<catalog>.<schema>`.

fields.name: push_down_metadata_patterns
fields.label: Push Down Metadata Patterns
fields.description: If enabled, pushes down database_pattern, schema_pattern, table_pattern, and view_pattern filtering to Snowflake information_schema metadata queries using the RLIKE operator for improved performance. This applies only to metadata extraction queries (information_schema.databases, schemata, tables, views) — NOT to lineage/usage queries (for those, see push_down_database_pattern_access_history). NOTE: view_pattern pushdown only works when fetch_views_from_information_schema is also enabled. With the default SHOW VIEWS, view_pattern filtering falls back to Python re.match(). IMPORTANT: Snowflake RLIKE requires FULL STRING match, unlike Python re.match() which matches prefixes. For prefix matching use 'PATTERN.*', for suffix use '.*PATTERN$', for contains use '.*PATTERN.*'. If the composed filter would exceed Snowflake's per-query size limit, that filter is automatically skipped and applied client-side instead (slower). See the [Metadata Pattern Pushdown](#metadata-pattern-pushdown) section for detailed usage and examples, and the [Snowflake RLIKE documentation](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/rlike) for regex syntax details.

fields.name: include_view_definitions
fields.label: Include View Definitions
fields.description: If enabled, populates the ingested views' definitions.

fields.name: include_technical_schema
fields.label: Include Technical Schema
fields.description: If enabled, populates the snowflake technical schema and descriptions.

fields.name: include_primary_keys
fields.label: Include Primary Keys
fields.description: If enabled, populates the snowflake primary keys.

fields.name: include_foreign_keys
fields.label: Include Foreign Keys
fields.description: If enabled, populates the snowflake foreign keys.

fields.name: include_external_url
fields.label: Include External Url
fields.description: Whether to populate Snowsight url for Snowflake Objects

fields.name: include_streams
fields.label: Include Streams
fields.description: If enabled, streams will be ingested as separate entities from tables/views.

fields.name: include_procedures
fields.label: Include Procedures
fields.description: If enabled, procedures will be ingested as pipelines/tasks.

fields.name: include_streamlits
fields.label: Include Streamlits
fields.description: If enabled, Streamlit apps will be ingested as dashboards.

fields.name: include_tasks
fields.label: Include Tasks
fields.description: If enabled, Snowflake Tasks will be ingested as DataJobs with DAG dependencies and SQL lineage.

fields.name: include_pipes
fields.label: Include Pipes
fields.description: If enabled, Snowflake Snowpipe objects will be ingested as DataJobs with COPY INTO lineage.

fields.name: temporary_tables_pattern
fields.label: Temporary Tables Pattern
fields.description: [Advanced] Regex patterns for temporary tables to filter in lineage ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. Defaults are to set in such a way to ignore the temporary staging tables created by known ETL tools.

fields.name: include_assertion_results
fields.label: Include Assertion Results
fields.description: Whether to ingest assertion run results for assertions [created using DataHub assertions CLI](/docs/assertions/snowflake/snowflake_dmfs) in Snowflake. Also required for external DMF ingestion.

fields.name: include_externally_managed_dmfs
fields.label: Include Externally Managed Dmfs
fields.description: Ingest user-created Snowflake DMFs (not created via DataHub) as external assertions. Requires `include_assertion_results: true`. When enabled, all DMFs (not just datahub__* prefixed) will be ingested with their execution results. IMPORTANT: External DMFs must return 1 for SUCCESS and 0 for FAILURE. DataHub interprets VALUE=1 as passed, VALUE=0 as failed. See [Snowflake DMF Assertions](/docs/assertions/snowflake/snowflake_dmfs) for details.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_table_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Lineage
fields.description: If enabled, populates the snowflake table-to-table and s3-to-snowflake table lineage. Requires appropriate grants given to the role and Snowflake Enterprise Edition or above.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates table->table and view->table column lineage. Requires appropriate grants given to the role and the Snowflake Enterprise Edition or above.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: include_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

fields.name: format_sql_queries
fields.label: Format Sql Queries
fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

fields.name: include_top_n_queries
fields.label: Include Top N Queries
fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: apply_view_usage_to_tables
fields.label: Apply View Usage To Tables
fields.description: Whether to apply view's usage to its base tables. If set to True, usage is applied to base tables only.

fields.name: include_usage_stats
fields.label: Include Usage Stats
fields.description: If enabled, populates the snowflake usage statistics. Requires appropriate grants given to the role.

fields.name: use_queries_v2
fields.label: Use Queries V2
fields.description: If enabled, uses the new queries extractor to extract queries from snowflake.

fields.name: include_queries
fields.label: Include Queries
fields.description: If enabled, generate query entities associated with lineage edges. Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled.

fields.name: include_query_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Query Usage Statistics
fields.description: If enabled, generate query popularity statistics. Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled.

fields.name: query_dedup_strategy
fields.label: Query Dedup Strategy
fields.description: Experimental: Choose the strategy for query deduplication (default value is appropriate for most use-cases; make sure you understand performance implications before changing it). Allowed values are: STANDARD, NONE

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: extract_tags
fields.label: Extract Tags
fields.description: Optional. Allowed values are `without_lineage`, `with_lineage`, and `skip` (default). `without_lineage` only extracts tags that have been applied directly to the given entity. `with_lineage` extracts both directly applied and propagated tags, but will be significantly slower. See the [Snowflake documentation](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/object-tagging.html#tag-lineage) for information about tag lineage/propagation.

fields.name: extract_tags_as_structured_properties
fields.label: Extract Tags As Structured Properties
fields.description: If enabled along with `extract_tags`, extracts snowflake's key-value tags as DataHub structured properties instead of DataHub tags.

fields.name: structured_properties_write_mode
fields.label: Structured Properties Write Mode
fields.description: How to write structured properties extracted from Snowflake tags. `upsert` (default) replaces the aspect each run — recipe is source of truth. `patch` adds each property individually so user/UI edits survive, but properties removed from the recipe no longer propagate to DataHub (clean those up via the UI or API).

fields.name: tag_pattern
fields.label: Tag Pattern
fields.description: List of regex patterns for tags to include in ingestion. Only used if `extract_tags` is enabled.

fields.name: include_stages
fields.label: Include Stages
fields.description: If enabled, Snowflake Stages will be ingested as containers with associated metadata.

fields.name: structured_property_pattern
fields.label: Structured Property Pattern
fields.description: List of regex patterns for structured properties to include in ingestion. Applied to tags with form `<database>.<schema>.<tag_name>`. Only used if `extract_tags` and `extract_tags_as_structured_properties` are enabled.

fields.name: enable_stateful_profiling
fields.label: Enable Stateful Profiling
fields.description: Enable stateful profiling. This will store profiling timestamps per dataset after successful profiling. and will not run profiling again in subsequent run if table has not been updated.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: enable_stateful_time_window
fields.label: Enable Stateful Time Window
fields.description: Enable stateful time window tracking. This will store the time window after successful extraction and adjust the time window in subsequent runs to avoid reprocessing. NOTE: This is ONLY applicable when using queries v2 (use_queries_v2=True). This replaces enable_stateful_lineage_ingestion and enable_stateful_usage_ingestion for the queries v2 extraction path, since queries v2 extracts lineage, usage, operations, and queries together from a single audit log and uses a unified time window.

fields.name: enable_stateful_usage_ingestion
fields.label: Enable Stateful Usage Ingestion
fields.description: Enable stateful lineage ingestion. This will store usage window timestamps after successful usage ingestion. and will not run usage ingestion for same timestamps in subsequent run. NOTE: This only works with use_queries_v2=False (legacy extraction path). For queries v2, use enable_stateful_time_window instead.

fields.name: enable_stateful_lineage_ingestion
fields.label: Enable Stateful Lineage Ingestion
fields.description: Enable stateful lineage ingestion. This will store lineage window timestamps after successful lineage ingestion. and will not run lineage ingestion for same timestamps in subsequent run. NOTE: This only works with use_queries_v2=False (legacy extraction path). For queries v2, use enable_stateful_time_window instead.

fields.name: incremental_properties
fields.label: Incremental Properties
fields.description: When enabled, emits dataset properties as incremental to existing dataset properties in DataHub. When disabled, re-states dataset properties on each run.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: email_domain
fields.label: Email Domain
fields.description: Email domain of your organization so users can be displayed on UI appropriately. This is used only if we cannot infer email ID.

fields.name: fetch_views_from_information_schema
fields.label: Fetch Views From Information Schema
fields.description: If enabled, uses information_schema.views to fetch view definitions instead of SHOW VIEWS command. This alternative method can be more reliable for databases with large numbers of views (> 10K views), as the SHOW VIEWS approach has proven unreliable and can lead to missing views in such scenarios. However, this method requires OWNERSHIP privileges on views to retrieve their definitions. For views without ownership permissions (where VIEW_DEFINITION is null/empty), the system will automatically fall back to using batched SHOW VIEWS queries to populate the missing definitions.

fields.name: lazy_schema_resolver
fields.label: Lazy Schema Resolver
fields.description: If enabled, uses lazy schema resolver to resolve schemas for tables and views. This is useful if you have a large number of schemas and want to avoid bulk fetching the schema for each table/view.

fields.name: snowsight_base_url
fields.label: Snowsight Base Url
fields.description: Override for the Snowsight base URL used when generating external URLs for Snowflake assets. Set this when Snowsight is only reachable via private link (for example `https://app.<region>.privatelink.snowflakecomputing.com/` or `https://app-<org>-<account>.privatelink.snowflakecomputing.com/`). If unset, defaults to the public `app.snowflake.com` URL. The value can be obtained by running `SELECT SYSTEM$GET_PRIVATELINK_CONFIG()` in Snowflake as ACCOUNTADMIN.

fields.name: validate_upstreams_against_patterns
fields.label: Validate Upstreams Against Patterns
fields.description: Whether to validate upstream snowflake tables against allow-deny patterns

fields.name: table_types
fields.label: Table Types
fields.description: Set of Snowflake TABLE_TYPE values to include in ingestion. Currently Supported values: 'BASE TABLE', 'EXTERNAL TABLE'. Remove 'EXTERNAL TABLE' to exclude external tables from ingestion.

fields.name: exclude_dynamic_tables
fields.label: Exclude Dynamic Tables
fields.description: If enabled, dynamic tables will be excluded from ingestion. Use this to speed up ingestion if you don't need dynamic tables in DataHub.

fields.name: semantic_views
fields.label: Semantic Views
fields.description: Configuration for semantic views ingestion.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: If enabled, semantic views will be ingested as datasets. Note: Semantic views require Snowflake Enterprise Edition or above, as they are part of the Cortex Analyst feature set. Set this to True only if you have Enterprise Edition or above.

group_fields.name: column_lineage
group_fields.label: Column Lineage
group_fields.description: If enabled, column-level lineage will be generated for semantic views, mapping dimensions, facts, and metrics to their source columns in base tables. Only applicable when enabled is True.

group_fields.name: include_usage
group_fields.label: Include Usage
group_fields.description: If enabled, usage statistics will be extracted for semantic views. This scans QUERY_HISTORY which can be slow on accounts with high query volume.

group_fields.name: include_queries
group_fields.label: Include Queries
group_fields.description: If enabled, generate query entities for queries against semantic views.

group_fields.name: max_queries_per_view
group_fields.label: Max Queries Per View
group_fields.description: Maximum number of query entities to emit per semantic view. Only applicable when include_queries is True.

fields.name: marketplace
fields.label: Marketplace
fields.description: Configuration for Snowflake Internal Marketplace (private data exchange) ingestion.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest Snowflake INTERNAL marketplace (private data exchange) listings as Data Products. When enabled, also ingests databases and usage statistics based on the marketplace_mode setting. NOTE: This is for INTERNAL marketplace only (IS_ORGANIZATION = TRUE), not the public Snowflake Data Marketplace.

group_fields.name: marketplace_mode
group_fields.label: Marketplace Mode
group_fields.description: Mode for marketplace ingestion: 'consumer' (default) - Track purchased/imported databases (IMPORTED DATABASE type), 'provider' - Track databases you're sharing via OUTBOUND shares and marketplace listings, 'both' - Track both consumer and provider perspectives. Consumer mode requires shares config to link imported databases to listings. Provider mode works with OUTBOUND shares without requiring imported databases. IMPORTANT: For 'provider' or 'both' modes, you MUST grant 'imported privileges on database snowflake' to the USER (not just the role), as share access is granted at the user level in Snowflake.

group_fields.name: listing_to_share_overrides
group_fields.label: Listing To Share Overrides
group_fields.description: Map of `listing_global_name` -> share name (top-level key in `shares`) to explicitly link a marketplace listing to a share. Useful when `SHOW SHARES` doesn't return `listing_global_name` or when automatic name-based matching fails.

group_fields.name: listing_to_schemas_overrides
group_fields.label: Listing To Schemas Overrides
group_fields.description: Map of `listing_global_name` -> list of schema names to enumerate when falling back to database-level asset discovery. Used in provider mode when `DESC SHARE` is not permitted (Snowflake requires share ownership for that command). Without this override the fallback enumerates all schemas in the source database, which may include schemas not exposed by the share. Example: `{GZSTZGQTPEW: [TPCH]}`.

group_fields.name: internal_marketplace_listing_pattern
group_fields.label: Internal Marketplace Listing Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns for INTERNAL marketplace listings to include in ingestion

group_fields.name: internal_marketplace_owner_patterns
group_fields.label: Internal Marketplace Owner Patterns
group_fields.description: Map regex patterns (matched against INTERNAL listing title or provider) to owner identifiers. Owners can be usernames, group names, or full URNs. Example: {'^Finance.*': ['finance-team'], '^.*Analytics.*': ['analytics-lead', 'urn:li:corpGroup:data']}

group_fields.name: fetch_internal_marketplace_listing_details
group_fields.label: Fetch Internal Marketplace Listing Details
group_fields.description: If enabled, fetches additional details for each INTERNAL marketplace listing via DESCRIBE AVAILABLE LISTING. WARNING: This executes one additional query per listing and may impact performance for many listings.

group_fields.name: marketplace_properties_as_structured_properties
group_fields.label: Marketplace Properties As Structured Properties
group_fields.description: If enabled, ingests INTERNAL marketplace custom properties (provider, category, listing_created_on, etc.) as DataHub structured properties instead of simple custom properties. This makes marketplace metadata searchable and filterable in the DataHub UI.

group_fields.name: organization_to_domain
group_fields.label: Organization To Domain
group_fields.description: Map of Snowflake ``ORGANIZATION_PROFILE_NAME`` to an existing DataHub domain (URN, GUID, or name resolvable via ``DomainRegistry``). Unmapped organizations get no domain; marketplace never auto-creates domain entities.

fields.name: shares
fields.label: Shares
fields.description: Required if current account owns or consumes snowflake share.If specified, connector creates lineage and siblings relationship between current account's database tables and consumer/producer account's database tables. Map of share name -> details of share.

fields.name: known_snowflake_edition
fields.label: Known Snowflake Edition
fields.description: Explicitly specify the Snowflake edition (STANDARD or ENTERPRISE). If unset, the edition will be inferred automatically using 'SHOW TAGS'.

fields.name: pushdown_deny_usernames
fields.label: Pushdown Deny Usernames
fields.description: List of snowflake usernames (SQL LIKE patterns, e.g., 'SERVICE_%', '%_PROD', 'TEST_USER') which will NOT be considered for lineage/usage/queries extraction. This is primarily useful for improving performance by filtering out users with extremely high query volumes. Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled.

fields.name: pushdown_allow_usernames
fields.label: Pushdown Allow Usernames
fields.description: List of snowflake usernames (SQL LIKE patterns, e.g., 'ANALYST_%', '%_USER', 'MAIN_ACCOUNT') which WILL be considered for lineage/usage/queries extraction. This is primarily useful for improving performance by filtering in only specific users. Only applicable if `use_queries_v2` is enabled. If not specified, all users not in deny list are included.

fields.name: push_down_database_pattern_access_history
fields.label: Push Down Database Pattern Access History
fields.description: If enabled, pushes down database pattern filtering to the access_history table for improved performance. This filters on the accessed objects in access_history.

fields.name: additional_database_names_allowlist
fields.label: Additional Database Names Allowlist
fields.description: Additional database names (no pattern matching) to be included in the access_history filter. Only applies if push_down_database_pattern_access_history=True. These databases will be included in the filter being pushed down regardless of database_pattern settings.This may be required in the case of _eg_ temporary tables being created in a different database than the ones in the database_name patterns.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Snowflake username.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Snowflake password.

fields.name: private_key
fields.label: Private Key
fields.description: Private key in a form of '<PEM block>\n' if using key pair authentication. Encrypted version of private key will be in a form of '<PEM block>\n' See: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth.html

fields.name: private_key_path
fields.label: Private Key Path
fields.description: The path to the private key if using key pair authentication. Ignored if `private_key` is set. See: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth.html

fields.name: private_key_password
fields.label: Private Key Password
fields.description: Password for your private key. Required if using key pair authentication with encrypted private key.

fields.name: oauth_config
fields.label: OAuth
fields.description: oauth configuration - https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/python-connector-example.html#connecting-with-oauth

fields.name: authentication_type
fields.label: Authentication Type
fields.description: The type of authenticator to use when connecting to Snowflake. Supports "DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATOR", "OAUTH_AUTHENTICATOR", "EXTERNAL_BROWSER_AUTHENTICATOR" and "KEY_PAIR_AUTHENTICATOR".

fields.name: account_id
fields.label: Account Id
fields.description: Snowflake account identifier. e.g. xy12345, xy12345.us-east-2.aws, xy12345.us-central1.gcp, xy12345.central-us.azure, xy12345.us-west-2.privatelink. Refer [Account Identifiers](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-account-identifier.html#format-2-legacy-account-locator-in-a-region) for more details.

fields.name: warehouse
fields.label: Warehouse
fields.description: Snowflake warehouse.

fields.name: role
fields.label: Role
fields.description: Snowflake role.

fields.name: connect_args
fields.label: Connect Args
fields.description: Connect args to pass to Snowflake SqlAlchemy driver

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.description: OAuth token from external identity provider. Not recommended for most use cases because it will not be able to refresh once expired.

fields.name: snowflake_domain
fields.label: Snowflake Domain
fields.description: Snowflake domain. Use 'snowflakecomputing.com' for most regions or 'snowflakecomputing.cn' for China (cn-northwest-1) region.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Will match against the full `database.schema` name if `match_fully_qualified_names` is enabled.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: stream_pattern
fields.label: Stream Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for streams to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: procedure_pattern
fields.label: Procedure Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for procedures to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire procedure name in database.schema.procedure format. e.g. to match all procedures starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: streamlit_pattern
fields.label: Streamlit Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for Streamlit app to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire Streamlit app name in database.schema.streamlit format. e.g. to match all Streamlit apps starting with dashboard in Analytics database and public schema, use the regex 'Analytics.public.dashboard.*'

fields.name: semantic_view_pattern
fields.label: Semantic View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for semantic views to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire semantic view name in database.schema.semantic_view format. e.g. to match all semantic views starting with sales in Analytics database and public schema, use the regex 'Analytics.public.sales.*'

fields.name: stage_pattern
fields.label: Stage Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for stages to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire stage name in database.schema.stage format.

fields.name: task_pattern
fields.label: Task Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tasks to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire task name in database.schema.task format.

fields.name: pipe_pattern
fields.label: Pipe Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for pipes to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire pipe name in database.schema.pipe format.

fields.name: match_fully_qualified_names
fields.label: Match Fully Qualified Names
fields.description: Whether `schema_pattern` is matched against fully qualified schema name `<catalog>.<schema>`.

fields.name: push_down_metadata_patterns
fields.label: Push Down Metadata Patterns
fields.description: If enabled, pushes down database_pattern, schema_pattern, table_pattern, and view_pattern filtering to Snowflake information_schema metadata queries using the RLIKE operator for improved performance. This applies only to metadata extraction queries (information_schema.databases, schemata, tables, views) — NOT to lineage/usage queries (for those, see push_down_database_pattern_access_history). NOTE: view_pattern pushdown only works when fetch_views_from_information_schema is also enabled. With the default SHOW VIEWS, view_pattern filtering falls back to Python re.match(). IMPORTANT: Snowflake RLIKE requires FULL STRING match, unlike Python re.match() which matches prefixes. For prefix matching use 'PATTERN.*', for suffix use '.*PATTERN$', for contains use '.*PATTERN.*'. If the composed filter would exceed Snowflake's per-query size limit, that filter is automatically skipped and applied client-side instead (slower). See the [Metadata Pattern Pushdown](#metadata-pattern-pushdown) section for detailed usage and examples, and the [Snowflake RLIKE documentation](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/functions/rlike) for regex syntax details.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: temporary_tables_pattern
fields.label: Temporary Tables Pattern
fields.description: [Advanced] Regex patterns for temporary tables to filter in lineage ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. Defaults are to set in such a way to ignore the temporary staging tables created by known ETL tools.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: email_domain
fields.label: Email Domain
fields.description: Email domain of your organization so users can be displayed on UI appropriately. This is used only if we cannot infer email ID.

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: pushdown_deny_usernames
fields.label: Pushdown Deny Usernames
fields.description: List of snowflake usernames (SQL LIKE patterns, e.g., 'SERVICE_%', '%_PROD', 'TEST_USER') which will NOT be considered for lineage/usage/queries extraction. This is primarily useful for improving performance by filtering out users with extremely high query volumes.

fields.name: pushdown_allow_usernames
fields.label: Pushdown Allow Usernames
fields.description: List of snowflake usernames (SQL LIKE patterns, e.g., 'ANALYST_%', '%_USER', 'MAIN_ACCOUNT') which WILL be considered for lineage/usage/queries extraction. This is primarily useful for improving performance by filtering in only specific users. If not specified, all users not in deny list are included.

fields.name: push_down_database_pattern_access_history
fields.label: Push Down Database Pattern Access History
fields.description: If enabled, pushes down database pattern filtering to the access_history table for improved performance. This filters on the accessed objects in access_history.

fields.name: additional_database_names_allowlist
fields.label: Additional Database Names Allowlist
fields.description: Additional database names (no pattern matching) to be included in the access_history filter. Only applies if push_down_database_pattern_access_history=True. These databases will be included in the filter being pushed down regardless of database_pattern settings.This may be required in the case of _eg_ temporary tables being created in a different database than the ones in the database_name patterns.

fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

Refer [Account Identifiers](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/admin-account-identifier.html#format-2-legacy-account-locator-in-a-region) for more details.

group_fields.description: Identity provider for oauth.Supported providers are microsoft and okta.
