fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

fields.name: bdp_connection
fields.label: Bdp Connection
fields.description: BDP Console API connection (required for BDP mode)

group_fields.name: organization_id
group_fields.label: Organization Id
group_fields.description: Organization UUID (found in BDP Console URL)

group_fields.name: api_key_id
group_fields.label: Api Key Id
group_fields.description: API Key ID from BDP Console credentials

group_fields.name: api_key
group_fields.label: Api Key
group_fields.description: API Key secret from BDP Console credentials

group_fields.name: console_api_url
group_fields.label: Console Api Url
group_fields.description: BDP Console API base URL

group_fields.name: timeout_seconds
group_fields.label: Timeout Seconds
group_fields.description: Request timeout in seconds

group_fields.name: max_retries
group_fields.label: Max Retries
group_fields.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed requests

fields.name: iglu_connection
fields.label: Iglu Connection
fields.description: Iglu Schema Registry connection (required for Iglu mode, optional for BDP mode as fallback)

group_fields.name: iglu_server_url
group_fields.label: Iglu Server Url
group_fields.description: Iglu server base URL (e.g., 'https://iglu.acme.com' or 'http://iglucentral.com')

group_fields.name: api_key
group_fields.label: Api Key
group_fields.description: API key for private Iglu registry (UUID format)

group_fields.name: timeout_seconds
group_fields.label: Timeout Seconds
group_fields.description: Request timeout in seconds

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter (vendor/name format)

fields.name: event_spec_pattern
fields.label: Event Spec Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for event specifications to filter

fields.name: tracking_plan_pattern
fields.label: Tracking Plan Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tracking plans to filter

fields.name: include_hidden_schemas
fields.label: Include Hidden Schemas
fields.description: Include schemas marked as hidden in BDP Console

fields.name: include_version_in_urn
fields.label: Include Version In Urn
fields.description: Include version in dataset URN (legacy behavior). When False (recommended), version is stored in dataset properties instead. Set to True for backwards compatibility with existing metadata.

fields.name: warehouse_lineage
fields.label: Warehouse Lineage
fields.description: Warehouse lineage configuration for linking enrichment outputs to warehouse tables

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable warehouse lineage extraction via data models API. Disabled by default - prefer using warehouse connector (Snowflake, BigQuery) for detailed lineage.

group_fields.name: platform_instance
group_fields.label: Platform Instance
group_fields.description: Default platform instance prefix for warehouse URNs (e.g., 'prod_snowflake'). Applied globally unless overridden by destination_mappings.

group_fields.name: env
group_fields.label: Env
group_fields.description: Default environment for warehouse datasets

group_fields.name: destination_mappings
group_fields.label: Destination Mappings
group_fields.description: Per-destination platform instance mappings. Overrides global platform_instance for specific destinations.

group_fields.name: validate_urns
group_fields.label: Validate Urns
group_fields.description: Validate that warehouse table URNs exist in DataHub before creating lineage. Requires DataHub Graph API access. Set to False to skip validation.

fields.name: enrichment_owner
fields.label: Enrichment Owner
fields.description: Default owner for enrichments (e.g., 'data-platform@company.com'). Applied as DATAOWNER to all enrichment DataJobs. Leave empty to skip enrichment ownership.

fields.name: field_tagging
fields.label: Field Tagging
fields.description: Field tagging configuration for auto-tagging schema fields

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Enable automatic field tagging

group_fields.name: tag_schema_version
group_fields.label: Tag Schema Version
group_fields.description: Tag fields with schema version (e.g., snowplow_schema_v1-0-0)

group_fields.name: tag_event_type
group_fields.label: Tag Event Type
group_fields.description: Tag fields with event type (e.g., snowplow_event_checkout)

group_fields.name: tag_data_class
group_fields.label: Tag Data Class
group_fields.description: Tag fields with data classification (e.g., PII, Sensitive)

group_fields.name: tag_authorship
group_fields.label: Tag Authorship
group_fields.description: Tag fields with authorship (e.g., added_by_ryan_smith)

group_fields.name: track_field_versions
group_fields.label: Track Field Versions
group_fields.description: Track which version each field was added in. When enabled, compares schema versions to determine when fields were introduced. Tags fields with their introduction version and adds 'Added in version X' to descriptions. Disabled by default as it requires fetching all schema versions (slower ingestion).

group_fields.name: use_structured_properties
group_fields.label: Use Structured Properties
group_fields.description: Use structured properties for field metadata instead of (or in addition to) tags. Structured properties provide strongly-typed metadata with better querying capabilities. When enabled, field authorship, version, timestamp, and classification are emitted as structured properties on the schemaField entity. Note: Requires structured property definitions to be registered in DataHub first. See snowplow_field_structured_properties.yaml in the connector directory.

group_fields.name: emit_tags_and_structured_properties
group_fields.label: Emit Tags And Structured Properties
group_fields.description: Emit both tags and structured properties for fields. When True, both tags and structured properties are emitted. When False, only the method specified by use_structured_properties is used. Useful during migration from tags to structured properties.

group_fields.name: pii_tags_only
group_fields.label: Pii Tags Only
group_fields.description: When emit_tags_and_structured_properties is true, only emit tags for PII/sensitive data classification. Version, authorship, and event type will only be in structured properties, not as tags. Useful when you want detailed structured properties but only highlight PII fields with tags.

group_fields.name: schema_version_pattern
group_fields.label: Schema Version Pattern
group_fields.description: Pattern for schema version tags. Use {version} placeholder.

group_fields.name: event_type_pattern
group_fields.label: Event Type Pattern
group_fields.description: Pattern for event type tags. Use {name} placeholder.

group_fields.name: authorship_pattern
group_fields.label: Authorship Pattern
group_fields.description: Pattern for authorship tags. Use {author} placeholder.

group_fields.name: use_pii_enrichment
group_fields.label: Use Pii Enrichment
group_fields.description: Extract PII fields from PII Pseudonymization enrichment config

group_fields.name: pii_field_patterns
group_fields.label: Pii Field Patterns
group_fields.description: Field name patterns to classify as PII (fallback if enrichment not available)

group_fields.name: sensitive_field_patterns
group_fields.label: Sensitive Field Patterns
group_fields.description: Field name patterns to classify as Sensitive

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration for deletion detection

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: deployment_environment
fields.label: Deployment Environment
fields.description: Filter schemas by Snowplow deployment environment. Matches the `env` field on a data structure deployment (e.g. `PROD`, `DEV`). Only schemas with a matching deployment will be ingested; schemas with no deployment info (e.g. drafts) are included. Case-insensitive. When not set, schemas from all environments are included.

fields.name: pipeline_label
fields.label: Pipeline Label
fields.description: Filter Snowplow pipelines by their `label` field in the BDP API. The label is free-form text set by the operator (commonly the deployment env, e.g. `prod` or `dev`, but not enforced by Snowplow). Only pipelines matching this label will be ingested; pipelines with no label are included by default. Case-insensitive. When not set, all pipelines are included.

fields.name: event_spec_statuses
fields.label: Event Spec Statuses
fields.description: Filter event specifications by status. Only event specs with a status in this list will be ingested. Valid values: draft, published, deprecated, archived. When not set, all statuses are included.

fields.name: extract_event_specifications
fields.label: Extract Event Specifications
fields.description: Extract event specifications (requires BDP connection)

fields.name: extract_tracking_plans
fields.label: Extract Tracking Plans
fields.description: Extract tracking plans (requires BDP connection)

fields.name: extract_pipelines
fields.label: Extract Pipelines
fields.description: Extract pipelines as DataFlow entities (requires BDP connection)

fields.name: extract_enrichments
fields.label: Extract Enrichments
fields.description: Extract enrichments as DataJob entities linked to pipelines (requires BDP connection)

fields.name: schema_types_to_extract
fields.label: Schema Types To Extract
fields.description: Schema types to extract: 'event' and/or 'entity'

fields.name: deployed_since
fields.label: Deployed Since
fields.description: Only extract schemas deployed/updated since this timestamp (ISO 8601 format: 2025-12-15T00:00:00Z). Enables incremental ingestion by filtering based on deployment timestamps. Leave empty to fetch all schemas.

fields.name: schema_page_size
fields.label: Schema Page Size
fields.description: Number of schemas to fetch per API page (default: 100). Adjust based on organization size and API performance.

fields.name: extract_standard_schemas
fields.label: Extract Standard Schemas
fields.description: Extract Snowplow standard schemas from Iglu Central that are referenced by event specifications. Standard schemas (vendor: com.snowplowanalytics.*) are not in the Data Structures API but are publicly available. When enabled, creates dataset entities for standard schemas and completes lineage from event specs. Only fetches schemas that are actually referenced, not all standard schemas. Disable if you don't want to fetch from Iglu Central.

fields.name: iglu_central_url
fields.label: Iglu Central Url
fields.description: Iglu Central base URL for fetching Snowplow standard schemas

fields.name: performance
fields.label: Performance
fields.description: Performance and scaling configuration for large deployments

group_fields.name: max_concurrent_api_calls
group_fields.label: Max Concurrent Api Calls
group_fields.description: Maximum concurrent API calls for deployment fetching. Increase for faster ingestion of large organizations with many schemas. Recommended: 5-20 depending on API rate limits.

group_fields.name: enable_parallel_fetching
group_fields.label: Enable Parallel Fetching
group_fields.description: Enable parallel fetching of schema deployments. Significantly speeds up ingestion when field version tracking is enabled. Disable for debugging or if API rate limits are strict.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: The platform for which to generate data, e.g. snowflake

fields.name: aws_config
fields.label: Aws Config
fields.description: AWS configuration for S3 access. Required when query_file is an S3 URI (s3://).

group_fields.name: aws_access_key_id
group_fields.label: Aws Access Key Id
group_fields.description: AWS access key ID. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
group_fields.label: Aws Secret Access Key
group_fields.description: AWS secret access key. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_session_token
group_fields.label: Aws Session Token
group_fields.description: AWS session token. Can be auto-detected, see [the AWS boto3 docs](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/credentials.html) for details.

group_fields.name: aws_role
group_fields.label: Aws Role
group_fields.description: AWS roles to assume. If using the string format, the role ARN can be specified directly. If using the object format, the role can be specified in the RoleArn field and additional available arguments are the same as [boto3's STS.Client.assume_role](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts.html?highlight=assume_role#STS.Client.assume_role).

group_fields.name: aws_profile
group_fields.label: Aws Profile
group_fields.description: The [named profile](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html) to use from AWS credentials. Falls back to default profile if not specified and no access keys provided. Profiles are configured in ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.aws/config.

group_fields.name: aws_region
group_fields.label: Aws Region
group_fields.description: AWS region code.

group_fields.name: aws_endpoint_url
group_fields.label: Aws Endpoint Url
group_fields.description: The AWS service endpoint. This is normally [constructed automatically](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/core/session.html), but can be overridden here.

group_fields.name: aws_proxy
group_fields.label: Aws Proxy
group_fields.description: A set of proxy configs to use with AWS. See the [botocore.config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_num
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Num
group_fields.description: Number of times to retry failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: aws_retry_mode
group_fields.label: Aws Retry Mode
group_fields.description: Retry mode to use for failed AWS requests. See the [botocore.retry](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/guide/retries.html) docs for details.

group_fields.name: read_timeout
group_fields.label: Read Timeout
group_fields.description: The timeout for reading from the connection (in seconds).

group_fields.name: aws_advanced_config
group_fields.label: Aws Advanced Config
group_fields.description: Advanced AWS configuration options. These are passed directly to [botocore.config.Config](https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html).

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: query_file
fields.label: Query File
fields.description: Path to file to ingest

fields.name: usage
fields.label: Usage
fields.description: The usage config to use when generating usage statistics

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

group_fields.name: top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

group_fields.name: user_email_pattern
group_fields.label: User Email Pattern
group_fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

group_fields.name: include_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

group_fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

group_fields.name: format_sql_queries
group_fields.label: Format Sql Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

group_fields.name: include_top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Include Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: default_db
fields.label: Default Db
fields.description: The default database to use for unqualified table names

fields.name: default_schema
fields.label: Default Schema
fields.description: The default schema to use for unqualified table names

fields.name: override_dialect
fields.label: Override Dialect
fields.description: The SQL dialect to use when parsing queries. Overrides automatic dialect detection.

fields.name: temp_table_patterns
fields.label: Temp Table Patterns
fields.description: Regex patterns for temporary tables to filter in lineage ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name. This is useful for platforms like Athena that don't have native temp tables but use naming patterns for fake temp tables.

fields.name: enable_lazy_schema_loading
fields.label: Enable Lazy Schema Loading
fields.description: Enable lazy schema loading for better performance. When enabled, schemas are fetched on-demand instead of bulk loading all schemas upfront, reducing startup time and memory usage.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: connect_uri
fields.label: Connect Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls

fields.name: platform
fields.label: Platform
fields.description: Name of platform being ingested, used in constructing URNs.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: host URL

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The name of the catalog from getting the usage

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: ingest_lineage_to_connectors
fields.label: Ingest Lineage To Connectors
fields.description: Whether lineage of datasets to connectors should be ingested

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: When emitting upstreamLineage to connector sources (e.g. Iceberg, Hive), include column-level (fine-grained) lineage. Requires schema to be available.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: include_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

fields.name: format_sql_queries
fields.label: Format Sql Queries
fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

fields.name: include_top_n_queries
fields.label: Include Top N Queries
fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: email_domain
fields.label: Email Domain
fields.description: The email domain which will be appended to the users

fields.name: audit_catalog
fields.label: Audit Catalog
fields.description: The catalog name where the audit table can be found

fields.name: audit_schema
fields.label: Audit Schema
fields.description: The schema name where the audit table can be found

fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: catalog_to_connector_details
fields.label: Catalog To Connector Details
fields.description: A mapping of trino catalog to its connector details like connector database, env and platform instance.This configuration is used to build lineage to the underlying connector. Use catalog name as key.

fields.name: trino_as_primary
fields.label: Trino As Primary
fields.description: Experimental feature. Whether trino dataset should be primary entity of the set of siblings

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: StarRocks FE host URL.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: Database in catalog.database format (e.g., 'default_catalog.quickstart'). If specified, only this database will be ingested. Leave unset for multi-catalog discovery.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion. Note: In StarRocks three-tier hierarchy (Catalog -> Database -> Table), this filters databases within catalogs. Specify regex to match the database name only (e.g., 'quickstart', not 'default_catalog.quickstart').

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: include_external_catalogs
fields.label: Include External Catalogs
fields.description: Whether to include external catalogs (Hive, Iceberg, etc.) in ingestion.

fields.name: catalog_pattern
fields.label: Catalog Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for catalogs to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the catalog name.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count

group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value

fields.description: Tableau token name, must be set if authenticating using a personal access token.

fields.name: token_value

fields.name: site

fields.description: Tableau Site. Always required for Tableau Online. Use emptystring to connect with Default site on Tableau Server.

fields.name: max_retries
fields.label: Max Retries

fields.name: ssl_verify

fields.description: Whether to verify SSL certificates. If using self-signed certificates, set to false or provide the path to the .pem certificate bundle.

fields.name: session_trust_env

fields.description: Configures the trust_env property in the requests session. If set to false (default value) it will bypass proxy settings. See https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/#requests.Session.trust_env for more information.

fields.name: extract_column_level_lineage
fields.label: Extract Column Level Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, extracts column-level lineage from Tableau Datasources

fields.name: projects
fields.label: Projects
fields.description: [deprecated] Use project_pattern instead. List of tableau projects

fields.name: project_pattern
fields.label: Project Pattern
fields.description: [deprecated] Use project_path_pattern instead. Filter for specific Tableau projects. For example, use 'My Project' to ingest a root-level Project with name 'My Project', or 'My Project/Nested Project' to ingest a nested Project with name 'Nested Project'. By default, all Projects nested inside a matching Project will be included in ingestion. You can both allow and deny projects based on their name using their name, or a Regex pattern. Deny patterns always take precedence over allow patterns. By default, all projects will be ingested.

fields.name: project_path_pattern
fields.label: Project Path Pattern
fields.description: Filters Tableau projects by their full path. For instance, 'My Project/Nested Project' targets a specific nested project named 'Nested Project'. This is also useful when you need to exclude all nested projects under a particular project. You can allow or deny projects by specifying their path or a regular expression pattern. Deny patterns always override allow patterns. By default, all projects are ingested.

fields.name: ingest_tables_external
fields.label: Ingest Tables External
fields.description: Ingest details for tables external to (not embedded in) tableau as entities.

fields.name: ingest_virtual_connections
fields.label: Ingest Virtual Connections
fields.description: Ingest details for virtual connections as entities.

fields.name: ingest_embed_url
fields.label: Ingest Embed Url
fields.description: Ingest a URL to render an embedded Preview of assets within Tableau.

fields.name: ingest_external_links_for_dashboards
fields.label: Ingest External Links For Dashboards
fields.description: Ingest a URL to link out to from dashboards.

fields.name: ingest_external_links_for_charts
fields.label: Ingest External Links For Charts
fields.description: Ingest a URL to link out to from charts.

fields.name: ingest_initial_sql
fields.label: Ingest Initial Sql
fields.description: [Experimental] When enabled, downloads published data sources and the workbooks that contain embedded data sources to extract their Initial SQL and emit lineage and/or a custom property from it. Disabled by default because the extra downloads can be slow on large sites.

fields.name: ingest_multiple_sites
fields.label: Ingest Multiple Sites
fields.description: When enabled, ingests multiple sites the user has access to. If the user doesn't have access to the default site, specify an initial site to query in the site property. By default all sites the user has access to will be ingested. You can filter sites with the site_name_pattern property. This flag is currently only supported for Tableau Server. Tableau Cloud is not supported.

fields.name: site_name_pattern
fields.label: Site Name Pattern
fields.description: Filter for specific Tableau sites. By default, all sites will be included in the ingestion. You can both allow and deny sites based on their name using their name, or a Regex pattern. Deny patterns always take precedence over allow patterns. This property is currently only supported for Tableau Server. Tableau Cloud is not supported.

fields.name: ingest_hidden_assets
fields.label: Ingest Hidden Assets
fields.description: When enabled, hidden worksheets and dashboards are ingested into Datahub. If a dashboard or worksheet is hidden in Tableau the luid is blank. A list of asset types can also be specified, to only ingest those hidden assets. Current options supported are 'worksheet' and 'dashboard'.

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform_instance mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: lineage_overrides
fields.label: Lineage Overrides
fields.description: Mappings to change generated dataset urns. Use only if you really know what you are doing.

group_fields.name: platform_override_map
group_fields.label: Platform Override Map
group_fields.description: A holder for platform -> platform mappings to generate correct dataset urns

group_fields.name: database_override_map
group_fields.label: Database Override Map
group_fields.description: A holder for database -> database mappings to generate correct dataset urns

fields.name: extract_lineage_from_unsupported_custom_sql_queries
fields.label: Extract Lineage From Unsupported Custom Sql Queries
fields.description: [Experimental] Extract lineage from Custom SQL queries using DataHub's SQL parser in cases where the Tableau Catalog API fails to return lineage for the query.

fields.name: force_extraction_of_lineage_from_custom_sql_queries
fields.label: Force Extraction Of Lineage From Custom Sql Queries
fields.description: [Experimental] Force extraction of lineage from Custom SQL queries using DataHub's SQL parser, even when the Tableau Catalog API returns lineage already.

fields.name: initial_sql_as_lineage
fields.label: Initial Sql As Lineage
fields.description: When ingest_initial_sql is enabled, parse the Initial SQL with DataHub's SQL parser and emit the referenced tables as upstream lineage of the data source.

fields.name: extract_usage_stats
fields.label: Extract Usage Stats
fields.description: [experimental] Extract usage statistics for dashboards and charts.

fields.name: ingest_tags
fields.label: Ingest Tags
fields.description: Ingest Tags from source. This will override Tags entered from UI

fields.name: ingest_owner
fields.label: Ingest Owner
fields.description: Ingest Owner from source. This will override Owner info entered from UI

fields.name: tags_for_hidden_assets
fields.label: Tags For Hidden Assets
fields.description: Tags to be added to hidden dashboards and views. If a dashboard or view is hidden in Tableau the luid is blank. This can only be used with ingest_tags enabled as it will overwrite tags entered from the UI.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: page_size
fields.label: Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of metadata objects (e.g. CustomSQLTable, PublishedDatasource, etc) to query at a time using the Tableau API.

fields.name: database_server_page_size
fields.label: Database Server Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of database servers to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: workbook_page_size
fields.label: Workbook Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of workbooks to query at a time using the Tableau API; defaults to `1` and fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: sheet_page_size
fields.label: Sheet Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of sheets to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: dashboard_page_size
fields.label: Dashboard Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of dashboards to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: embedded_datasource_page_size
fields.label: Embedded Datasource Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of embedded datasources to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: embedded_datasource_field_upstream_page_size
fields.label: Embedded Datasource Field Upstream Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of upstream fields to query at a time for embedded datasources using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` * 10 if not set.

fields.name: published_datasource_page_size
fields.label: Published Datasource Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of published datasources to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: published_datasource_field_upstream_page_size
fields.label: Published Datasource Field Upstream Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of upstream fields to query at a time for published datasources using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` * 10 if not set.

fields.name: virtual_connection_page_size
fields.label: Virtual Connection Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of virtual connections to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: custom_sql_table_page_size
fields.label: Custom Sql Table Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of custom sql datasources to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: database_table_page_size
fields.label: Database Table Page Size
fields.description: [advanced] Number of database tables to query at a time using the Tableau API; fallbacks to `page_size` if not set.

fields.name: project_path_separator
fields.label: Project Path Separator
fields.description: The separator used for the project_path_pattern field between project names. By default, we use a slash. You can change this if your Tableau projects contain slashes in their names, and you'd like to filter by project.

fields.name: default_schema_map
fields.label: Default Schema Map
fields.description: Default schema to use when schema is not found.

fields.name: use_email_as_username
fields.label: Use Email As Username
fields.description: Use email address instead of username for entity owners. Requires ingest_owner to be True.

fields.name: emit_all_published_datasources
fields.label: Emit All Published Datasources
fields.description: Ingest all published data sources. When False (default), only ingest published data sources that belong to an ingested workbook.

fields.name: emit_all_embedded_datasources
fields.label: Emit All Embedded Datasources
fields.description: Ingest all embedded data sources. When False (default), only ingest embedded data sources that belong to an ingested workbook.

fields.name: database_hostname_to_platform_instance_map
fields.label: Database Hostname To Platform Instance Map
fields.description: Mappings to change platform instance in generated dataset urns based on database. Use only if you really know what you are doing.

fields.name: extract_project_hierarchy
fields.label: Extract Project Hierarchy
fields.description: Whether to extract entire project hierarchy for nested projects.

fields.name: initial_sql_as_custom_property
fields.label: Initial Sql As Custom Property
fields.description: When ingest_initial_sql is enabled, store the raw Initial SQL text as the `initialSql` custom property on the data source dataset.

fields.name: sql_parsing_disable_schema_awareness
fields.label: Sql Parsing Disable Schema Awareness
fields.description: [Experimental] Ignore pre ingested tables schemas during parsing of SQL queries (allows to workaround ingestion errors when pre ingested schema and queries are out of sync)

fields.name: add_site_container
fields.label: Add Site Container
fields.description: When enabled, sites are added as containers and therefore visible in the folder structure within Datahub.

fields.name: permission_ingestion
fields.label: Permission Ingestion
fields.description: Configuration settings for ingesting Tableau groups and their capabilities as custom properties.

group_fields.name: enable_workbooks
group_fields.label: Enable Workbooks
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable group permission ingestion for workbooks. Default: True

group_fields.name: group_name_pattern
group_fields.label: Group Name Pattern
group_fields.description: Filter for Tableau group names when ingesting group permissions. For example, you could filter for groups that include the term 'Consumer' in their name by adding '^.*Consumer$' to the allow list.By default, all groups will be ingested. You can both allow and deny groups based on their name using their name, or a Regex pattern. Deny patterns always take precedence over allow patterns.

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: Environment to use in namespace when constructing URNs.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: host URL

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: scheme
fields.label: Scheme
fields.description: database scheme

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: database_pattern
fields.label: Database Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for databases to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Whether to include view lineage in the ingestion. This requires to have the view lineage feature enabled.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_table_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Lineage
fields.description: Whether to include table lineage in the ingestion. This requires to have the table lineage feature enabled.

fields.name: include_historical_lineage
fields.label: Include Historical Lineage
fields.description: Whether to include historical lineage data from PDCRINFO.DBQLSqlTbl_Hst in addition to current DBC.QryLogV data. This provides access to historical query logs that may have been archived. The historical table existence is checked automatically and gracefully falls back to current data only if not available.

fields.name: lineage_fetch_stall_warning_seconds
fields.label: Lineage Fetch Stall Warning Seconds
fields.description: If no lineage row batch arrives from DBC.QryLogV within this many seconds, emit a warning identifying the stalled phase. Set to 0 to disable. Default is 300 (5 minutes).

fields.name: lineage_fetch_batch_size
fields.label: Lineage Fetch Batch Size
fields.description: Number of rows fetched per batch when streaming results from DBC.QryLogV during lineage extraction. Each row can carry several KB of query_text, so larger values increase peak memory usage while smaller values increase the number of round-trips to the database. Lower this (e.g. to a few hundred, or lower still) if the ingestion process runs out of memory during lineage extraction; raise it to reduce round-trips when rows are small and network latency is high. Must be a positive integer (a batch size of 0 would fetch no rows and stall the stream). NOTE: this only reduces memory when `use_server_side_cursors` is true (the default). With client-side cursors the driver buffers the entire result set in memory before this batching applies, so lowering the batch size will not prevent out-of-memory errors in that mode — it only changes the Python iteration chunk size. Default is 5000.

fields.name: lineage_slow_query_log_seconds
fields.label: Lineage Slow Query Log Seconds
fields.description: When the total database time for a single lineage query (execute call plus all fetchmany calls, excluding downstream processing time) exceeds this many seconds, emit a warning with the query label, elapsed DB time, and the first 500 characters of the SQL text so slow queries can be identified and tuned. Note: when the driver retries a failed fetchmany call, the retry backoff sleep time is included in the measurement, so the threshold should be set well above the expected base query time. Set to 0 to disable. Default is 60 seconds.

fields.name: include_queries
fields.label: Include Queries
fields.description: Whether to generate query entities for SQL queries. Query entities provide metadata about individual SQL queries including execution timestamps, user information, and query text.

fields.name: usage
fields.label: Usage
fields.description: The usage config to use when generating usage statistics

group_fields.name: bucket_duration
group_fields.label: Bucket Duration
group_fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

group_fields.name: end_time
group_fields.label: End Time
group_fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

group_fields.name: start_time
group_fields.label: Start Time
group_fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

group_fields.name: top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

group_fields.name: user_email_pattern
group_fields.label: User Email Pattern
group_fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

group_fields.name: include_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

group_fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
group_fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
group_fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

group_fields.name: format_sql_queries
group_fields.label: Format Sql Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

group_fields.name: include_top_n_queries
group_fields.label: Include Top N Queries
group_fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistic.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
