fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: catalog_to_connector_details
fields.label: Catalog To Connector Details
fields.description: A mapping of trino catalog to its connector details like connector database, env and platform instance.This configuration is used to build lineage to the underlying connector. Use catalog name as key.

fields.name: trino_as_primary
fields.label: Trino As Primary
fields.description: Experimental feature. Whether trino dataset should be primary entity of the set of siblings

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.

fields.name: workspace_url
fields.label: Workspace Url
fields.description: Databricks workspace url. e.g. https://my-workspace.cloud.databricks.com

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Databricks service principal client ID

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Databricks service principal client secret

fields.name: token
fields.label: Token
fields.description: Databricks personal access token

fields.name: azure_auth
fields.label: Azure Auth
fields.description: Azure configuration

group_fields.name: client_secret
group_fields.label: Client Secret
group_fields.description: Azure application client secret used for authentication. This is a confidential credential that should be kept secure.

group_fields.name: client_id
group_fields.label: Client Id
group_fields.description: Azure application (client) ID. This is the unique identifier for the registered Azure AD application.

group_fields.name: tenant_id
group_fields.label: Tenant Id
group_fields.description: Azure tenant (directory) ID. This identifies the Azure AD tenant where the application is registered.

fields.name: warehouse_id
fields.label: Warehouse Id
fields.description: SQL Warehouse id, for running queries. Must be explicitly provided to enable SQL-based features. Required for the following features that need SQL access: 1) Tag extraction (include_tags=True) - queries system.information_schema.tags 2) Hive Metastore catalog (include_hive_metastore=True) - queries legacy hive_metastore catalog 3) System table lineage (lineage_data_source=SYSTEM_TABLES) - queries system.access.table_lineage/column_lineage 4) Data profiling (profiling.enabled=True) - runs SELECT/ANALYZE queries on tables. When warehouse_id is missing, these features will be automatically disabled (with warnings) to allow ingestion to continue.

fields.name: extra_client_options
fields.label: Extra Client Options
fields.description: Additional options to pass to Databricks SQLAlchemy client.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to the full `metastore.catalog.schema` name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex `^mymetastore\.mycatalog\.analytics$`.

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `catalog.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer catalog and public schema, use the regex `Customer\.public\.customer.*`.

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: include_metastore
fields.label: Include Metastore
fields.description: Whether to ingest the workspace's metastore as a container and include it in all urns. Changing this will affect the urns of all entities in the workspace. This config is deprecated and will be removed in the future, so it is recommended to not set this to `True` for new ingestions. If you have an existing unity catalog ingestion, you'll want to avoid duplicates by soft deleting existing data. If stateful ingestion is enabled, running with `include_metastore: false` should be sufficient. Otherwise, we recommend deleting via the cli: `datahub delete --platform databricks` and re-ingesting with `include_metastore: false`.

fields.name: ingest_data_platform_instance_aspect
fields.label: Ingest Data Platform Instance Aspect
fields.description: Option to enable/disable ingestion of the data platform instance aspect. The default data platform instance id for a dataset is workspace_name

fields.name: catalog_pattern
fields.label: Catalog Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for catalogs to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the full `metastore.catalog` name.

fields.name: notebook_pattern
fields.label: Notebook Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for notebooks to filter in ingestion, based on notebook *path*. Specify regex to match the entire notebook path in `/<dir>/.../<name>` format. e.g. to match all notebooks in the root Shared directory, use the regex `/Shared/.*`.

fields.name: metric_view_pattern
fields.label: Metric View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for Unity Catalog Metric Views to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the full `catalog.schema.metric_view_name`. Only applies when `include_metric_views` is True.

fields.name: include_metric_views
fields.label: Include Metric Views
fields.description: Enable enriched ingestion of Unity Catalog Metric Views: subtype 'Metric View', YAML body as ViewProperties, upstream and column-level lineage from `source` / `joins` / `dimensions.expr` / `measures.expr`, `Dimension` / `Measure` tags on matching columns, `materialization` → `ViewProperties.materialized`, and `filter` as a custom property. Default `false` keeps metric views as plain Tables. Requires a `databricks-sdk` recent enough to expose `TableType.METRIC_VIEW`.

fields.name: include_notebooks
fields.label: Include Notebooks
fields.description: Ingest notebooks, represented as DataHub datasets.

fields.name: include_federation_column_backfill
fields.label: Include Federation Column Backfill
fields.description: For foreign (Lakehouse Federation) catalog tables whose columns Unity Catalog has not synced yet, resolve the schema from the already-ingested external source dataset via the DataHub graph. Requires a graph connection (e.g. a datahub-rest sink) and the external source to be ingested; otherwise it is a no-op.

fields.name: include_table_constraints
fields.label: Include Table Constraints
fields.description: If enabled, fetches primary key and foreign key constraints for each table via an additional tables.get() API call per table (one call per table). Disabled by default to avoid unexpected API load on large catalogs. Enables PK/FK visualisation in the DataHub schema view when set to true.

fields.name: include_partition_keys
fields.label: Include Partition Keys
fields.description: If enabled, the `isPartitioningKey` field is populated on schema fields for columns that are part of the table's partition key. Partition key information is already present in the tables.list() response so no additional API calls are made.

fields.name: include_ml_models
fields.label: Include Ml Models
fields.description: Whether to ingest ML models (MLModelGroups and MLModels) registered in Unity Catalog. Set to False to skip ML model discovery entirely — no calls are made to the registered-models API.

fields.name: include_ml_model_aliases
fields.label: Include Ml Model Aliases
fields.description: Whether to include ML model aliases in the ingestion.

fields.name: include_hive_metastore
fields.label: Include Hive Metastore
fields.description: Whether to ingest legacy `hive_metastore` catalog. This requires executing queries on SQL warehouse.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: Populates view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_table_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Lineage
fields.description: Option to enable/disable lineage generation.

fields.name: include_external_lineage
fields.label: Include External Lineage
fields.description: Option to enable/disable lineage generation for external tables. Only external S3 tables are supported at the moment.

fields.name: include_column_lineage
fields.label: Include Column Lineage
fields.description: Option to enable/disable lineage generation. Currently we have to call a rest call per column to get column level lineage due to the Databrick api which can slow down ingestion.

fields.name: include_federation_lineage
fields.label: Include Federation Lineage
fields.description: Emit an upstream COPY lineage edge from each Lakehouse Federation foreign catalog table to its external source dataset (with column-level lineage when include_column_lineage is set). Disable to skip the cross-platform link.

fields.name: lineage_data_source
fields.label: Lineage Data Source
fields.description: Source for lineage data extraction. Options: 'AUTO' - Use system tables when SQL warehouse is available, fallback to API; 'SYSTEM_TABLES' - Force use of system.access.table_lineage and system.access.column_lineage tables (requires SQL warehouse); 'API' - Force use of REST API endpoints for lineage data

fields.name: ignore_start_time_lineage
fields.label: Ignore Start Time Lineage
fields.description: Option to ignore the start_time and retrieve all available lineage. When enabled, the start_time filter will be set to zero to extract all lineage events regardless of the configured time window.

fields.name: column_lineage_column_limit
fields.label: Column Lineage Column Limit
fields.description: Limit the number of columns to get column level lineage.

fields.name: skip_sqlglot_when_system_table_lineage_missing
fields.label: Skip Sqlglot When System Table Lineage Missing
fields.description: If enabled on the system-tables usage path, queries with no matching rows in system.access.table_lineage (for their statement_id within the configured time window) are skipped instead of parsed with sqlglot. Only applies when usage is fetched via system.query.history joined with system.access.table_lineage. Queries that have lineage but unresolvable dataset URNs still fall back to sqlglot.

fields.name: top_n_queries
fields.label: Top N Queries
fields.description: Number of top queries to save to each table.

fields.name: user_email_pattern
fields.label: User Email Pattern
fields.description: regex patterns for user emails to filter in usage.

fields.name: include_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to display operational stats.

fields.name: include_read_operational_stats
fields.label: Include Read Operational Stats
fields.description: Whether to report read operational stats. Experimental.

fields.name: format_sql_queries
fields.label: Format Sql Queries
fields.description: Whether to format sql queries

fields.name: include_top_n_queries
fields.label: Include Top N Queries
fields.description: Whether to ingest the top_n_queries.

fields.name: include_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Usage Statistics
fields.description: Generate usage statistics.

fields.name: usage_data_source
fields.label: Usage Data Source
fields.description: Source for usage/query history data extraction. Options: 'AUTO' (default) - Automatically use system.query.history table when SQL warehouse is configured, otherwise fall back to REST API. This provides better performance for multi-workspace setups and large query volumes when warehouse_id is set. 'SYSTEM_TABLES' - Force use of system.query.history table (requires SQL warehouse and SELECT permission on system.query.history). 'API' - Force use of REST API endpoints for query history (legacy method, may have limitations with multiple workspaces).

fields.name: include_queries
fields.label: Include Queries
fields.description: If enabled, emit DataHub Query entities for the SQL statements seen in query history (the statement text and the datasets it reads/writes). Only effective on the system-tables usage path; identical statements are de-duplicated by fingerprint.

fields.name: include_query_usage_statistics
fields.label: Include Query Usage Statistics
fields.description: If enabled, emit per-query usage/popularity statistics (queryUsageStatistics) for the emitted Query entities. Only effective when include_queries is enabled.

fields.name: include_column_usage_stats
fields.label: Include Column Usage Stats
fields.description: If enabled, force full sqlglot parsing of usage queries so column-level usage statistics (fieldCounts) are produced. This bypasses the faster system-table preparsed lineage path, so usage extraction is slower. Only changes behavior on the system-tables usage path (the REST API path already parses every query). Takes precedence over push_down_database_pattern_access_history and skip_sqlglot_when_system_table_lineage_missing, which are ignored when set.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

fields.name: profiling
fields.label: Profiling
fields.description: Data profiling configuration

fields.name: incremental_ownership
fields.label: Incremental Ownership
fields.description: When enabled, emits ownership as incremental to existing ownership in DataHub. When disabled, re-states ownership on each run.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: include_ownership
fields.label: Include Ownership
fields.description: Option to enable/disable ownership generation for metastores, catalogs, schemas, and tables.

fields.name: include_tags
fields.label: Include Tags
fields.description: Option to enable/disable column/table tag extraction. Requires warehouse_id to be set since tag extraction needs to query system.information_schema.tags. If warehouse_id is not provided, this will be automatically disabled to allow ingestion to continue.

fields.name: emit_federation_structured_properties
fields.label: Emit Federation Structured Properties
fields.description: Define and assign structured properties marking foreign catalogs as federated (facetable in the UI).

fields.name: federation_structured_property_namespace
fields.label: Federation Structured Property Namespace
fields.description: Qualified-name prefix for the federation structured properties; each property is this prefix plus its suffix (e.g. 'databricks.federation.platform').

fields.name: enable_stateful_profiling
fields.label: Enable Stateful Profiling
fields.description: Enable stateful profiling. This will store profiling timestamps per dataset after successful profiling. and will not run profiling again in subsequent run if table has not been updated.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Unity Catalog Stateful Ingestion Config.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: incremental_properties
fields.label: Incremental Properties
fields.description: When enabled, emits dataset properties as incremental to existing dataset properties in DataHub. When disabled, re-states dataset properties on each run.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: bucket_duration
fields.label: Bucket Duration
fields.description: Size of the time window to aggregate usage stats.

fields.name: end_time
fields.label: End Time
fields.description: Latest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Current time in UTC

fields.name: start_time
fields.label: Start Time
fields.description: Earliest date of lineage/usage to consider. Default: Last full day in UTC (or hour, depending on `bucket_duration`). You can also specify relative time with respect to end_time such as '-7 days' Or '-7d'.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to catalogs, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: catalogs
fields.label: Catalogs
fields.description: Fixed list of catalogs to ingest. If not specified, catalogs will be ingested based on `catalog_pattern`.

fields.name: federation_connection_details
fields.label: Federation Connection Details
fields.description: Per-connection overrides keyed by Unity Catalog connection name.

fields.name: databricks_api_page_size
fields.label: Databricks Api Page Size
fields.description: Page size for Databricks API calls when listing resources (catalogs, schemas, tables, etc.). When set to 0 (default), uses server-side configured page length (recommended). When set to a positive value, the page length is the minimum of this value and the server configured value. Must be a non-negative integer.

fields.name: push_down_database_pattern_access_history
fields.label: Push Down Database Pattern Access History
fields.description: If enabled, pushes down catalog pattern filtering to system.access.table_lineage for improved performance during usage extraction. This filters on source and target catalogs in table_lineage. Maps to Snowflake's database_pattern semantics via catalog_pattern (Unity catalog = Snowflake database). Only applies when usage is fetched via system tables (usage_data_source AUTO with warehouse or SYSTEM_TABLES). Also adds a statement_id semi-join against table_lineage, so only queries that have at least one lineage row in the configured time window are fetched; queries without system.access.table_lineage rows are omitted entirely (not sqlglot-fallbacked).

fields.name: emit_siblings
fields.label: Emit Siblings
fields.description: Whether to emit siblings relation with corresponding delta-lake platform's table. If enabled, this will also ingest the corresponding delta-lake table.

fields.name: delta_lake_options
fields.label: Delta Lake Options
fields.description: Details about the delta lake, incase to emit siblings

group_fields.name: platform_instance_name
group_fields.label: Platform Instance Name
group_fields.description: Delta-lake platform instance name

group_fields.name: env
group_fields.label: Env
group_fields.description: Delta-lake environment

fields.name: ml_model_max_results
fields.label: Ml Model Max Results
fields.description: Maximum total number of ML models to ingest per schema. Set to 0 to ingest none. To disable ML model ingestion entirely (including API calls), use `include_ml_models: false` instead.

fields.name: workspace_name
fields.label: Workspace Name
fields.description: Name of the workspace. Default to deployment name present in workspace_url

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Region
fields.description: [deprecated] Single Vertex AI region. Prefer 'regions' or 'discover_regions'.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project Id
fields.description: Project id to set the credentials

fields.name: private_key_id
fields.label: Private Key Id
fields.description: Private key id

fields.name: private_key
fields.label: Private Key
fields.description: Private key in a form of '<PEM block>\n'

fields.name: client_email
fields.label: Client Email
fields.description: Client email

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client Id
fields.description: Client Id

fields.name: auth_uri
fields.label: Auth Uri
fields.description: Authentication uri

fields.name: token_uri
fields.label: Token Uri
fields.description: Token uri

fields.name: auth_provider_x509_cert_url
fields.label: Auth Provider X509 Cert Url
fields.description: Auth provider x509 certificate url

fields.name: type
fields.label: Type
fields.description: Authentication type

fields.name: client_x509_cert_url
fields.label: Client X509 Cert Url
fields.description: If not set it will be default to https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/client_email

fields.name: include_models
fields.label: Include Models
fields.description: Ingest models and model versions from the registry.

fields.name: include_training_jobs
fields.label: Include Training Jobs
fields.description: Ingest training jobs and related run events.

fields.name: include_experiments
fields.label: Include Experiments
fields.description: Ingest experiments and experiment runs.

fields.name: include_pipelines
fields.label: Include Pipelines
fields.description: Ingest pipelines and tasks.

fields.name: include_evaluations
fields.label: Include Evaluations
fields.description: Ingest model evaluations and evaluation metrics.

fields.name: experiment_name_pattern
fields.label: Experiment Name Pattern
fields.description: Regex allow/deny pattern for experiment names.

fields.name: training_job_type_pattern
fields.label: Training Job Type Pattern
fields.description: Regex allow/deny pattern for training job class names (e.g., CustomJob).

fields.name: model_name_pattern
fields.label: Model Name Pattern
fields.description: Regex allow/deny pattern for model display names.

fields.name: project_ids
fields.label: Project Ids
fields.description: Ingest specified GCP project ids. Overrides project_id_pattern.

fields.name: project_id_pattern
fields.label: Project Id Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for project ids to include/exclude.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: use_ml_metadata_for_lineage
fields.label: Use Ml Metadata For Lineage
fields.description: Extract lineage from Vertex AI ML Metadata API for CustomJob and other training jobs. This enables input dataset → training job → output model lineage for non-AutoML jobs.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Stateful ingestion configuration for tracking and removing stale metadata.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: normalize_external_dataset_paths
fields.label: Normalize External Dataset Paths
fields.description: Strip partition segments from external dataset paths (GCS/S3/ABS) to create stable dataset URNs. When enabled, 'gs://bucket/data/year=2024/month=01/' becomes 'gs://bucket/data/'. Partition-level information is captured via DataProcessInstance. Default is False for backward compatibility. Will default to True in a future major version.

fields.name: partition_pattern_rules
fields.label: Partition Pattern Rules
fields.description: Regex patterns to identify and strip partition segments from paths. Applied when normalize_external_dataset_paths is enabled. Patterns are applied in order.

fields.name: extract_execution_metrics
fields.label: Extract Execution Metrics
fields.description: Extract hyperparameters and metrics from ML Metadata Executions. Useful for training jobs that don't use Experiments but log to ML Metadata.

fields.name: max_models
fields.label: Max Models
fields.description: Maximum number of models to ingest. Models are ordered by update_time descending (most recently updated first). Default: 10000, Max: 50000. Set to None for unlimited (not recommended).

fields.name: max_training_jobs_per_type
fields.label: Max Training Jobs Per Type
fields.description: Maximum training jobs per type (CustomJob, AutoML, etc.). Jobs are ordered by update_time descending (most recently updated first). Default: 1000, Max: 10000. Set to None for unlimited (not recommended).

fields.name: max_experiments
fields.label: Max Experiments
fields.description: Maximum number of experiments to ingest. Experiments are ordered by update_time descending (most recently updated first). Default: 1000, Max: 10000. Set to None for unlimited (not recommended).

fields.name: max_runs_per_experiment
fields.label: Max Runs Per Experiment
fields.description: Maximum experiment runs per experiment. Runs are ordered by update_time descending (most recently updated first). Default: 100, Max: 1000. Set to None for unlimited (not recommended).

fields.name: max_evaluations_per_model
fields.label: Max Evaluations Per Model
fields.description: Maximum evaluations per model. Default: 10, Max: 100. Set to None for unlimited (not recommended).

fields.name: ml_metadata_max_execution_search_limit
fields.label: Ml Metadata Max Execution Search Limit
fields.description: Maximum number of ML Metadata executions to retrieve when searching for a training job. Ordered by LAST_UPDATE_TIME descending. Lower this if ingestion is slow or timing out.

fields.name: rate_limit
fields.label: Rate Limit
fields.description: Slow down ingestion to avoid hitting Vertex AI API quota limits. Enable if you see '429 Quota Exceeded' errors during ingestion.

fields.name: requests_per_min
fields.label: Requests Per Min
fields.description: Max API requests per minute when rate_limit is enabled. 600 is Google's quota ceiling for resource management requests per project per region (see https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/quotas). Lower this only if you share quota with other workloads running in the same project and region.

fields.name: project_labels
fields.label: Project Labels
fields.description: Ingest projects with these labels (key:value). Applied before project_id_pattern.

fields.name: regions
fields.label: Regions
fields.description: List of Vertex AI regions to scan. If empty and discover_regions is false, falls back to 'region'.

fields.name: discover_regions
fields.label: Discover Regions
fields.description: If true, discover available Vertex AI regions per project and scan all.

fields.name: bucket_uri
fields.label: Bucket Uri
fields.description: Bucket URI used in your project

fields.name: vertexai_url
fields.label: Vertexai Url
fields.description: VertexUI URI

fields.name: platform_instance_map
fields.label: Platform Instance Map
fields.description: Map external platform names (gcs, bigquery, s3, azure_blob_storage, snowflake) to their platform instance and env. Ensures URNs match native connectors for lineage connectivity.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: host_port
fields.label: Host Port
fields.description: host URL

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: database (catalog)

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: username

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: password

fields.name: sqlalchemy_uri
fields.label: Sqlalchemy Uri
fields.description: URI of database to connect to. See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#database-urls. Takes precedence over other connection parameters.

fields.name: schema_pattern
fields.label: Schema Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for schemas to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to only match the schema name. e.g. to match all tables in schema analytics, use the regex 'analytics'

fields.name: table_pattern
fields.label: Table Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for tables to filter in ingestion. Specify regex to match the entire table name in database.schema.table format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: view_pattern
fields.label: View Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for views to filter in ingestion. Note: Defaults to table_pattern if not specified. Specify regex to match the entire view name in database.schema.view format. e.g. to match all views starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

fields.name: include_views
fields.label: Include Views
fields.description: Whether views should be ingested.

fields.name: include_tables
fields.label: Include Tables
fields.description: Whether tables should be ingested.

fields.name: models_pattern
fields.label: Models Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns for ml models to filter in ingestion.

fields.name: include_projections
fields.label: Include Projections
fields.description: Whether projections should be ingested.

fields.name: include_models
fields.label: Include Models
fields.description: Whether Models should be ingested.

fields.name: incremental_lineage
fields.label: Incremental Lineage
fields.description: When enabled, emits lineage as incremental to existing lineage already in DataHub. When disabled, re-states lineage on each run.

fields.name: include_table_location_lineage
fields.label: Include Table Location Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include table lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_lineage
fields.label: Include View Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include view lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: include_view_column_lineage
fields.label: Include View Column Lineage
fields.description: Populates column-level lineage for view->view and table->view lineage using DataHub's sql parser. Requires `include_view_lineage` to be enabled.

fields.name: include_projection_lineage
fields.label: Include Projection Lineage
fields.description: If the source supports it, include view lineage to the underlying storage location.

fields.name: profile_pattern
fields.label: Profile Pattern
fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables (or specific columns) for profiling during ingestion. Note that only tables allowed by the `table_pattern` will be considered.

group_fields.name: method
group_fields.label: Method
group_fields.description: Profiling method to use. `sqlalchemy` (default) runs profiling queries directly against your source's existing SQLAlchemy connection. `ge` selects the legacy Great Expectations profiler, which is deprecated and requires `pip install 'acryl-datahub[profiling-ge]'`.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether profiling should be done.

group_fields.name: operation_config
group_fields.label: Operation Config
group_fields.description: Experimental feature. To specify operation configs.

group_fields.name: lower_freq_profile_enabled
group_fields.label: Lower Freq Profile Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to do profiling at lower freq or not. This does not do any scheduling just adds additional checks to when not to run profiling.

group_fields.name: profile_day_of_week
group_fields.label: Profile Day Of Week
group_fields.description: Number between 0 to 6 for day of week (both inclusive). 0 is Monday and 6 is Sunday. If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: profile_date_of_month
group_fields.label: Profile Date Of Month
group_fields.description: Number between 1 to 31 for date of month (both inclusive). If not specified, defaults to Nothing and this field does not take affect.

group_fields.name: limit
group_fields.label: Limit
group_fields.description: Max number of documents to profile. By default, profiles all documents.

group_fields.name: offset
group_fields.label: Offset
group_fields.description: Offset in documents to profile. By default, uses no offset.

group_fields.name: profile_table_level_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Level Only
group_fields.description: Whether to perform profiling at table-level only, or include column-level profiling as well.

group_fields.name: include_field_null_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Null Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of nulls for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_count
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Count
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the number of distinct values for each column.

group_fields.name: include_field_min_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Min Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the min value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_max_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Max Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the max value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_mean_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Mean Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the mean value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_median_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Median Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the median value of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_stddev_value
group_fields.label: Include Field Stddev Value
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the standard deviation of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_quantiles
group_fields.label: Include Field Quantiles
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the quantiles of numeric columns.

group_fields.name: include_field_distinct_value_frequencies
group_fields.label: Include Field Distinct Value Frequencies
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for distinct value frequencies.

group_fields.name: include_field_histogram
group_fields.label: Include Field Histogram
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the histogram for numeric fields.

group_fields.name: include_field_sample_values
group_fields.label: Include Field Sample Values
group_fields.description: Whether to profile for the sample values for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker threads to use for profiling. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: report_dropped_profiles
group_fields.label: Report Dropped Profiles
group_fields.description: Whether to report datasets or dataset columns which were not profiled. Set to `True` for debugging purposes.

group_fields.name: turn_off_expensive_profiling_metrics
group_fields.label: Turn Off Expensive Profiling Metrics
group_fields.description: Whether to turn off expensive profiling or not. This turns off profiling for quantiles, distinct_value_frequencies, histogram & sample_values. This also limits maximum number of fields being profiled to 10.

group_fields.name: field_sample_values_limit
group_fields.label: Field Sample Values Limit
group_fields.description: Upper limit for number of sample values to collect for all columns.

group_fields.name: max_number_of_fields_to_profile
group_fields.label: Max Number Of Fields To Profile
group_fields.description: A positive integer that specifies the maximum number of columns to profile for any table. `None` implies all columns. The cost of profiling goes up significantly as the number of columns to profile goes up.

group_fields.name: profile_if_updated_since_days
group_fields.label: Profile If Updated Since Days
group_fields.description: Profile table only if it has been updated since these many number of days. If set to `null`, no constraint of last modified time for tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, and `Dremio`. Note: for Dremio this compares against DataHub's last-profiled timestamp (Dremio exposes no table modification time), so it controls profile frequency rather than reacting to upstream change.

group_fields.name: profile_table_size_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Size Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their size is less than specified GBs. If set to `null`, no limit on the size of tables to profile. Supported in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`, `Databricks`, `Oracle`, and `Teradata`. `Oracle` uses calculated size from gathered stats. `Teradata` uses DBC space accounting.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_limit
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Limit
group_fields.description: Profile tables only if their row count is less than specified count. If set to `null`, no limit on the row count of tables to profile. Supported only in `Snowflake`, `BigQuery`. Supported for `Oracle` based on gathered stats.

group_fields.name: profile_table_row_count_estimate_only
group_fields.label: Profile Table Row Count Estimate Only
group_fields.description: Use an approximate query for row count. This will be much faster but slightly less accurate. Only supported for Postgres and MySQL.

group_fields.name: query_combiner_enabled
group_fields.label: Query Combiner Enabled
group_fields.description: *This feature is still experimental and can be disabled if it causes issues.* Reduces the total number of queries issued and speeds up profiling by dynamically combining SQL queries where possible.

group_fields.name: partition_profiling_enabled
group_fields.label: Partition Profiling Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether to profile partitioned tables. Only BigQuery and Aws Athena supports this. If enabled, latest partition data is used for profiling.

group_fields.name: partition_datetime
group_fields.label: Partition Datetime
group_fields.description: If specified, profile only the partition which matches this datetime. If not specified, profile the latest partition. Only Bigquery supports this.

group_fields.name: use_sampling
group_fields.label: Use Sampling
group_fields.description: Whether to profile column level stats on sample of table. Only BigQuery and Snowflake support this. If enabled, profiling is done on rows sampled from table. Sampling is not done for smaller tables.

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of rows to be sampled from table for column level profiling.Applicable only if `use_sampling` is set to True.

group_fields.name: profile_external_tables
group_fields.label: Profile External Tables
group_fields.description: Whether to profile external tables. Only Snowflake and Redshift supports this.

group_fields.name: tags_to_ignore_sampling
group_fields.label: Tags To Ignore Sampling
group_fields.description: Fixed list of tags to ignore sampling. Each entry may be a full tag URN (e.g. `urn:li:tag:my_tag`) or just the tag name (e.g. `my_tag`). If not specified, tables will be sampled based on `use_sampling`.

group_fields.name: profile_nested_fields
group_fields.label: Profile Nested Fields
group_fields.description: Whether to profile complex types like structs, arrays and maps.

fields.name: classification
fields.label: Classification
fields.description: For details, refer to [Classification](../../../../metadata-ingestion/docs/dev_guides/classification.md).

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether classification should be used to auto-detect glossary terms

group_fields.name: sample_size
group_fields.label: Sample Size
group_fields.description: Number of sample values used for classification.

group_fields.name: max_workers
group_fields.label: Max Workers
group_fields.description: Number of worker processes to use for classification. Set to 1 to disable.

group_fields.name: table_pattern
group_fields.label: Table Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter tables for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the entire table name in `database.schema.table` format. e.g. to match all tables starting with customer in Customer database and public schema, use the regex 'Customer.public.customer.*'

group_fields.name: column_pattern
group_fields.label: Column Pattern
group_fields.description: Regex patterns to filter columns for classification. This is used in combination with other patterns in parent config. Specify regex to match the column name in `database.schema.table.column` format.

group_fields.name: info_type_to_term
group_fields.label: Info Type To Term
group_fields.description: Optional mapping to provide glossary term identifier for info type

group_fields.name: classifiers
group_fields.label: Classifiers
group_fields.description: Classifiers to use to auto-detect glossary terms. If more than one classifier, infotype predictions from the classifier defined later in sequence take precedance.

fields.name: stateful_ingestion
fields.label: Stateful Ingestion
fields.description: Base specialized config for Stateful Ingestion with stale metadata removal capability.

group_fields.name: enabled
group_fields.label: Enabled
group_fields.description: Whether or not to enable stateful ingest. Default: True if a pipeline_name is set and either a datahub-rest sink or `datahub_api` is specified, otherwise False

group_fields.name: remove_stale_metadata
group_fields.label: Remove Stale Metadata
group_fields.description: Soft-deletes the entities present in the last successful run but missing in the current run with stateful_ingestion enabled.

group_fields.name: fail_safe_threshold
group_fields.label: Fail Safe Threshold
group_fields.description: Prevents large amount of soft deletes & the state from committing from accidental changes to the source configuration if the relative change percent in entities compared to the previous state is above the 'fail_safe_threshold'.

fields.name: convert_urns_to_lowercase
fields.label: Convert Urns To Lowercase
fields.description: Whether to convert dataset urns to lowercase.

fields.name: domain
fields.label: Domain
fields.description: Attach domains to databases, schemas or tables during ingestion using regex patterns. Domain key can be a guid like *urn:li:domain:ec428203-ce86-4db3-985d-5a8ee6df32ba* or a string like "Marketing".) If you provide strings, then datahub will attempt to resolve this name to a guid, and will error out if this fails. There can be multiple domain keys specified.

fields.name: use_file_backed_cache
fields.label: Use File Backed Cache
fields.description: Whether to use a file backed cache for the view definitions.

fields.name: env
fields.label: Env
fields.description: The environment that all assets produced by this connector belong to

fields.name: platform_instance
fields.label: Platform Instance
fields.description: The instance of the platform that all assets produced by this recipe belong to. This should be unique within the platform. See https://docs.datahub.com/docs/platform-instances/ for more details.

fields.name: options
fields.label: Options
fields.description: Any options specified here will be passed to [SQLAlchemy.create_engine](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/core/engines.html#sqlalchemy.create_engine) as kwargs. To set connection arguments in the URL, specify them under `connect_args`.
